description: Unique surrogate identifier for each BAM/CRAM alignment result record produced by a bioinformatics pipeline run. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this entity represents a discrete computational event (alignment job output) with its own lifecycle, QC status, and lineage.

Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: Alignment results are produced from a specific aliquot's sequencing data. Multiple aliquots can exist per specimen, so specimen_id alone is ambiguous. Clinical chain-of-custody and CLIA/CAP compliance

Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Alignment results with QC failures (mapping rate below spec, high chimeric read rate) trigger formal deviations in GxP genomics labs. Linking alignment_result to quality.deviation enables investigatio

Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Alignment QC metrics (mapping rate, coverage uniformity) are directly correlated with extraction quality (RIN, purity). Method validation studies and QC failure investigations in genomics labs routine

Identifier of the sequencing Flow Cell used to generate the raw reads for this alignment. The Flow Cell is the consumable cartridge on which sequencing chemistry occurs. Traceability to the flow cell enables investigation of batch-level sequencing failures, reagent lot issues, and instrument performance trends.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; ISO 15189 Traceability Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: alignment_result is METADATA about an alignment (QC metrics, statistics). The actual BAM/CRAM file is a genomic_artifact. alignment_result currently has output_file_path, output_file_format, output_fi

Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Alignment results are library-specific outputs required for library QC, coverage analysis, and prep troubleshooting. Labs use this to identify failed libraries, optimize prep protocols, and determine

business_glossary_term: Library Prep Reagent Catalog Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Alignment QC metrics (duplicate rate, insert size distribution, GC bias) are library prep chemistry-dependent. Troubleshooting workflow: when coverage uniformity degrades, correlate to library prep re

Foreign key linking to instrument.model. Business justification: Platform-specific QC benchmarking and method validation reports stratify alignment metrics (mapping rate, coverage uniformity) by instrument model. A genomics bioinformatics engineer expects alignment

Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced this alignment result. Links the alignment output back to the specific pipeline execution context, enabling full data lineage from raw FASTQ input to aligned output. Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE / RESOURCE_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 15189; GxP Data Integrity Guidelines; ALCOA+ Principles

description: Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Alignment results depend on upstream sample QC (DNA integrity, concentration, purity) performed in laboratory. Poor alignment metrics often trace to failed sample QC. Enables integrated quality invest

Foreign key linking to sample.run_sample. Business justification: Alignment results are produced per run_sample (per-sample per-lane sequencing record). LIMS-to-bioinformatics result reconciliation for clinical reporting requires linking alignment results to the LIM

Reference to the biological sample that was sequenced and aligned. Establishes traceability from the alignment result back to the originating sample, supporting clinical reporting, regulatory audit, and downstream variant analysis workflows.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Alignment results must trace to originating sequencing run for quality control, coverage analysis, and reprocessing decisions. Essential for clinical laboratories to validate alignment quality against

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Alignment QC metrics (coverage depth, mapping rate) establish analytical validity in regulatory submissions for NGS-based IVDs. New attribute links alignment results to submissions for performance cla

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Alignment results generated during pipeline validation studies must be linked to the quality.validation_study for traceability. Regulatory submissions require demonstrating which alignment results sup

Name of the alignment software tool used to produce this result (e.g., BWA-MEM, BWA-MEM2, DRAGEN, STAR, Bowtie2, minimap2). The choice of aligner affects sensitivity, specificity, and computational cost. Required for reproducibility and regulatory method validation documentation.
reference: ISO 15189; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; Bioinformatics Pipeline Validation Guidelines

description: Semantic version string of the aligner tool used (e.g., 0.7.17, 2.2.1). Version tracking is mandatory for bioinformatics method validation, regulatory submissions (FDA, EMA), and reproducibility of clinical genomic results. A version change may require re-validation under ISO 15189 and CLIA.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; ICH E6 GCP; GxP Software Validation

description: The date and time at which the alignment job completed and the BAM/CRAM output file was finalized. Represents the principal real-world business event time for this record. Used for Turnaround Time (TAT) calculations, SLA monitoring, and pipeline performance analytics. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA TAT Requirements; GxP ALCOA+ Principles

description: Percentage of reads flagged as chimeric (reads where the two ends map to different chromosomes or distant genomic locations). Elevated chimeric rates indicate library preparation artifacts, structural variant signals, or sequencing errors. Monitored as a QC metric in clinical WGS and structural variant detection workflows.
reference: STAR Aligner Documentation; Picard CollectAlignmentSummaryMetrics; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

description: Compute infrastructure platform on which the alignment job was executed: AWS (Amazon Web Services), on-premise High-Performance Computing (HPC) cluster, Azure, GCP, or Illumina DRAGEN server. Required for cost allocation, performance benchmarking, and infrastructure capacity planning.
reference: AWS Well-Architected Framework; ISO 27001; GxP Cloud Computing Guidelines (EMA/ISPE)

description: Wall-clock time in seconds taken to complete the alignment job from job start to job completion. Used for pipeline performance monitoring, SLA compliance tracking, HPC/cloud resource optimization, and cost modeling. Enables identification of performance regressions across aligner versions or reference genome builds.
reference: AWS Cost Management; HPC Job Scheduler Logs; GxP Computerized Systems Validation

description: Percentage of target bases covered at ≥20% of the mean coverage depth, measuring the uniformity of sequencing coverage across the target region. High uniformity indicates consistent library preparation and sequencing performance. Low uniformity may indicate GC bias or capture inefficiency in WES/panel assays.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

description: Number of CPU cores allocated and used for the alignment job. Together with compute_runtime_seconds and memory_used_gb, enables accurate cloud compute cost attribution per alignment result and supports capacity planning for HPC/AWS infrastructure.
reference: AWS Cost Management; HPC Resource Management Standards

description: The date and time when this alignment result record was first ingested into the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Distinct from alignment_timestamp (job completion time). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and ETL monitoring. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: GxP ALCOA+ Principles; ISO 27001; SOX Section 404

description: Percentage of mapped reads flagged as PCR or optical duplicates (duplicate_reads / mapped_reads × 100). High duplicate rates reduce effective coverage depth and can introduce variant calling bias. Typically assessed using Picard MarkDuplicates or equivalent. Threshold violations trigger QC warning or failure.
reference: Picard Tools Documentation; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines; ISO 15189

description: Percentage of guanine (G) and cytosine (C) bases in the aligned reads. GC content deviating significantly from the expected genomic average (~41% for human) indicates GC bias in library preparation or PCR amplification, which can cause uneven coverage and affect Copy Number Variation (CNV) analysis accuracy.
reference: FastQC Quality Control Tool; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Standard deviation of the insert size distribution in base pairs. A narrow distribution indicates consistent library preparation; a wide distribution may indicate mixed fragment populations or library quality issues. Used alongside median_insert_size_bp for comprehensive library QC assessment.
reference: Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics; Illumina Library Preparation QC Standards

description: Regulatory intended use classification for this alignment result: Research Use Only (RUO), In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), Laboratory Developed Test (LDT), clinical research, agricultural genomics, or companion diagnostic. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, IVDR, CLIA) and data handling requirements including PHI/HIPAA obligations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; EU IVDR 2017/746; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; HIPAA 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this alignment result is a reanalysis of a previously aligned sample (True) or an original first-pass alignment (False). Reanalyses may be triggered by reference genome updates, aligner version upgrades, QC failures, or clinical reinterpretation requests. Supports data lineage and version management.
reference: GxP Data Integrity Guidelines; ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Number of sequencing reads that successfully mapped to the reference genome. Together with total_reads, this is used to compute the mapping rate. Low mapped read counts may indicate sample contamination, library preparation failure, or reference genome mismatch.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; SAMtools Flagstat Specification

description: Percentage of total reads that successfully mapped to the reference genome (mapped_reads / total_reads × 100). A primary alignment QC metric; typical acceptable thresholds are ≥90% for human WGS. Values below threshold trigger QC failure and potential re-sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines; ISO 15189

description: Mean Phred Score across all bases in the aligned reads. The Phred Score is a logarithmic measure of base call accuracy (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key sequencing run quality metric; Q30 ≥ 80% is a standard threshold for clinical-grade sequencing. Directly impacts variant calling sensitivity and specificity.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green 1998); ISO 15189

description: Average number of sequencing reads covering each base position in the target region or genome (Coverage Depth). Expressed as a fold value (e.g., 30×, 100×). A fundamental QC metric determining the statistical power for variant detection; minimum thresholds vary by application (e.g., 30× for WGS, 100× for somatic panels).
reference: ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Median length of the DNA fragment insert in base pairs (bp) as measured from the aligned paired-end reads. Insert size distribution is a key library preparation QC metric; unexpected values indicate library degradation, adapter contamination, or incorrect size selection. Affects read overlap and variant calling accuracy.
reference: Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics; Illumina Library Preparation QC Standards

description: Peak memory (RAM) consumed in gigabytes during the alignment job execution. Memory usage varies significantly by aligner (e.g., BWA-MEM2 requires ~60 GB for human WGS). Used for cloud instance type selection, cost optimization, and pipeline resource configuration management.
reference: AWS Cost Management; HPC Resource Management Standards; Bioinformatics Pipeline Optimization Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases at ≥10× Coverage Depth
description: Percentage of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 10× reads. A standard coverage adequacy metric used in clinical genomics reporting to confirm that the majority of the target region has sufficient depth for reliable variant calling. Commonly reported in clinical assay validation and CAP/CLIA compliance.
reference: CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases at ≥30× Coverage Depth
description: Percentage of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 30× reads. The 30× threshold is the standard minimum for clinical Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) and is required by many regulatory and accreditation bodies. Distinct from pct_bases_above_10x as it represents a higher stringency clinical threshold.
reference: ACMG/AMP WGS Guidelines; CAP Accreditation Standards; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases with Phred Score ≥Q30
description: Percentage of sequenced bases with a Phred Score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. The Q30 percentage is the primary sequencing run quality KPI reported by Illumina instruments and BaseSpace. Minimum thresholds (typically ≥75–80%) are defined in clinical assay SOPs and regulatory submissions.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Phred Quality Score Standard; FDA IVD Submission Guidelines; ISO 15189

description: Percentage of read pairs where both reads mapped to the reference genome in the expected orientation and within the expected insert size range. Low properly paired rates indicate library preparation issues, adapter contamination, or reference genome incompatibility. A standard SAMtools flagstat metric.
reference: SAMtools Flagstat Specification; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; ISO 15189

description: Free-text notes entered by a bioinformatics scientist or QC reviewer during manual review of this alignment result. Captures the rationale for QC status overrides, observations about anomalous metrics, or instructions for downstream processing. Required for GxP audit trail and CLIA/CAP documentation.
reference: GxP ALCOA+ Principles; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Accreditation Standards; ISO 15189

description: Overall Quality Control (QC) pass/fail/warning determination for this alignment result based on evaluation of all QC metrics against defined thresholds. Drives downstream workflow routing: pass results proceed to variant calling; fail results trigger re-sequencing or sample rejection. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Accreditation Standards; GxP Data Integrity

description: Length of sequencing reads in base pairs (e.g., 150 bp for standard Illumina short-read, 10,000+ bp for long-read platforms). Read length affects alignment accuracy, variant detection capability (especially for structural variants and repeat regions), and computational resource requirements.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

description: Human-readable label of the reference genome assembly build used for alignment (e.g., GRCh38/hg38, T2T-CHM13). Stored denormalized here for rapid querying and reporting without joining to the reference genome dimension. Critical for clinical interpretation — variant coordinates are build-specific.
reference: NCBI RefSeq; Genome Reference Consortium (GRC); ACMG Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this alignment result (e.g., ALN-2024-00123456). Used in LIMS reports, bioinformatics dashboards, and regulatory submissions to uniquely identify an alignment output without exposing surrogate keys. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.

Sequencing instrument platform used to generate the raw reads (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, PacBio Revio, Oxford Nanopore PromethION). Platform determines read characteristics (length, error profile), applicable alignment tools, and QC thresholds. Required for method validation documentation and regulatory submissions.
reference: FDA IVD Submission Guidelines; ISO 15189; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Classification of the sequencing assay type for which this alignment was performed: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq, amplicon, or metagenomics. Determines applicable QC thresholds, coverage requirements, and downstream analysis workflows. Satisfies CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: ACMG/AMP Sequencing Guidelines; ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; FDA IVD Submission Guidelines

description: Ratio of reads mapping to the forward strand versus the reverse strand (forward_reads / total_mapped_reads). Expected value is approximately 0.5 for unstranded libraries. Significant deviation indicates strand bias, which can introduce systematic variant calling errors, particularly for SNP and indel detection.
reference: Picard CollectAlignmentSummaryMetrics; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

description: Total number of sequencing reads in the input FASTQ file(s) submitted for alignment. Reflects sequencing depth and library complexity. Used to assess whether sufficient input data was provided for the intended application (e.g., WGS, WES, targeted panel). Key QC input metric.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub QC Metrics; ACMG/AMP Sequencing QC Guidelines

description: The date and time when this alignment result record was last modified, such as when QC status was updated after manual review or when supplementary metrics were appended. Supports audit trail and data governance requirements. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: GxP ALCOA+ Principles; ISO 27001; SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each data lineage event record in the bioinformatics processing chain. Primary key for this table. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — captures immutable transformation events across the genomic data processing pipeline from raw FASTQ ingestion to clinical report generation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP Good Practice Regulations

Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.compute_job. Business justification: data_lineage_event tracks transformations and has hpc_job_id as STRING. compute_job is the authoritative registry of HPC/cloud jobs with compute_job_id as PK. Normalizing hpc_job_id STRING to compute_

Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Lineage events capture demultiplexing as a transformation step in data processing workflows. This link enables tracking FASTQ file generation and subsequent processing, required for GxP-compliant data

Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Data lineage events recording errors or unexpected data transformations (event_status=error, unexpected record counts) trigger formal deviations in GxP genomics operations. This link enables investiga

Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Data lineage events must trace back to the extraction step as the origin of digital genomic data. GxP data integrity requirements and regulatory submissions require a complete, unbroken chain from ext

Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Lineage tracking at library level enables sample-level audit trails and consent management. This link supports clinical laboratories in demonstrating complete chain of custody from sample receipt thro

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding lineage event in the processing chain. Enables construction of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of transformation events for end-to-end data lineage traversal from raw FASTQ to clinical report.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; HIPAA; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Reference to the parent bioinformatics pipeline run that encompasses this lineage event. Enables grouping of all lineage events within a single pipeline execution for end-to-end traceability.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Reference to the specific pipeline run step that produced or triggered this lineage event. Links the lineage record to the bioinformatics pipeline execution context for reproducibility and audit traceability.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP Good Practice Regulations; HIPAA

description: Reference to the genomic data artifact (e.g., FASTQ, BAM, VCF, gVCF, annotation file) that served as the input to this transformation event. Supports forward and backward data lineage tracing.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GxP

description: Reference to the biological sample associated with this lineage event, as tracked in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Provides traceability from raw genomic data back to the originating sample.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA; CAP; HIPAA; LabVantage LIMS

description: Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Lineage events track transformations from sequencing runs through pipeline stages, required for GxP compliance and FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trails. This link enables complete data provenance from raw

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Data lineage events in GxP bioinformatics environments must be traceable to the regulatory submission they support, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 and Annex 11 audit trail requirements. Regulators inspecti
Reference to the genomic data artifact produced as the output of this transformation event (e.g., aligned BAM, called VCF, annotated VCF, clinical report). Enables downstream lineage traversal.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GxP

description: Serialized string (JSON or key=value pairs) of the runtime parameters passed to the bioinformatics tool for this transformation event (e.g., minimum base quality score, minimum mapping quality, ploidy setting). Critical for reproducibility and regulatory audit trails.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; ISO 13485

description: Type of genomic assay associated with this lineage event, indicating the sequencing or array methodology applied (e.g., Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted gene panel, SNP array, Copy Number Variation (CNV) analysis, RNA-seq, amplicon sequencing). Used for assay-specific pipeline routing and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|CNV_analysis|RNA_seq|amplicon — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA IVD Regulations; CLIA; CAP; ISO 13485; IVDR 2017/746

description: The computing infrastructure platform on which this transformation event was executed (e.g., AWS Batch, AWS EMR, HPC SLURM cluster, Databricks). Used for cost attribution, performance benchmarking, and infrastructure capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aws_batch|aws_emr|hpc_slurm|hpc_lsf|databricks|local|on_premise_hpc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Number of CPU cores allocated and consumed by the compute job executing this transformation event. Used for HPC/cloud resource consumption tracking, cost attribution, and pipeline performance optimization.

Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this lineage event record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (the real-world transformation time). Required for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 record integrity.

Structured error or exception code returned by the bioinformatics tool or pipeline orchestrator when a transformation event fails (e.g., TOOL_EXIT_NONZERO, INSUFFICIENT_COVERAGE, CHECKSUM_MISMATCH, TIMEOUT). Used for failure root-cause analysis and automated alerting.

Human-readable error or exception message captured when a transformation event fails or produces a warning. Provides detailed context for failure root-cause analysis, pipeline debugging, and GxP deviation investigation.

Externally-known business identifier for this lineage event, formatted as LIN- followed by an alphanumeric code. Used for cross-system referencing, audit log correlation, and regulatory submission traceability.

Current lifecycle state of the data transformation event. Indicates whether the transformation is pending execution, actively running, successfully completed, failed with error, skipped due to conditional logic, or retried after a prior failure. Used for pipeline monitoring and failure root-cause analysis.

The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the data transformation event occurred in the bioinformatics pipeline. This is the principal real-world event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Critical for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.

Categorical discriminator identifying the kind of data transformation that occurred in the bioinformatics pipeline. Values: ingest (raw FASTQ ingestion), transform (alignment/variant calling), filter (QC filtering), merge (combining artifacts), split (demultiplexing), annotate (variant annotation), export (clinical report generation), archive (long-term storage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ingest|transform|filter|merge|split|annotate|export|archive — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; HIPAA; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Wall-clock elapsed time in seconds for the transformation event to complete, from job start to job end. Used for pipeline performance benchmarking, Turnaround Time (TAT) analysis, and SLA monitoring.

Indicates whether this transformation event is subject to GxP (Good Practice Regulations including GMP, GLP, GCP) audit trail requirements (True = GxP-regulated event, False = non-regulated event). Used to filter events for regulatory audit reporting and inspection readiness.
reference: GxP Good Practice Regulations; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA; EMA; ICH

business_glossary_term: HIPAA Protected Health Information (PHI) Involved Flag
description: Indicates whether this transformation event processed or produced data containing Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA (True = PHI involved, False = no PHI). Used for HIPAA compliance reporting, data access controls, and breach risk assessment.
reference: HIPAA Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act; 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164

description: Number of input records, reads, or variants consumed by this transformation event (e.g., number of FASTQ reads ingested, number of variants in input VCF). Used for data volume tracking, QC validation, and pipeline performance analysis.
reference: GxP; 21 CFR Part 11; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Amount of RAM in gigabytes consumed by the compute job executing this transformation event. Used for HPC/cloud resource consumption tracking, cost attribution, and pipeline performance optimization.

Username, service account name, or system identity (human user or automated pipeline agent) that initiated or executed this transformation event. Required for GxP audit trails, 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature traceability, and HIPAA access accountability.

Categorizes whether the transformation event was initiated by a human user, an automated bioinformatics pipeline, a system service account, or a scheduled job. Supports audit trail differentiation and compliance reporting under 21 CFR Part 11.

Number of output records, reads, or variants produced by this transformation event (e.g., number of aligned reads in output BAM, number of variants in output VCF). Used for data volume tracking, QC validation, and yield analysis.
reference: GxP; 21 CFR Part 11; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: High-level stage of the bioinformatics processing pipeline in which this transformation event occurred. Values: ingestion (raw FASTQ intake), preprocessing (trimming/QC), alignment (BAM/CRAM generation), variant_calling (VCF/gVCF), annotation (variant annotation), qc_filtering (QC-based filtering), reporting (clinical report generation), archival (long-term storage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ingestion|preprocessing|alignment|variant_calling|annotation|qc_filtering|reporting|archival — promote to reference product]
reference: GxP; 21 CFR Part 11; CLIA; CAP; ISO 13485

description: Name of the primary Quality Control (QC) metric evaluated at this transformation step (e.g., mean_coverage_depth, percent_mapped_reads, phred_score_q30_percent, ts_tv_ratio, variant_call_rate). Provides context for the QC pass/fail determination.
reference: ISO 13485; CLIA; CAP; GxP; Phred Score Standard

description: Numeric value of the primary Quality Control (QC) metric evaluated at this transformation step (e.g., 45.7 for mean coverage depth, 98.3 for percent mapped reads). Used for QC trending, threshold monitoring, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 13485; CLIA; CAP; GxP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the output artifact of this transformation event passed Quality Control (QC) criteria (True = QC passed, False = QC failed). Used for pipeline gating, clinical report eligibility, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 13485; CLIA; CAP; GxP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Number of times this transformation event has been retried following a prior failure. Used for pipeline reliability monitoring, failure pattern analysis, and identifying systemic infrastructure or data quality issues.

Cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA-256, or SHA-512) of the input genomic data artifact at the time of consumption. Used to verify data integrity, detect corruption, and confirm that the correct artifact version was used. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP data integrity compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 13485; FDA Data Integrity Guidance

description: File format of the input genomic data artifact consumed by this transformation event (e.g., FASTQ for raw reads, BAM/CRAM for aligned reads, VCF/gVCF for variant calls). Enables format-aware lineage queries and pipeline validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|FASTA|TSV|CSV|JSON|HTML|PDF — promote to reference product]
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification; VCF Specification v4.3; FASTQ Format Standard

description: Cloud or network storage path (e.g., S3 URI, ADLS path) of the input genomic data artifact consumed by this transformation event. Confidential as it may reveal internal infrastructure topology and data organization. Required for data recovery, re-processing, and audit traceability.

Cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA-256, or SHA-512) of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event. Used to verify data integrity, detect corruption, and confirm artifact authenticity for downstream steps. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP data integrity compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 13485; FDA Data Integrity Guidance

description: File format of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event (e.g., BAM for alignment output, VCF for variant calling output, annotated VCF for annotation output). Enables format-aware lineage queries and downstream pipeline validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|FASTA|TSV|CSV|JSON|HTML|PDF — promote to reference product]
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification; VCF Specification v4.3; FASTQ Format Standard

description: Cloud or network storage path (e.g., S3 URI, ADLS path) of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event. Confidential as it may reveal internal infrastructure topology. Required for downstream pipeline steps, data recovery, and audit traceability.

Docker or container image URI (including registry, repository, and digest/tag) used to execute the bioinformatics tool, ensuring exact computational environment reproducibility. Example: docker.io/broadinstitute/gatk:4.2.6.1. Supports GxP validation and 21 CFR Part 11 audit requirements.

Name of the bioinformatics software tool or algorithm used to perform the data transformation (e.g., BWA-MEM, GATK HaplotypeCaller, STAR, Trimmomatic, SnpEff, VEP). Essential for reproducibility and regulatory validation documentation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 13485; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Semantic version string of the bioinformatics tool used for the transformation (e.g., 4.2.6.1 for GATK, 0.7.17 for BWA). Required for reproducibility, pipeline validation, and regulatory submission documentation under GxP and 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 13485; ICH E6

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this lineage event record was last modified in the data platform (e.g., status update from running to completed, error message appended). Required for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 record integrity.

Regulatory use classification of the genomic data processed in this lineage event. Values: RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), clinical_research, agricultural, pharmaceutical. Determines applicable regulatory framework and data handling requirements.
reference: FDA IVD Regulations; IVDR 2017/746; CLIA; CAP; ISO 13485

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each genomic data artifact record in the master registry. Primary key for the genomic_artifact data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed data file asset produced or consumed by bioinformatics pipelines.
reference: ISO 27001; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

Identifier of the sequencing flow cell on which the originating sequencing run was performed. Enables traceability to the physical consumable lot, instrument, and run conditions. Critical for QC investigations and reagent lot correlation analysis.

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate upstream artifact from which this artifact was derived (e.g., a BAM artifact's parent is the FASTQ artifact; a VCF artifact's parent is the BAM artifact). Enables reconstruction of the full data lineage chain from raw to interpreted results.

description: Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced or last processed this artifact. Establishes provenance linkage from the artifact back to the specific pipeline execution, algorithm version, and compute parameters used. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE (resource reference) for this entity.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 27001

Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (FASTQ, BAM, VCF) are generated from samples that underwent laboratory QC testing. Linking enables complete data lineage from sample receipt through QC to final bioinformatics output

Foreign key linking to sample.run_sample. Business justification: FASTQ artifacts are generated per run_sample after demultiplexing. The run_sample record defines the barcode/index sequence and lane that produced the artifact. This link is essential for demultiplexi

Reference to the biological sample from which this genomic artifact was derived. Enables traceability from the final computational artifact back to the physical sample, supporting clinical reporting, QC, and regulatory audit requirements.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189; HIPAA

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (BAM, VCF, FASTQ files) submitted as raw data packages in FDA/EMA regulatory submissions must be directly traceable to the submission record. Required for data integrity verification

Timestamp when this artifact was moved to archival storage (e.g., AWS S3 Glacier), in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Null if the artifact has not been archived. Used for storage cost tracking, retrieval SLA management, and compliance with data lifecycle policies.
reference: ISO 8601; HIPAA (45 CFR §164.530); CLIA; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; AWS S3 Glacier

description: Externally-known unique identifier for the artifact as assigned by the originating system of record (e.g., Illumina BaseSpace file ID, LabVantage LIMS artifact accession number, or cloud storage object key). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Human-readable name or label assigned to the genomic artifact, typically derived from the sample identifier, run identifier, and artifact type (e.g., 'SampleA_WGS_R1.fastq.gz'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — File Naming Convention; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Current lifecycle state of the genomic artifact within the bioinformatics pipeline ecosystem. 'pending' indicates awaiting generation; 'available' indicates ready for downstream consumption; 'processing' indicates actively being transformed; 'failed' indicates a pipeline error; 'archived' indicates moved to cold storage; 'deleted' indicates purged per retention policy. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: ISO 27001; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GxP (Good Practice Regulations)

description: Classification of the genomic data artifact by its file format and biological content type. Drives downstream pipeline routing, storage tier assignment, and retention policy application. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|ANNOTATION|REPORT|INDEX|QC_METRICS — promote to reference product]
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs); VCF Specification v4.3 (GA4GH); FASTQ Format (Cock et al. 2010); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Index/barcode nucleotide sequence used to identify this sample within a multiplexed sequencing pool during demultiplexing. Enables traceability of the artifact to its specific library preparation and sample assignment in a pooled run.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; FASTQ Format Specification

description: MD5 hash digest of the artifact file content used for data integrity verification during transfer, storage, and retrieval. Enables detection of file corruption or unauthorized modification. Widely used in genomics data exchange protocols.
reference: GA4GH Data Object Service (DOS); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; ISO 27001

description: SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the artifact file content providing stronger integrity assurance than MD5. Required for regulatory-grade data integrity verification under GxP and HIPAA security rule compliance. Preferred checksum for clinical and regulatory submissions.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164); GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 27001; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Compression algorithm applied to the artifact file. BGZF (Blocked GNU Zip Format) is standard for BAM/CRAM/VCF files enabling random access; GZIP is standard for FASTQ files. Determines decompression requirements for downstream pipeline steps and storage cost calculations.
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs); FASTQ Format Specification; AWS S3

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this artifact contains Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA. Drives access control enforcement, audit logging requirements, and data handling procedures. Must be set to True for any artifact linked to an identified clinical sample.
reference: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) 45 CFR §164.501; GDPR Article 9

description: Timestamp when this genomic artifact record was first created in the registry, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and retention policy calculation.
reference: ISO 8601; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; HIPAA Security Rule

description: Privacy and regulatory classification of the genomic data contained in this artifact. 'PHI' (Protected Health Information) indicates data subject to HIPAA; 'de_identified' indicates PHI has been removed per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination; 'research' indicates IRB-governed research data; 'restricted' indicates controlled-access data (e.g., dbGaP); 'public' indicates open-access data.
reference: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) 45 CFR §164.514; GDPR Article 9; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; IRB (Institutional Review Board)

description: Timestamp when the physical artifact file was generated by the pipeline or instrument, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Distinct from the registry record creation timestamp — represents the actual file creation time as reported by the pipeline or storage system. Used for data freshness assessment and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.
reference: ISO 8601; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; GxP; CLIA

description: Physical size of the genomic artifact file in bytes at the time of creation or last verification. Used for storage capacity planning, cost allocation, and data transfer monitoring. Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: AWS S3 Object Metadata; ISO 27001 — Asset Management

description: Specific reference genome assembly build used during alignment or variant calling (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19). Stored directly on the artifact for rapid querying without joining to the reference genome table. Critical for variant interpretation and cross-cohort comparability.
reference: Genome Reference Consortium (GRC); NCBI RefSeq; GA4GH Variant Representation Specification

description: Full URI path to the index file associated with this artifact (e.g., '.bai' for BAM, '.tbi' for VCF). Stored alongside the artifact URI to enable direct access by pipeline tools without requiring a separate lookup.
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs); AWS S3; ISO 27001

description: Boolean flag indicating whether a corresponding index file exists for this artifact (e.g., BAI index for BAM, TBI/CSI index for VCF). Indexed artifacts support random-access queries by genomic coordinate, which is required for most downstream analysis tools.
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs); Tabix (hts-specs); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the sequencing reads in this artifact were generated using paired-end sequencing chemistry (True) or single-end (False). Paired-end reads improve alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Determines applicable alignment and QC parameters.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs)

description: Lane number on the sequencing flow cell from which the reads in this artifact were generated. Used for per-lane QC analysis, demultiplexing traceability, and identification of lane-specific sequencing artifacts or failures.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs)

description: Mean sequencing coverage depth (average number of reads covering each base) for alignment artifacts (BAM/CRAM). A key QC metric indicating whether sufficient data was generated for reliable variant calling. Expressed as fold-coverage (e.g., 30.0x for 30-fold coverage).
reference: CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Mean Phred base quality score across all reads in the artifact. Phred score (Q) represents the probability of an incorrect base call (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A primary QC metric for sequencing run quality assessment per CLIA and CAP standards.
reference: Phred Quality Score (Ewing & Green 1998); CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases at Q30 or Above (%Q30)
description: Percentage of bases in the artifact with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard QC threshold metric for NGS data quality acceptance. Typically required to be ≥80% for clinical-grade sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189

description: Stage in the bioinformatics processing pipeline at which this artifact was produced. Defines the artifact's position in the data lineage chain from raw sequencing output to interpreted genomic results: 'raw' (FASTQ from demux), 'aligned' (BAM/CRAM), 'variant_called' (VCF/gVCF), 'annotated' (annotated VCF), 'filtered' (filtered VCF), 'reported' (final clinical/research report input).
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GxP; CLIA; CAP

description: Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) for a QC failure or warning on this artifact (e.g., 'low_coverage', 'high_duplication_rate', 'adapter_contamination', 'low_q30'). Populated only when qc_status is 'fail' or 'warning'. Supports root cause analysis and corrective action tracking.

Quality control assessment outcome for this artifact based on applicable QC metrics (coverage depth, %Q30, alignment rate, etc.). 'pass' indicates all QC thresholds met; 'fail' indicates one or more critical thresholds not met; 'warning' indicates borderline metrics requiring review; 'pending_review' indicates QC evaluation not yet completed.
reference: CLIA (42 CFR §493); CAP; ISO 15189; LabVantage LIMS

description: Length of sequencing reads in base pairs for FASTQ and BAM artifacts (e.g., 150 for 2x150bp paired-end). Determines applicable alignment parameters, variant calling sensitivity, and QC thresholds. Stored in base pairs (bp).
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs)

description: Date on which this artifact becomes eligible for archival or deletion per the assigned retention policy. Calculated from the artifact creation date and the applicable retention period. Used by automated data lifecycle management processes.
reference: HIPAA (45 CFR §164.530); CLIA (42 CFR §493.1105); FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Retention policy classification governing how long this artifact must be retained before it is eligible for archival or deletion. 'short_term' (e.g., 90 days for intermediate files); 'standard' (e.g., 2 years); 'long_term' (e.g., 7 years for clinical); 'permanent' (e.g., reference datasets); 'regulatory_hold' (litigation or regulatory investigation hold).
reference: HIPAA (45 CFR §164.530); CLIA (42 CFR §493.1105); FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(e); GxP

description: Type of genomic sequencing or array assay that generated the raw data underlying this artifact. Determines applicable QC thresholds, coverage expectations, and downstream analysis pipelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|RNA_SEQ|AMPLICON|TARGETED_PANEL|ARRAY|CHIP_SEQ|METHYL_SEQ|ATAC_SEQ|SINGLE_CELL — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189

description: Cloud or on-premises storage platform where the artifact is physically stored. Drives data access patterns, cost allocation, and egress fee tracking. Supports multi-cloud and hybrid storage architecture management.
reference: AWS (Amazon Web Services); ISO 27001 — Asset Management

description: Full URI path to the artifact file in the storage backend (e.g., S3 bucket path 's3://genomics-biotech-prod/artifacts/sample123/output.bam', HDFS path, or NFS mount path). Used by pipeline orchestration and data access layers to locate and retrieve the file.
reference: AWS S3; ISO 27001 — Asset Management; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Total number of sequencing reads contained in this artifact. For FASTQ files, represents raw read count; for BAM/CRAM files, represents total aligned and unaligned reads. Used for QC assessment, yield reporting, and billing calculations.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; CLIA

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this genomic artifact record in the registry, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks record modifications for audit trail compliance and change detection in downstream pipelines.
reference: ISO 8601; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; HIPAA Security Rule

description: Regulatory use classification of the artifact indicating its intended application context. 'RUO' (Research Use Only) — not for diagnostic use; 'IVD' (In Vitro Diagnostic) — cleared/approved for clinical diagnostics; 'LDT' (Laboratory Developed Test) — validated in-house clinical test; 'clinical' — used in clinical reporting; 'research' — used in research studies; 'internal_qc' — internal quality control purposes.
reference: FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration) IVD Regulations; IVDR (EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation 2017/746); CLIA; CAP

description: Total number of genomic variants (SNPs, indels, CNVs, SVs) recorded in this artifact for VCF and gVCF file types. Provides a high-level QC indicator and enables rapid assessment of variant calling yield without parsing the full VCF file.

Unique surrogate identifier for each variant annotation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a genomic variant annotation artifact with its own identity, lifecycle, and rich set of interpreted attributes derived from annotation pipelines.

Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Annotation pipeline deviations (e.g., annotation database version mismatch, unexpected ACMG reclassification in production) trigger formal deviations in GxP clinical genomics labs. Linking variant_ann
Reference to the bioinformatics annotation pipeline run that produced this annotation record. Enables data lineage tracing from raw FASTQ through to interpreted genomic results.
Reference to the biological sample from which the sequencing data and variant call originated. Provides traceability from annotation back to the source specimen managed in the LIMS.

Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Annotations inherit sequencing run context from variant calls for clinical reporting and quality assessment. This link enables run-level annotation QC, batch effect analysis, and regulatory audit trai

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Variant annotations (ClinVar classifications, ACMG criteria, oncology tier assignments) are submitted as clinical evidence in companion diagnostic and IVD submissions. Direct traceability from annotat

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Annotation pipelines used for clinical variant interpretation require validation demonstrating database version control, annotation accuracy, and ACMG classification consistency. Links annotation to v
Reference to the source VCF record from which this annotation was derived. Links the annotation back to the raw variant call produced by the variant calling pipeline.
reference: GA4GH VCF Specification v4.3; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.variant_knowledge_base. Business justification: Variant annotations are enriched from the clinical variant knowledge base — the authoritative source for ACMG classifications and ClinVar accessions. Tracing which knowledge base entry informed an ann

Variant classification assigned per ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines for germline variants (Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Variant of Uncertain Significance (VUS), Likely Benign, Benign). The authoritative classification for clinical genetic testing reports and regulatory submissions.
reference: ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines (Richards et al. 2015); CAP; CLIA; FDA IVD Guidance

description: Pipe-delimited list of ACMG/AMP evidence criteria codes applied to support the variant classification (e.g., PVS1|PS3|PM2|PP3). Provides the evidence basis for the classification decision and supports audit and regulatory review.
reference: ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines (Richards et al. 2015); CAP; CLIA

description: Version of the annotation database(s) used during annotation (e.g., ClinVar 2024-01, gnomAD v3.1.2, dbSNP b156). Required for regulatory submissions and reproducibility of clinical-grade annotations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; CLIA; ISO 15189; Veeva Vault

description: Current lifecycle status of the annotation record: pending (awaiting annotation), completed (annotation finalized), failed (annotation pipeline error), review_required (flagged for manual curation), superseded (replaced by a newer annotation run).
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Veeva Vault; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Timestamp when the annotation pipeline completed and this annotation record was generated. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the annotation lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Name of the annotation pipeline tool used to generate this annotation record (VEP=Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor, ANNOVAR, SnpEff). Enables tool-specific interpretation and reproducibility tracking.
reference: Ensembl VEP; ANNOVAR; SnpEff; Benchling R&D Notebook

description: Version string of the annotation tool used (e.g., VEP 108.0, ANNOVAR 2023-01-12). Critical for reproducibility, regulatory submissions, and audit trails per GxP and CLIA requirements.
reference: GxP (GLP/GCP); CLIA; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189

description: Combined Annotation Dependent Depletion (CADD) Phred-scaled score integrating multiple functional annotations to predict variant deleteriousness. Scores ≥20 indicate top 1% most deleterious variants. Used for variant prioritization in research and clinical pipelines.
reference: CADD (Kircher et al.); ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Chromosome on which the variant is located, using standard UCSC notation (e.g., chr1, chrX, chrM). Fundamental genomic coordinate for variant localization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chr1|chr2|chr3|chr4|chr5|chr6|chr7|chr8|chr9|chr10|chr11|chr12|chr13|chr14|chr15|chr16|chr17|chr18|chr19|chr20|chr21|chr22|chrX|chrY|chrM — promote to reference product]
reference: UCSC Genome Browser; GA4GH VCF Specification v4.3

description: ClinVar Reference Variant (RCV) accession number uniquely identifying the variant-condition record in the ClinVar database. Enables direct cross-referencing to the authoritative clinical variant database for regulatory submissions.

Clinical significance classification of the variant as curated in the NCBI ClinVar database (Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Uncertain Significance (VUS), Likely Benign, Benign). Central to clinical reporting and IVD/LDT assay interpretation.
reference: ClinVar; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines (Richards et al. 2015); FDA IVD Guidance

description: Sequence Ontology (SO) term describing the predicted molecular consequence of the variant on the transcript (e.g., missense_variant, frameshift_variant, stop_gained, splice_site_variant). Primary driver of clinical significance classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missense_variant|frameshift_variant|stop_gained|stop_lost|splice_donor_variant|splice_acceptor_variant|splice_site_variant|synonymous_variant|intron_variant|5_prime_UTR_variant|3_prime_UTR_variant|upstream_gene_variant|downstream_gene_variant|intergenic_variant — promote to reference product]
reference: Sequence Ontology (SO); Ensembl VEP; ACMG/AMP Guidelines

description: Catalogue of Somatic Mutations in Cancer (COSMIC) identifier for the variant (e.g., COSM476). Relevant for oncology applications, somatic variant interpretation, and OncoKB tier assignment.
reference: COSMIC; OncoKB; ACMG/AMP Somatic Variant Guidelines (Li et al. 2017)

description: Timestamp when this annotation record was first inserted into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and GxP/CLIA compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 27001; CLIA

description: NCBI dbSNP reference SNP identifier (rsID) for the variant (e.g., rs80357906). Standard cross-reference identifier used in clinical reports, publications, and regulatory submissions.
reference: NCBI dbSNP; GA4GH VCF Specification v4.3

description: Exon number within the canonical transcript where the variant is located (e.g., '14/23' indicating exon 14 of 23). Reported as a string to accommodate fractional or range notation. Used in clinical reports and splice-site analysis.

Official HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee) gene symbol for the gene in which the variant is located (e.g., BRCA1, TP53, EGFR). Primary human-readable identifier for gene-level reporting and clinical interpretation.

Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) of the variant in the Genome Aggregation Database (gnomAD) global population. Used to assess variant rarity and support pathogenicity classification per ACMG/AMP criteria (BA1, BS1, PM2).
reference: gnomAD v3.1; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines; MAF definition

description: gnomAD population subgroup for which the MAF is reported (e.g., AFR=African, AMR=Latino/Admixed American, EAS=East Asian, NFE=Non-Finnish European). Required for population-stratified allele frequency interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AFR|AMR|ASJ|EAS|FIN|NFE|OTH|SAS — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: gnomAD v3.1; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Human Genome Variation Society (HGVS) notation describing the variant at the coding DNA sequence level (e.g., NM_007294.4:c.5266dupC). Standard notation for clinical reporting, regulatory submissions, and variant database submissions.
reference: HGVS Nomenclature v20.05; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: HGVS notation describing the predicted amino acid change at the protein level (e.g., NP_009225.1:p.Gln1756Profs). Required for clinical variant reporting and interpretation of functional impact.
reference: HGVS Nomenclature v20.05; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Predicted functional impact severity of the variant as classified by the annotation tool (HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, MODIFIER per SnpEff/VEP convention). Used for variant prioritization in clinical and research workflows.
reference: SnpEff; Ensembl VEP; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Intron number within the canonical transcript where the variant is located (e.g., '5/22'). Populated for intronic and splice-site variants. Used in clinical reports and splicing impact assessment.

Indicates whether this annotation record is based on the canonical (primary) transcript for the gene as defined by Ensembl or MANE Select. Canonical transcript annotations are used for primary clinical reporting; non-canonical records are retained for completeness.
reference: Ensembl MANE Select; NCBI RefSeq Select; ACMG/AMP Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the variant is classified as somatic (True) or germline (False). Drives routing to oncology vs. hereditary disease interpretation workflows, and determines applicable regulatory and reporting frameworks.
reference: ACMG/AMP Somatic Variant Guidelines (Li et al. 2017); COSMIC; OncoKB

description: OncoKB-curated functional effect of the somatic mutation on the gene product (Gain-of-function, Loss-of-function, Neutral, Switch-of-function, Unknown). Supports oncology biomarker reporting and therapeutic decision support.
reference: OncoKB; ACMG/AMP Somatic Variant Interpretation Guidelines (Li et al. 2017)

description: OncoKB evidence-based therapeutic actionability tier for the somatic variant (Level 1=FDA-approved biomarker, Level 2=Standard care, Level 3A/3B=Investigational, Level 4=Biological evidence, R1/R2=Resistance). Critical for oncology precision medicine and clinical trial matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3A|3B|4|R1|R2 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: OncoKB (Chakravarty et al.); FDA Companion Diagnostic Guidance; ACMG/AMP Somatic Guidelines

description: Categorical PolyPhen-2 prediction for the amino acid substitution (probably damaging, possibly damaging, benign, unknown). Provides human-readable interpretation of the PolyPhen-2 score for clinical reporting.
reference: PolyPhen-2 (Adzhubei et al.); ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Polymorphism Phenotyping v2 (PolyPhen-2) score predicting the impact of an amino acid substitution on protein structure and function (0=benign, 1=probably damaging). Used as evidence for ACMG/AMP pathogenicity criteria (PP3, BP4).
reference: PolyPhen-2 (Adzhubei et al.); ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: 1-based genomic coordinate (POS) on the chromosome where the variant is located, as defined in the VCF specification. Used for precise variant localization and cross-database lookups.
reference: GA4GH VCF Specification v4.3; HGVS Nomenclature

description: Level of expert review applied to the variant classification (e.g., not_reviewed, single_submitter, expert_panel, practice_guideline). Mirrors ClinVar review status levels and indicates confidence in the reported classification for clinical use.
reference: ClinVar Review Status; ACMG/AMP Guidelines; CAP; CLIA

description: Categorical SIFT prediction for the amino acid substitution (deleterious or tolerated, with confidence qualifier). Provides human-readable interpretation of the SIFT score for clinical reporting.
reference: SIFT (Kumar et al.); ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: Sorting Intolerant From Tolerant (SIFT) score predicting whether an amino acid substitution affects protein function (0=deleterious, 1=tolerated). Used as evidence for ACMG/AMP pathogenicity criteria (PP3, BP4).
reference: SIFT (Kumar et al.); ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines

description: DNA strand orientation of the gene/transcript relative to the reference genome (+ for forward/sense strand, - for reverse/antisense strand). Required for correct HGVS notation and splice-site interpretation.

Timestamp when this annotation record was last modified (e.g., re-annotation, manual curation update, status change). Required for change tracking and GxP audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 27001; CLIA

description: Classification of the variant by its molecular nature: Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP), Insertion/Deletion (INDEL), Multi-Nucleotide Polymorphism (MNP), Copy Number Variation (CNV), Structural Variant (SV), or gene Fusion. Drives downstream clinical and research interpretation workflows.
reference: GA4GH VCF Specification v4.3; ClinVar; COSMIC

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each variant call result record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete genomic analysis event (variant detection/genotyping run output) with a clear lifecycle from raw FASTQ to interpreted result.

description: Average sequencing coverage depth (mean number of reads aligned per base) across the target region for this sample and run. A fundamental NGS QC metric; minimum thresholds vary by assay (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for targeted oncology panels).

description: Proportion of target bases achieving at least 20x sequencing coverage depth (range 0.0000–1.0000). A standard NGS QC threshold metric used to assess adequacy of coverage for reliable variant detection.

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the pipeline (e.g., 'NGS-ALIGN-BWA-001', 'VC-GATK-WGS-002'). Used in SOPs, regulatory submissions, and cross-system references.

Free-text narrative description of the pipeline's purpose, methodology, and key capabilities. Provides context for bioinformaticians, clinical scientists, and regulatory reviewers who need to understand the pipeline's function without reading the source code.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2 — Document Control; GxP Documentation Standards

description: Human-readable name of the bioinformatics pipeline (e.g., 'BWA-MEM Alignment Pipeline', 'GATK Variant Calling Pipeline', 'VEP Annotation Pipeline'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and user interfaces.

Current lifecycle state of the pipeline definition (draft, active, deprecated, retired, under_validation). Controls whether the pipeline is available for production runs. Active pipelines are cleared for use; deprecated/retired pipelines are preserved for historical traceability.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; Veeva Vault — Document Lifecycle

description: Categorical classification of the pipeline by its primary computational function (e.g., alignment, variant calling, annotation, CNV/SNP analysis, MAF calculation, QC). Drives workflow routing and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|snp_analysis|maf_calculation|qc|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Benchling R&D Notebook; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pipeline Classification

description: Name and version of the reference genome assembly used by this pipeline (e.g., 'GRCh38', 'GRCh37/hg19', 'GRCm39'). Critical for variant calling accuracy, annotation consistency, and regulatory traceability. Links to the Reference Genomics data domain.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; NCBI Reference Sequence (RefSeq); GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date on which this pipeline version was retired or deprecated and is no longer available for new production runs. Historical runs executed under this version remain traceable. Nullable for active pipelines.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; Veeva Vault — Document Lifecycle

description: Indicates whether the pipeline includes Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) detection and genotyping capability. SNP analysis is foundational for population genomics, pharmacogenomics, and GWAS studies.
reference: CAP Molecular Pathology Checklist; dbSNP (NCBI); FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVDs

description: Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the use and execution of this pipeline (e.g., 'SOP-BIO-0042-v3'). Required for GxP compliance and CLIA/CAP laboratory accreditation.
reference: GxP SOP Management; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Accreditation; MasterControl — Document Control

description: URL of the version-controlled source code repository for this pipeline (e.g., GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket repository URL). Enables code traceability, change history review, and CI/CD integration for GxP-compliant software development.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11 — Audit Trail; ISO 13485 Section 7.3; ICH Q10 — Pharmaceutical Quality System

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pipeline record. Required for GxP audit trail compliance and change management tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11 — Audit Trail; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; GDPR Article 5

description: Limit of Detection (LOD) for variant calling expressed as the minimum variant allele frequency (VAF) detectable by this pipeline. Critical for somatic variant calling pipelines and clinical assay validation documentation.
reference: FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVDs; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Molecular Pathology Checklist

description: Semantic version number of the pipeline (e.g., '1.0.0', '2.3.1'). Tracks iterative changes to algorithms, parameters, or reference genome compatibility. Critical for GxP validation traceability and reproducibility of genomic results.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) — Data Integrity; ISO 13485 Section 7.3

description: Workflow definition language used to implement the pipeline (e.g., WDL — Workflow Description Language, Nextflow, Snakemake, CWL — Common Workflow Language). Determines tooling requirements for execution and portability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WDL|Nextflow|Snakemake|CWL|Bash|Python|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each pipeline QC metric record generated during genomic data processing. Primary key for this entity.

Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that generated this QC metric. Links the metric to its parent execution context.

Reference to the biological sample whose processed data this QC metric evaluates. Enables per-sample QC trending and failure analysis.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Pipeline QC metrics (coverage depth, Q30 rates, mapping rates) are included as analytical validation evidence in regulatory submissions. Direct FK enables automated extraction of QC evidence for dossi

Type of genomic assay whose data was processed when this QC metric was generated (e.g., WGS, WES, NGS panel, RNA-seq). Metric thresholds and acceptable ranges vary by assay type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|NGS_panel|RNA_seq|amplicon|SNP_array|CNV_array — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Compute infrastructure on which the QC tool was executed (e.g., AWS HPC cluster, on-premise HPC, Databricks). Supports cloud cost attribution and environment-specific performance benchmarking.
Date and time when this QC metric record was first ingested into the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GDPR Article 5

description: Date and time when this QC metric was evaluated against its threshold. This is the principal business event timestamp for the metric record, used for longitudinal QC trending and SLA compliance tracking.

Structured description of why this metric failed its threshold (e.g., 'mean_coverage below 30x minimum', 'duplication_rate exceeds 20% threshold'). Populated only when qc_status is 'fail' or 'warn'. Enables systematic failure pattern analysis.

Indicates whether the sequencing data evaluated by this QC metric was generated using paired-end (True) or single-end (False) sequencing chemistry. Affects interpretation of insert size and duplication rate metrics.
Indicates whether a failure on this metric blocks downstream pipeline progression (True) or is advisory only (False). Critical for automated QC gating decisions in the bioinformatics pipeline.
Lane number on the sequencing flow cell from which the data for this QC metric was derived. Enables per-lane QC analysis to detect lane-specific quality issues.

Functional category grouping the QC metric for reporting and trend analysis. Enables domain-specific QC dashboards (e.g., coverage metrics vs. base quality metrics vs. alignment metrics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coverage|base_quality|duplication|alignment|contamination|library_complexity|variant_calling — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Wall-clock time in seconds taken by the QC tool to compute this metric. Supports HPC/cloud compute resource optimization and pipeline performance benchmarking.

Standardized name of the QC metric being recorded (e.g., mean_coverage, pct_q30_bases, duplication_rate, on_target_rate, uniformity, insert_size_median, gc_bias_score). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mean_coverage|pct_q30_bases|duplication_rate|on_target_rate|uniformity|insert_size_median|gc_bias_score|chimeric_read_rate|adapter_contamination_rate|phred_score_mean — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, FastQC, Picard, MultiQC, ISO 15189

description: Unit of measurement for the metric value (e.g., 'x' for coverage depth, '%' for percentage-based metrics, 'bp' for base-pair measurements, 'reads' for read counts). Enables correct interpretation and cross-metric comparison.
Numeric value of the QC metric as computed by the QC tool for this pipeline step and sample. This is the principal quantitative observation (e.g., 45.3 for mean_coverage, 0.92 for pct_q30_bases).
Minimum (or maximum, per threshold_direction) acceptable value for this metric to be considered passing. Defines the QC gate boundary used for automated pass/fail evaluation.
Logical stage of the bioinformatics pipeline at which this QC metric was evaluated. Enables stage-specific QC dashboards and failure pattern analysis across the FASTQ-to-VCF data lineage.

Automated evaluation result for this metric against the defined pass threshold. 'pass' indicates the metric meets the threshold; 'fail' triggers remediation workflow; 'warn' indicates a marginal result requiring review; 'pending' indicates evaluation not yet completed; 'not_evaluated' indicates the metric was captured but no threshold was applicable.

Name of the bioinformatics QC tool that computed this metric (e.g., FastQC, Picard, MultiQC, Samtools). Enables tool-specific metric interpretation and version-controlled reproducibility tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FastQC|Picard|MultiQC|GATK|Trimmomatic|Samtools|Qualimap|Fastp|Mosdepth|VerifyBamID — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, FastQC, Picard, MultiQC

description: Version string of the QC tool used to compute this metric (e.g., '0.11.9' for FastQC, '2.27.5' for Picard). Essential for reproducibility, audit trails, and identifying metric drift caused by tool upgrades.
Sequencing read length in base pairs (bp) for the run associated with this QC metric (e.g., 150 for 2x150bp paired-end). Read length affects coverage and base quality metric interpretation.

Regulatory classification of the assay context in which this QC metric was generated (IVD, RUO, LDT, clinical trial, research). Determines applicable QC standards and regulatory reporting obligations.

Documented corrective or remediation action taken or recommended in response to a QC failure (e.g., 're-sequence sample', 'increase library input', 'repeat library preparation'). Supports CAPA workflows and GxP compliance.
reference: ISO 15189, CLIA, GxP, FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Current status of the remediation workflow for a failed QC metric. 'not_required' for passing metrics; 'pending', 'in_progress', 'resolved', or 'escalated' for failing metrics requiring corrective action.
reference: ISO 15189, CLIA, GxP, FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Formal outcome of the QA/QC reviewer's assessment of this metric result. 'waived' indicates a documented exception approval for a failing metric. Required for GxP and clinical laboratory compliance.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, MasterControl

description: Date and time when a QA/QC reviewer formally reviewed this metric result. Supports GxP electronic record requirements and TAT (Turnaround Time) tracking for clinical workflows.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Document identifier of the SOP governing the QC evaluation procedure for this metric (e.g., 'SOP-BIO-042-v3'). Links the metric record to the controlled document system for GxP and regulatory compliance.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA, MasterControl, Veeva Vault

description: File system or object storage path to the QC tool output file (e.g., FastQC HTML report, Picard metrics file, MultiQC JSON) from which this metric was extracted. Supports data lineage and audit traceability.
File path or identifier of the BED file defining the genomic target regions used for on-target rate and coverage uniformity calculations. Applicable for WES and targeted panel assays.

Indicates whether the pass_threshold is a minimum bound (metric must be >= threshold), maximum bound (metric must be <= threshold), or a range (metric must fall within defined bounds). Required for correct automated QC gating logic.
Lower boundary of an acceptable range for this metric when threshold_direction is 'range'. Null when threshold_direction is 'min' or 'max'.
Upper boundary of an acceptable range for this metric when threshold_direction is 'range'. Null when threshold_direction is 'min' or 'max'.

Version identifier of the QC threshold specification applied during evaluation (e.g., 'v1.2.0'). Enables audit traceability when thresholds are updated over time and supports longitudinal comparability of QC results.
Date and time when this QC metric record was last modified (e.g., following reviewer update or remediation status change). Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GDPR Article 5

description: Documented scientific or clinical justification for waiving a QC failure and allowing downstream processing to proceed. Populated only when review_outcome is 'waived'. Required for regulatory audit trails.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, Veeva Vault
