business_glossary_term: Input FASTQ (Fast All Quality) File Path
description: Cloud or HPC storage path to the input FASTQ file(s) consumed by this pipeline run. FASTQ is the raw sequencing data format produced by NGS instruments. Required for data lineage tracking, reprocessing, and audit trail from raw data to result.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312 Data Integrity; ISO 27001 Information Asset Management

description: Average number of sequencing reads aligned to each base position in the target region or genome. A key QC metric indicating sequencing depth adequacy. Minimum thresholds vary by assay type (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for targeted panels).
reference: CAP NGS Checklist; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189

business_glossary_term: Output BAM (Binary Alignment Map) File Path
description: Cloud or HPC storage path to the output BAM (Binary Alignment Map) file produced by alignment pipeline runs. BAM is the standard compressed binary format for aligned sequencing reads. Used for downstream variant calling and data lineage.
reference: SAM/BAM Format Specification (hts-specs); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312

description: Cloud or HPC storage path to the final analytical report or summary output artifact (e.g., QC report, annotation summary, CNV report) generated by this pipeline run. Used for result delivery to downstream consumers and regulatory documentation.
reference: Veeva Vault Document Management; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4 Document Control; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

business_glossary_term: Output VCF (Variant Call Format) File Path
description: Cloud or HPC storage path to the output VCF (Variant Call Format) file produced by variant calling pipeline runs. VCF is the standard format for representing SNPs, indels, and structural variants. Central artifact for clinical and research genomic interpretation.
reference: VCF Format Specification v4.3 (hts-specs); FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests; IVDR EU 2017/746

description: Maximum RAM (in gigabytes) consumed at any point during this pipeline run. Used for resource right-sizing, out-of-memory failure diagnosis, and HPC/cloud instance type optimization.
reference: AWS EC2 Instance Sizing Guidelines; HPC Resource Management Best Practices

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases Above Phred Score Q30
description: Percentage of sequencing bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher, indicating 99.9% base call accuracy. A primary sequencing QC metric used to assess run quality before downstream analysis. Industry standard threshold is ≥80%.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Quality Metrics; CAP NGS Checklist; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests; Phred Score Standard

description: Percentage of total sequencing reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. A key alignment QC metric; low mapping rates indicate sample quality issues, contamination, or reference genome mismatch.
reference: SAMtools Alignment Statistics; CAP NGS Checklist; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Classification of the bioinformatics pipeline category executed in this run. Drives downstream routing, result schema expectations, and domain-specific SLA thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|snp_genotyping|methylation|rna_seq|gene_editing_qc — promote to reference product]
reference: Genomics England Bioinformatics Pipeline Classification; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Human-readable description of the specific QC metric(s) that caused this pipeline run to fail QC assessment (e.g., 'Mean coverage depth 18x below 30x threshold', 'Mapping rate 62% below 80% threshold'). Populated only when qc_pass_flag is False.
reference: CAP NGS Checklist; ISO 13485 Section 8.3 Nonconforming Output; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Boolean indicator of whether this pipeline run passed all defined QC thresholds (coverage depth, mapping rate, Phred score, variant count sanity checks). Drives automated downstream routing — only QC-passed runs proceed to clinical or research reporting.
reference: CAP NGS Checklist; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 13485 Section 8.2.4; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests

description: Regulatory classification of the intended use of this pipeline run's outputs. IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) runs are subject to FDA/IVDR validation requirements; RUO (Research Use Only) runs have different compliance obligations. Drives audit trail depth and result release controls.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 IVD Regulations; IVDR EU 2017/746; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 13485

description: Indicates whether this pipeline run is a reprocessing of a previously executed run (e.g., triggered by QC failure, pipeline version upgrade, or parameter correction). When True, data_lineage_run_id should reference the original run.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 13485 Section 8.3; CAP NGS Checklist

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to this pipeline execution at submission time. Used for cross-system traceability, SLA tracking, and audit references across LIMS, BaseSpace, and downstream reporting systems.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub Run Naming Convention; LabVantage LIMS Run Identifier Standard

description: Current lifecycle state of the pipeline execution. Drives operational dashboards, SLA breach alerting, failure triage workflows, and batch-level completion monitoring. Transitions: queued → running → completed | failed | cancelled.
reference: ServiceNow Workflow State Model; ISO 13485 Section 8.3 Nonconforming Output

description: The type of genomic sequencing assay whose data is being processed in this pipeline run (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel). Determines applicable pipeline parameters, QC thresholds, and regulatory classification.
reference: FDA Guidance on Next Generation Sequencing (NGS) Tests; IVDR EU 2017/746; CAP NGS Checklist

description: Indicates whether this pipeline run exceeded its defined SLA target TAT. Populated upon run completion. Drives SLA breach alerting, escalation workflows in ServiceNow, and operational performance reporting.
reference: ServiceNow SLA Management; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; Genomics Biotech SLA Policy

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Turnaround Time (Hours)
description: The contracted or internally defined SLA target for Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours for this pipeline run, based on assay type and regulatory classification. Used to assess SLA compliance by comparing against actual wall-clock duration.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 TAT Requirements; CAP Laboratory Accreditation; Genomics Biotech SLA Policy

description: The date and time when the pipeline run transitioned from queued to actively executing on the compute environment. Used to calculate queue wait time (start_timestamp minus submission_timestamp) and actual wall-clock execution duration.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; ServiceNow SLA Tracking

description: Total intermediate and output storage consumed by this pipeline run in gigabytes. Includes FASTQ inputs, BAM alignment files, VCF outputs, and annotation artifacts. Used for storage cost attribution and capacity planning.
reference: AWS S3 Storage Cost Model; Genomics Biotech Data Retention Policy; ISO 27001 Asset Management

description: The date and time when this pipeline run was submitted to the compute queue. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement start, TAT (Turnaround Time) calculation, and queue wait-time analytics.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 TAT Requirements

description: Total number of sequencing reads processed by this pipeline run. Used as a primary input volume metric for QC assessment, cost normalization, and performance benchmarking across pipeline runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub Run Metrics; CAP NGS Checklist; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests

description: The date and time when this pipeline_run record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail completeness per GxP requirements.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Total number of genomic variants (SNPs, indels, CNVs) identified and reported in the VCF output of this pipeline run. Used for QC sanity checks, result completeness validation, and cohort-level variant burden analytics.
reference: VCF Format Specification v4.3; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Tests; IVDR EU 2017/746

description: Total elapsed real-world time in seconds from pipeline start to completion. Used for performance benchmarking, SLA compliance verification, and identifying pipeline runs that exceed expected execution time thresholds.
reference: ServiceNow SLA Management; Genomics Biotech Bioinformatics SLA Policy

Whether this run met the acceptance criteria defined in the validation protocol. Values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, PENDING_REVIEW. Belongs to the relationship because acceptance is evaluated against the specific study's protocol criteria, not the batch's general quality disposition.

Number of deviations recorded specifically during the execution of this batch as a validation run. Distinct from the production_batch.deviation_count which covers all batch deviations. Run-specific deviations are evaluated against the validation protocol's acceptance criteria.

The date on which this production batch was executed as a validation run. May differ from the batch's manufacturing_start_date if the batch was produced for other purposes and subsequently designated as a validation run.

The overall outcome of this specific validation run: PASS, FAIL, or INCONCLUSIVE. Distinct from batch disposition — a batch may be released (PASS disposition) but still FAIL a validation run criterion.

The sequential order of this batch within the validation study (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Required by regulatory guidance to demonstrate consecutive run consistency. Belongs to the relationship, not to the batch or study alone.

description: Boolean indicator of whether the deviation investigation identified a systemic root cause requiring formal CAPA workflow to prevent recurrence. True triggers CAPA initiation.

Date and time when this deviation record was first created in the quality management system. Audit trail field for record lifecycle tracking.
Boolean indicator of whether affected customers must be notified of the deviation due to potential impact on product performance, safety, or data quality. Triggers customer communication workflow.

Detailed narrative description of what occurred, including the specific departure from the approved procedure, specification, or regulatory requirement. Must be sufficiently detailed for regulatory inspection review.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the deviation, typically system-generated and used in regulatory submissions and audit trails.

Current lifecycle state of the deviation: open (newly reported), under_investigation (root cause analysis in progress), pending_approval (awaiting QA sign-off), closed (investigation complete and approved), cancelled (determined not to be a deviation).

Classification of the deviation by operational category: process (procedure departure), equipment (instrument malfunction), material (reagent/consumable issue), environmental (cleanroom/storage condition), documentation (record error), or personnel (training/qualification gap).

Final quality decision on the affected batch, lot, or data: accept (meets specifications despite deviation), reject (does not meet specifications), rework (reprocess to meet specifications), retest (repeat testing), use_as_is (acceptable with justification), scrap (destroy).
Regulatory framework under which the deviation occurred: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice), GLP (Good Laboratory Practice), GCP (Good Clinical Practice), or non-GxP (research use only operations).
Description of containment actions taken immediately upon discovery of the deviation to prevent further impact, such as quarantine of affected materials, equipment shutdown, or process halt.
Target completion date for the deviation investigation and root cause analysis, typically driven by internal quality metrics or regulatory inspection readiness requirements.
Date and time when this deviation record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking and regulatory inspection readiness.
External system identifier linking this deviation to the corresponding record in MasterControl document control and training management system for cross-system traceability.
Date and time when the deviation actually occurred or was discovered during operations. May differ from reported_timestamp if deviation was identified retrospectively.
Quality assessment determining whether the deviation affected product quality, safety, efficacy, or data integrity. Includes scientific justification for disposition decision.
Boolean indicator of whether the deviation meets criteria for mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA (Field Alert Report, Medical Device Report) or EMA (Vigilance Report).

Unique surrogate identifier for each storage state change or custody transfer event record in the chain-of-custody audit trail. Primary key of the storage_event product.

Reference to the specific aliquot (sub-divided portion of a specimen) involved in this storage event. Nullable when the event applies to the primary specimen rather than a derived aliquot. Populated from LabVantage LIMS aliquot tracking.

Foreign key linking to instrument.asset. Business justification: Automated sample handling traceability: robotic liquid handlers and automated storage systems execute storage events in genomics labs. Linking storage_event to the instrument asset enables deviation r

Foreign key linking to sample.container. Business justification: storage_event tracks custody and storage state changes for specimens and aliquots. The physical container is central to storage tracking — storage_event currently carries container_barcode (STRING) an

Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP biobanking traceability: cryopreservation storage events consume specific reagent lots (e.g., DMSO, glycerol). Regulatory and GMP chain-of-custody requirements mandate recording which cryoprotecta

Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) to which the specimen or aliquot was moved or placed. Null for disposal or external transfer events where no internal destination exists.

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Storage events (temperature excursions, unauthorized access, location errors) trigger deviations. Labs document which storage event caused each deviation for regulatory compliance and audit trails.

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Storage events with temperature excursions, unauthorized transfers, or chain-of-custody breaks trigger nonconformance records. Genomics biobanks require direct LIMS-to-QMS linkage from the storage eve

Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) from which the specimen or aliquot was retrieved or transferred. Null for initial placement events where no prior location exists.
Reference to the primary biological specimen (parent sample) involved in this storage event. Links to the specimen master record in LabVantage LIMS for full sample metadata and IRB consent context.
reference: LabVantage LIMS, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Date and time at which the electronic acknowledgment or e-signature was applied to this storage event record. Distinct from the event_timestamp (when the physical action occurred). Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail completeness.

Monotonically increasing integer representing the ordinal position of this storage event within the complete chain-of-custody history for the specimen or aliquot. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full custody timeline for forensic genomics, legal, and regulatory audit purposes.
reference: GxP, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, ISO 15189, CAP, forensic genomics standards

description: Date and time at which this storage event record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Represents the record audit creation time, distinct from the event_timestamp which captures when the physical action occurred.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, ISO 15189, ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether this storage event involved a deviation from the applicable SOP or standard storage protocol. True triggers a deviation investigation workflow in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl) and requires documented justification.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, FDA 21 CFR Part 211, ISO 13485, Veeva Vault

description: Indicates whether the storage event has received a valid electronic acknowledgment (e-signature) from the responsible operator or custodian in compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements. True confirms the event is electronically signed and locked.

Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for this storage event, formatted as SE-{YYYY}-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}. Used in audit reports, chain-of-custody documentation, and regulatory submissions to uniquely reference a specific custody transfer or placement event.

Current lifecycle state of the storage event record. Indicates whether the event has been completed, is pending electronic acknowledgment, was cancelled, encountered a failure condition, or is under supervisory review for compliance purposes.

The precise real-world date and time at which the storage state change or custody transfer physically occurred, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. This is the principal business event time, distinct from the record audit timestamps. Critical for chain-of-custody sequencing and GxP compliance.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Categorical discriminator identifying the nature of the storage state change. Drives chain-of-custody logic and audit trail classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_placement|relocation|retrieval|return|inter_section_transfer|external_transfer|disposal|quarantine_placement — promote to reference product]
reference: GxP, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, ISO 15189, CAP

description: Name of the external facility, partner laboratory, or clinical site to which the specimen or aliquot was transferred during an inter-site or external transfer event. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer. Supports chain-of-custody documentation for multi-site genomics studies.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, HIPAA, GDPR

description: Shipping or courier tracking number associated with an external specimen transfer event. Enables end-to-end shipment traceability for specimens transferred to external sites, partner labs, or clinical collaborators. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, IATA P650 (Biological Substances), CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Cumulative number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen or aliquot has undergone up to and including this storage event. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid and protein integrity; this count is critical for sample quality assessment in genomic and clinical workflows.
reference: ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, GxP, CAP

description: Indicates whether this storage event record has been locked and made immutable following electronic acknowledgment and audit finalization. Immutable records cannot be modified or deleted, satisfying FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP requirements for tamper-evident audit trails.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Event Reference
description: The native event or transaction identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this storage event. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.

Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot involved in this storage event has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True means PHI has been removed or anonymized; False means the specimen retains linkage to identifiable patient data.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.514, GDPR Article 4, NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot was placed into or removed from quarantine status as part of this storage event. Quarantined samples are restricted from use in assays or downstream workflows pending investigation or quality review.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Standardized reason for placing the specimen or aliquot into quarantine. Populated only when quarantine_flag is True. Drives investigation workflows and determines the path to quarantine release or disposal.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Standardized reason code explaining the business justification for the storage event. Used for operational reporting, compliance audits, and workflow analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine_storage|retrieval_for_assay|transfer_to_external_site|quarantine|inter_section_transfer|disposal_scheduled|emergency_retrieval — promote to reference product]
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, CAP

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the storage event beyond the standardized reason code. Captures operator notes, special circumstances, or deviations from standard operating procedures (SOPs) that require documentation.

The maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used in conjunction with required_temp_min_c to define the acceptable temperature window.

The minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used to evaluate whether a temperature excursion occurred.

Document identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the storage event type performed. Links to the controlled document in MasterControl or Veeva Vault for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, FDA 21 CFR Part 211, MasterControl, Veeva Vault

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this storage event was originated or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the lakehouse and upstream operational systems.
reference: GxP, ISO 27001, Snowflake Data Cloud, LabVantage LIMS

description: Volume of the specimen or aliquot in microliters (µL) at the time of this storage event. Captured to track volume consumption across retrieval events and to flag low-volume specimens that may be insufficient for planned assays.
reference: ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, LabVantage LIMS

description: Standardized code representing the required storage condition category for the specimen or aliquot at the destination location. Drives storage unit assignment logic and temperature monitoring alert thresholds in the LIMS and facility management systems.
reference: ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, GxP, LabVantage LIMS

description: Recorded ambient or storage unit temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the storage event. Captured from connected monitoring systems or manually entered by the operator. Used to assess sample integrity and document temperature excursion conditions.
reference: GxP, ISO 15189, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from the required storage temperature range) was detected or reported in association with this storage event. True signals a potential sample integrity risk requiring investigation and documentation per GxP and CLIA requirements.
reference: GxP, CLIA 42 CFR Part 493, ISO 15189, FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Date and time at which this storage event record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Tracks the last update for audit trail completeness and data lineage purposes. Immutable records will not have an updated_timestamp after locking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, ISO 15189, ISO 27001

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the accession record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — represents the formal intake event of a specimen into laboratory custody.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Accessions represent received samples from customer accounts. Required for order fulfillment tracking, billing reconciliation, customer-specific SLA enforcement, and regulatory classification routing.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Clinical genomics accessions for regulated IVD assays must reference the regulatory approval (510(k), CE-IVD) under which the assay is cleared. gxp_applicable_flag on accession signals GxP context. Re

National Provider Identifier (NPI) or internal identifier of the ordering physician or clinical provider who requisitioned the test. Used for result reporting, billing, and clinical communication. Classified as confidential.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Ordering Provider; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.402 (NPI); CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241

description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Lab accessioning links each incoming specimen to the ordered assay/test product (e.g., WGS panel, array). This drives TAT tracking, billing, and result reporting. assay_panel_code is a denormalized re

Foreign key linking to sample.collection_event. Business justification: Accession (formal laboratory intake) and collection_event (physical specimen collection) are closely coupled transactional records in the sample lifecycle. An accession record corresponds to a specifi

Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Accessioning is performed under a controlled SOP (e.g., specimen receipt and accessioning procedure). GxP and CLIA compliance require the accession record to reference the governing controlled documen

Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Accessioning deviations (e.g., specimen received outside protocol temperature window, missing requisition) must be formally documented. GxP-regulated genomics labs require the accession event to refer

Reference to the originating clinical or research order that triggered specimen collection and submission. Links the accession to the order management domain, enabling traceability from test requisition through laboratory intake.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Order Linkage; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241

description: Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Specimen accession maps to an inbound delivery record for chain-of-custody, cold-chain compliance, and logistics tracking. Genomics biotech labs receiving specimens from external sites use inbound del

Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: In decentralized genomics testing models, accessions are processed on customer-installed instruments (e.g., on-site sequencers). Linking accession to installed_instrument enables instrument utilizatio

Foreign key linking to research.irb_submission. Business justification: Accessions for human subjects research must be traceable to the IRB submission authorizing sample collection — a GxP regulatory requirement. accession.irb_protocol_number is a denormalized plain attri
Reference to the laboratory site that physically received and accessioned the specimen. Supports multi-site operations, routing logic, and site-level compliance reporting under CLIA and CAP.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Site Configuration; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1

description: Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Specimens arriving in unacceptable condition (wrong tube, hemolysis, temperature excursion) trigger a nonconformance record. Genomics labs require QMS linkage from the accession receipt event to the f

Reference to the de-identified or pseudonymized patient/subject record associated with this specimen. Classified as restricted PHI under HIPAA. Used for clinical traceability while maintaining PHI separation per de-identification protocols.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; GDPR Article 9; IRB Protocol Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Accessions (sample receipt events) in clinical genomics labs are assigned to production batches for processing. Linking accession to production_batch enables TAT tracking from receipt to batch complet

Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Accessioned samples are collected under R&D projects. Linking accessions to projects enables project-level cohort analysis, R&D spend allocation to sample processing, IRB compliance reporting by proje
Reference to the biological sample master record in the Sample Management domain. Establishes the chain-of-custody link between the accession intake event and the canonical sample identity record.

Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Accessions are governed by customer service agreements that define TAT targets, QC thresholds, and billing terms. SLA compliance reporting and invoice generation for accessioned samples require knowin

Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Accessions are received in the context of specific studies. Study-level accession tracking is needed for enrollment monitoring, sample accountability reports, and TAT reporting by study. accession alr

Foreign key linking to sample.subject. Business justification: accession records the formal intake of a specimen into the laboratory. The specimen originates from a subject (the de-identified/pseudonymized biological source individual). accession has patient_id (

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In IVD/clinical genomics, sample accessions collected under regulated clinical trials or IVD studies must reference the governing regulatory submission (IND, IDE, PMA). accession.regulatory_submission

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: An accession is created for a specific orderable test. Linking accession to test_catalog normalizes the assay_panel_code denormalization, enables TAT SLA enforcement per test type, drives specimen req

LIMS-generated, externally-visible unique identifier assigned to the specimen at the point of intake into LabVantage. Serves as the primary chain-of-custody reference number communicated to ordering clinicians, research teams, and downstream bioinformatics pipelines. Format: ACC-{YYYY}-{8-digit sequence}.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Accession Number; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; CAP Laboratory Accreditation Standards

description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the accession record within LabVantage. Drives downstream routing: 'received' = physically in lab but not yet reviewed; 'in_review' = condition assessment underway; 'accepted' = cleared for processing; 'rejected' = specimen rejected and not processed; 'on_hold' = pending additional information or consent; 'cancelled' = order cancelled prior to processing.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Accession Workflow States; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232

description: Date and time the specimen was formally registered and accessioned into the LIMS by the receiving technician. May differ from receipt_timestamp if there is a delay between physical receipt and system registration. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Accession Registration Time; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Assessment of the physical condition of the specimen upon arrival at the laboratory. 'acceptable' = meets pre-analytical quality criteria; 'compromised' = degraded but may still be processable with notation; 'rejected' = fails minimum quality criteria and cannot be processed. Triggers rejection workflows and recollection requests when set to 'rejected'.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Condition on Arrival; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6; CAP Checklist GEN.40490; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232

description: Calendar date on which the biological specimen was collected from the patient or subject. Critical for pre-analytical stability assessments, TAT calculations, and clinical result interpretation. Distinct from receipt_timestamp.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Date; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; CAP Checklist GEN.40490; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Name or identifier of the clinical facility, hospital, clinic, or research site where the specimen was collected from the patient. Supports multi-site clinical trial tracking, referring provider analytics, and logistics routing.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Site; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Precise date and time the specimen was collected from the patient or subject, including timezone. Used for time-sensitive pre-analytical stability calculations (e.g., RNA degradation, cell viability) and clinical result contextualization.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Time; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232

description: Name of the phlebotomist, nurse, or clinician who collected the specimen from the patient. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and pre-analytical error investigation. Classified as confidential business data.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collector; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Free-text description of observed pre-analytical issues or anomalies noted at specimen receipt (e.g., hemolysis, lipemia, insufficient volume, broken container, temperature excursion, clotted sample). Supports root cause analysis and pre-analytical QC reporting.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Condition Comments; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Status of the patient or subject's informed consent for specimen use in research or clinical testing. 'consented' = valid consent obtained; 'declined' = subject declined consent; 'pending' = consent process initiated but not completed; 'withdrawn' = previously consented subject has withdrawn consent; 'not_required' = clinical specimen exempt from research consent requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; GDPR Article 7 & 9; 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule); IRB Protocol Requirements

description: Type of container or tube in which the specimen was collected and transported (e.g., EDTA tube, SST tube, PAXgene RNA tube, cryovial, FFPE block, dry swab). Determines extraction protocol eligibility and pre-analytical QC criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edta_tube|sst_tube|paxgene_rna|paxgene_dna|cryovial|ffpe_block|dry_swab|streck_tube|citrate_tube — promote to reference product]
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Container Type; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4; CLSI GP44-A4

description: Name of the courier or logistics provider that transported the specimen to the laboratory (e.g., FedEx, World Courier, BioMatik, internal courier). Used for vendor performance tracking and temperature excursion investigations.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Courier; IATA DGR P650; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was first created in the source LIMS system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and GxP 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 27001; LabVantage LIMS Audit Trail

description: Test requisition number assigned by the ordering facility's own system (e.g., hospital LIS, EHR order number). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the external ordering system and the internal LIMS accession record.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — External Reference; HL7 FHIR ServiceRequest.requisition; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241

description: Indicates whether this accession falls under GxP (Good Practice Regulations) requirements including GMP, GLP, or GCP. True = GxP controls apply, requiring full audit trail, electronic signature compliance per 21 CFR Part 11, and enhanced documentation in MasterControl.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; GCP ICH E6(R2); ISO 13485:2016

description: Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this accession has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True = PHI has been de-identified and the record may be used in downstream analytics without PHI restrictions; False = PHI is present and full access controls apply.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; GDPR Article 89; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Processing priority assigned to the accession at intake. STAT (Short Turn-Around Time) specimens require immediate processing; routine specimens follow standard workflow queues; urgent specimens are expedited but below STAT; research specimens follow non-clinical timelines. Drives SLA (Service Level Agreement) enforcement and workflow queue prioritization in LabVantage.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Priority Flag; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; Internal SLA Policy

description: Date and time the specimen was physically received at the laboratory, marking the transition from external courier custody to laboratory custody. This is the principal business event timestamp for the accession transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Receipt Date/Time; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Indicates whether a recollection request has been issued to the ordering provider or collection site due to specimen rejection or insufficient quality. True = recollection has been requested; False = no recollection required.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Recollection Workflow; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Indicates whether this accession is associated with a regulatory submission (e.g., FDA IND/NDA clinical trial specimen, CE-IVD validation study, EMA submission). True = specimen is part of a regulated submission context requiring enhanced documentation and audit trail.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); FDA 21 CFR Part 820; IVDR EU 2017/746; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Veeva Vault — Regulatory Document Linkage

description: Standardized code indicating the primary reason a specimen was rejected at accessioning. Populated only when arrival_condition = 'rejected'. Used for pre-analytical QC trending, rejection rate KPI reporting, and recollection request generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_volume|hemolysis|lipemia|clotted|wrong_tube|unlabeled|temperature_excursion|expired_container|broken_container|mislabeled — promote to reference product]
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Rejection Reason; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6; CAP Checklist GEN.40490; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1232

description: Native record identifier from the LabVantage LIMS source system for this accession. Used for data lineage traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational system of record, supporting reconciliation and re-ingestion workflows.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Internal Record ID; ISO 27001; GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Standardized classification of the biological specimen material submitted for analysis (e.g., whole blood, FFPE tissue, saliva, buccal swab, plasma, serum, urine, bone marrow, CSF). Drives QC thresholds, extraction protocols, and assay eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|ffpe_tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|plasma|serum|urine|bone_marrow|csf|fresh_frozen_tissue — promote to reference product]
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Specimen Type Codelist; SNOMED CT Specimen Hierarchy; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Laboratory storage location code (freezer, rack, box, position) where the specimen was placed upon acceptance. Supports physical specimen retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody continuity within the LIMS.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Storage Location; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.7; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Target Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours from specimen receipt to result delivery, as defined by the SLA (Service Level Agreement) for the assigned priority level and assay type. Used for TAT compliance monitoring and SLA breach alerting.
reference: Internal SLA Policy; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1291; CAP Checklist GEN.40490

description: Measured temperature of the specimen or shipping container at the time of receipt, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and flag potential temperature excursions that may affect specimen integrity and downstream assay validity.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Receipt Temperature; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.5; IATA DGR P650

description: Required transport temperature condition under which the specimen was shipped to the laboratory. Used to verify compliance with specimen stability requirements and to flag potential temperature excursions during transit.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Transport Condition; IATA DGR P650; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.5

description: Barcode or label identifier physically affixed to the specimen container at the time of collection or receipt. Used for physical specimen tracking, label verification at accessioning, and reconciliation against the LIMS accession number.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Container Barcode; CAP Checklist GEN.40490; ISBT 128 Standard

description: Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was most recently modified in the source LIMS system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and GxP audit trail requirements.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 27001; LabVantage LIMS Audit Trail

description: Intended use classification of the specimen at the time of accessioning. 'clinical' = IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) or LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) clinical testing; 'research' = RUO (Research Use Only) investigation; 'QC' = Quality Control specimen for assay performance monitoring; 'validation' = analytical validation or clinical validation study specimen.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; FDA IVD Guidance; CLIA 42 CFR §493; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Measured volume of the specimen received at the laboratory, expressed in millilitres. Used to assess adequacy for requested assays, trigger insufficient volume rejection flags, and support aliquoting planning. Represents the principal quantitative measurement of the accession transaction.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Volume Received; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6; CLSI EP7-A2

description: Unit of measure for the volume_received_ml field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens. Allows the schema to accommodate non-liquid specimen types without type conversion.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Volume Unit; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4; CLSI EP7-A2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen collection event record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Collection events occur at customer sites. Essential for site-specific protocol compliance monitoring, collection kit logistics coordination, site performance metrics, and regulatory audit trails. Rea

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Collection kit batch traceability is a GMP requirement — knowing which supply batch of collection kit material was used at each collection event enables batch recalls and deviation investigations. col

Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products (PAXgene, Tempus, saliva kits). Critical for tracking kit performance across collection sites, supplier quality monitoring, and correlating pre-a

business_glossary_term: Collection Kit Catalog Item Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products. Currently denormalized as collection_kit_sku. Business need: kit lot traceability for regulatory compliance, collection protocol compliance veri

Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP/GLP collection kit lot traceability: collection_kit_lot_number is a plain text denormalization. Regulatory requirements (CAP, CLIA, ISO 15189) mandate linking collection events to specific reagent

Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) document in MasterControl that governed this collection event. Ensures traceability to the approved procedure version used, supporting GxP compliance and audit readiness.
reference: MasterControl — Document Control; GxP (GLP/GCP); ISO 13485:2016 §4.2.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 820.40

description: Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Collection events with protocol deviations (wrong tube, collection outside time window, fasting protocol violated) require formal deviation records. The plain attributes deviation_flag/description/typ

Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Collection events are initiated by service orders in genomics biotech; linking to order.header enables TAT reporting, billing reconciliation, and order-to-collection traceability. collection_order_num

Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: collection_event has a collection_site_code column typed as BIGINT — this is semantically a reference to a lab_site rather than a free-text code. lab_site is the authoritative master for laboratory an

Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Collection kits are materials with SKUs and lot numbers. Linking enables kit inventory management, automated consumption tracking, lot traceability for specimen provenance, and recall management. Remo

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Collection nonconformances (hemolysis at collection, insufficient volume collected, wrong anatomical site) trigger NCRs distinct from deviations. Genomics labs require direct linkage from collection e

Reference to the research study, clinical trial, or diagnostic project under which this collection event was performed. Enables cohort-level aggregation and study-specific pre-analytical QC reporting.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Study Module; ICH E6(R2) GCP; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Reference to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved protocol under which this collection event was conducted. Mandatory for research specimens; ensures regulatory traceability of consent and ethical approval.
reference: IRB Protocol Requirements; 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule); FDA 21 CFR Part 50; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Reference to the informed consent record obtained from the subject prior to collection. Links to the IRB consent management entity. Mandatory for research and clinical genomics specimens; supports HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; GDPR Article 7; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; 45 CFR Part 46

description: Reference to the primary biological sample record created as a result of this collection event. Links to the sample master entity in the Sample Management domain for downstream LIMS workflow assignments, aliquoting, and storage tracking.

Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Collection events occur within studies — study-level collection tracking is required for enrollment monitoring, protocol deviation reporting, and collection timeline analysis. collection_event links t

Reference to the human subject, patient, or research participant from whom the specimen was collected. Links to the subject/patient master record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category. Classified restricted/pii_identifier as it directly identifies a research or clinical participant.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; GDPR Article 4; IRB Protocol Requirements; LabVantage LIMS — Subject Module

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In clinical trial genomics, collection events are performed under a regulatory submission (IND, IDE, PMA clinical study). collection_event.irb_protocol_number signals regulated context. Linking to sub

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: A specimen collection event is directly triggered by a clinical test order. This link enables collection-to-order traceability for TAT calculation, recollection workflows, and regulatory audit trails.

Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that collectors are qualified to perform collections. Labs link training to collection events for regulatory compliance and audit trails.

Standardized anatomical location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left antecubital vein, right breast core, nasopharynx). Encoded using SNOMED CT or ICD-10 topography codes where applicable. Critical for oncology and clinical genomics interpretation.
reference: SNOMED CT Anatomy Hierarchy; ICD-10-CM Topography; HL7 FHIR Specimen.collection.bodySite; CAP Cancer Protocols

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the specimen was collected. Required for international regulatory compliance (GDPR, IVDR), cross-border data transfer governance, and geographic cohort stratification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GDPR Article 44 (Cross-border transfers); IVDR 2017/746

description: Calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which the specimen was collected. Stored separately from collection_timestamp to support date-level partitioning, reporting, and cases where only the date (not exact time) is known or required.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Event; ISO 15189:2022; HIPAA Safe Harbor De-identification

description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the collection event, assigned by LabVantage LIMS at accessioning. Used for chain-of-custody documentation, deviation reports, and cross-system traceability. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.

Standardized method used to obtain the biological specimen. Drives pre-analytical QC interpretation and NGS library preparation protocol selection. Values: venipuncture (blood draw), biopsy (tissue), swab (mucosal/surface), saliva_kit (self-collected saliva), liquid_biopsy (cell-free DNA from blood), fine_needle_aspiration (FNA cytology).
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Method Reference; CLSI GP33; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.3

description: Free-text field for additional collector observations, special circumstances, or contextual notes recorded at the time of collection that do not fit into structured fields. Supports clinical interpretation and deviation investigation.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Event Notes; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Version number of the SOP document used at the time of collection (e.g., v1.2, v3.0). Stored alongside the SOP ID to capture the exact approved version in effect, as SOP versions change over time.

Ambient or transport temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of or immediately following specimen collection. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and pre-analytical stability. Critical for cfDNA liquid biopsy and RNA specimens.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pre-Analytical Conditions; CLSI; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.5

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the physical specimen was collected from the subject or environment. This is the principal real-world event time and is critical for pre-analytical time calculations (e.g., time-to-processing, cold-chain compliance). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Event; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.4

description: Number of collection tubes or containers used during the collection event. Relevant for blood draw events with multiple tube types (EDTA, SST, Streck cfDNA). Supports aliquot planning and chain-of-custody reconciliation.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Event; CLSI H3-A6

description: Priority classification of the collection event that governs Turnaround Time (TAT) expectations and workflow prioritization in LabVantage LIMS. 'stat' = immediate priority; 'urgent' = same-day; 'routine' = standard TAT.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Workflow Priority; SLA Management; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Volume of specimen collected, expressed in millilitres (mL). Used for pre-analytical QC assessment (e.g., short draw detection), yield estimation, and aliquot planning. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category (non-monetary transaction).
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Collection Metrics; CLSI H3-A6 (Venipuncture); ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Unit of measure for the collection volume field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens; uL for micro-volume collections.

Professional certification or license number of the individual who performed the collection (e.g., phlebotomy certification, nursing license). Required for CLIA and CAP compliance documentation and chain-of-custody integrity.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR §493.1489; CAP Accreditation Standards; ISO 15189:2022 §5.1

description: Timestamp when the collection event record was first created in LabVantage LIMS. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Audit Trail; ISO 27001; GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Current lifecycle state of the collection event workflow. 'scheduled' = collection planned but not yet performed; 'in_progress' = collector has initiated; 'completed' = specimen successfully collected and handed off; 'cancelled' = event voided before collection; 'deviated' = completed with documented pre-analytical deviation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Workflow State Machine; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4

description: Pre-analytical fasting status of the subject at the time of collection. Relevant for metabolomics, lipid panels, and certain genomic assays where fasting state affects analyte stability and downstream data quality interpretation.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pre-Analytical Conditions; CLSI EP7; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.2

description: Semi-quantitative assessment of hemolysis in the collected blood specimen, graded on a standardized scale (none, 1+ to 4+). Hemolysis is a key pre-analytical variable that degrades DNA/RNA quality and affects NGS library preparation success rates.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Specimen QC; CLSI EP7; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.6

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Accession Number
description: Accession number assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of specimen receipt and registration. Serves as the primary LIMS-side identifier for chain-of-custody tracking and workflow assignment.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Accessioning Module; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.4

description: Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this collection event has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method. True = de-identified; False = identifiable PHI present. Governs data access controls and downstream sharing permissions.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; GDPR Article 89; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Date and time the specimen was received at the processing laboratory. Used to calculate time-in-transit and total pre-analytical time (collection to receipt). Critical for TAT reporting and cold-chain compliance verification.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Specimen Receipt; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.4; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1241

description: Planned calendar date for the collection event as defined in the study protocol or clinical order. Compared against collection_date to calculate collection compliance and protocol adherence metrics.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Study Schedule; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Clinical Trial Protocol

description: Biological specimen type collected. Determines downstream processing protocols, storage conditions, and applicable NGS assay types (e.g., WGS, WES, liquid biopsy ctDNA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|buffy_coat|saliva|tissue_ffpe|tissue_fresh_frozen|buccal_swab|urine|csf|stool|bone_marrow|amniotic_fluid — promote to reference product]
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Specimen Type Reference; SNOMED CT; HL7 FHIR Specimen Resource; ISO 15189:2022

description: Elapsed time in minutes between specimen collection timestamp and the start of primary processing (e.g., centrifugation, nucleic acid extraction). A critical pre-analytical variable for NGS data quality; exceeding thresholds may trigger QC flags or deviation records.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pre-Analytical Metrics; CLSI H18-A4; ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.5

description: Required transport temperature condition for the collected specimen from collection site to processing laboratory. Drives cold-chain logistics and is compared against actual temperature readings for pre-analytical QC.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Transport Conditions; IATA P650 (Biological Substances); ISO 15189:2022 §5.4.5

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the collection event record in LabVantage LIMS. Supports audit trail requirements and Silver Layer incremental load detection. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Audit Trail; GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 27001

description: Regulatory use classification of the specimen collected. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic; LDT = Laboratory Developed Test; clinical_trial = collected under an approved clinical trial protocol; quality_control = internal QC specimen. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, CLIA, CAP) and data handling requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 809 (IVD); CLIA 42 CFR §493; CAP Accreditation; ISO 13485:2016

description: Unique identifier for the nucleic acid extraction record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Extraction method validations use real extraction runs to demonstrate performance. Labs track which extractions were part of validation studies for regulatory submissions and method qualification.

Absorbance ratio at 260nm and 230nm wavelengths, indicating contamination by organic compounds or chaotropic salts. Expected range is 2.0-2.2 for pure nucleic acid.

description: Unique barcode identifier assigned to the extracted nucleic acid aliquot for tracking and traceability.

description: Percentage of RNA fragments greater than 200 nucleotides, used as quality metric for degraded RNA samples (e.g., FFPE). Values >30% are typically acceptable for NGS.

description: Final elution volume of the extracted nucleic acid, measured in microliters (µL).

reference: ISO 15189 Laboratory Quality Management - Information Management

description: Free-text field for operator notes, observations, or deviations from standard protocol during the extraction process.
Type of nucleic acid extracted: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid), RNA (ribonucleic acid), cfDNA (cell-free DNA), gDNA (genomic DNA), total nucleic acid, or mRNA (messenger RNA).
reference: ISO 20395 Biotechnology Requirements for Evaluating Performance of Quantification Methods

description: Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or protocol used for extraction, ensuring traceability to documented procedures.
reference: ISO 15189 Laboratory Quality Management - Document Control

description: Quality control determination for the extraction: pass (meets all QC criteria), fail (does not meet QC criteria), pending (QC not yet complete), conditional_pass (marginal quality, proceed with caution).

description: Method used to quantify the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., NanoDrop spectrophotometry, Qubit fluorometry, Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, qPCR, PicoGreen).

Storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., -20°C for DNA, -80°C for RNA).
reference: ISO 20166-1 Molecular In Vitro Diagnostic Examinations - Pre-examination Processes

description: Total yield of extracted nucleic acid in nanograms (ng), calculated as concentration multiplied by elution volume.
reference: ISO 20395 Biotechnology Requirements for Evaluating Performance of Quantification Methods

File system path or cloud storage URI to the demultiplexed FASTQ files for this specimen from this run. Links sequencing data to downstream bioinformatics pipelines.

Unique index barcode sequence (or dual-index pair) assigned to this specimen for multiplexed sequencing. Used by demultiplexing software to separate reads by specimen. Format: single index (ACGTACGT) or dual index (ACGTACGT+TGCATGCA). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.

Molar concentration (nanomolar, nM) of this specimen's library at the time of loading into the sequencing run. Influences cluster density and read balance across multiplexed specimens. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.
Average insert size (distance between paired-end reads) for this specimen's library in base pairs. Used to assess library preparation quality and detect size selection issues.
Percentage of this specimen's reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. Low alignment rates indicate contamination, adapter issues, or sample quality problems.

Percentage of bases with Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥99.9% accuracy) for this specimen's reads in this run. Key quality metric for per-specimen QC assessment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.
Position or slot number of this specimen within the multiplexed pool loaded into the lane. Used for tracking specimen order during library pooling. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.
Date and time when QC review was completed and run_qc_status was assigned for this specimen-run pairing.
Boolean flag indicating whether this specimen requires re-sequencing due to insufficient coverage, quality issues, or run failure. Drives downstream workflow decisions.

Quality control status for this specimen in this run: pass (meets quality thresholds), fail (requires re-sequencing), warning (marginal quality), pending (QC not yet complete). Drives re-sequencing decisions. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.

Planned average sequencing coverage depth (e.g., 30X for WGS, 100X for WES) for this specific specimen in this run. May differ from the run-level target if specimens have different coverage requirements. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.

Unique surrogate identifier for the aliquot record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the aliquot data product within the sample domain. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a physical sub-divided specimen unit managed as an independent trackable resource in LabVantage LIMS.

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: GMP traceability requires linking each aliquot to the specific supply batch of material used during aliquoting. Enables batch recall investigations and deviation root-cause analysis — a standard regul
FK to sample.container

Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Aliquot preparation uses specific supply materials (buffers, consumables). GMP and LIMS traceability requires knowing which material was used per aliquot for quality investigations and regulatory audi

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent aliquot when this record represents a secondary or tertiary sub-division (aliquot-of-aliquot). Null for first-generation aliquots derived directly from the specimen. Enables multi-level aliquot hierarchy traversal.

Classification of the aliquot within the sub-division hierarchy. 'primary' indicates first-generation aliquot from specimen; 'secondary' and 'tertiary' indicate downstream sub-divisions; 'pooled' indicates a combined aliquot from multiple sources; 'reference' indicates a reference material aliquot.

Date and time at which this aliquot was physically created from the parent specimen or parent aliquot. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp for this entity. Used for pre-analytical variable tracking, stability calculations, and chain-of-custody documentation.
reference: LabVantage LIMS Aliquoting Workflow; ISO 15189; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP)

description: Unique barcode label affixed to the physical container holding this aliquot. Used for barcode-driven tracking, scanning at workstations, and chain-of-custody verification throughout laboratory workflows in LabVantage LIMS.

description: Date on which the original biological specimen was collected from the subject. Inherited from the parent specimen record. Critical for calculating specimen age, assessing pre-analytical variable impact, and meeting regulatory reporting timelines under CLIA and CAP.

description: Measured concentration of the biological analyte (e.g., DNA, RNA, protein) in the aliquot. The unit of measure is captured in concentration_unit. Critical for library preparation normalization in Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) workflows and Quality Control (QC) acceptance criteria.

'in_use' indicates container holds active aliquot material; 'empty' indicates container has been emptied; 'discarded' indicates container has been disposed of; 'pre_registered' indicates container registered in LabVantage LIMS prior to receiving material (empty container inventory management).

Timestamp when this aliquot record was first created in the source LabVantage LIMS system and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, CLIA, and SOX financial reporting for laboratory operations.

description: Date on which the aliquot was physically disposed of or destroyed per retention policy, consent withdrawal, or regulatory requirement. Populated when lifecycle_status transitions to 'disposed' or 'destroyed'. Required for audit trail completeness under GxP and HIPAA.
reference: LabVantage LIMS Disposal Workflow; ISO 15189; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; GxP Good Practice Regulations

description: Reason code for the disposal or destruction of the aliquot. Supports regulatory audit trails and consent management reporting. 'consent_withdrawn' is critical for GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure and HIPAA consent revocation workflows.

description: DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score measured by TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to genomic DNA aliquots. Used to assess suitability for Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) and Whole Exome Sequencing (WES) library preparation. Null for non-DNA aliquots.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Metrics; ISO 15189; Agilent TapeStation DIN Standard

description: Date after which the aliquot is considered expired and should not be used in clinical or research workflows. Calculated based on specimen type, storage conditions, and validated stability data per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). Triggers automated alerts in LabVantage LIMS for disposal or review.
reference: LabVantage LIMS Stability Management; ISO 15189; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP); FDA 21 CFR Part 211

description: Number of freeze-thaw cycles the aliquot has undergone since creation. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid integrity and protein activity. Used to enforce maximum freeze-thaw limits per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) and to flag aliquots that may be unsuitable for sensitive assays.
reference: LabVantage LIMS Sample Integrity Tracking; ISO 15189; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP)

description: Numeric depth of this aliquot within the parent-child hierarchy. Level 1 indicates a direct sub-division of the primary specimen; level 2 indicates an aliquot-of-aliquot; and so on. Enables efficient hierarchy traversal queries and enforces maximum sub-division depth policies.

Original volume of biological material dispensed into the aliquot container at the time of aliquoting, expressed in microliters (µL). Provides the baseline for volume consumption tracking and yield calculations.

Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this aliquot has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True indicates de-identified; False indicates PHI is present and access controls apply. Governs data sharing eligibility for research and external collaborations.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514 De-identification Standard; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GDPR Article 89

description: Human-readable label or name assigned to the aliquot for identification in laboratory workflows, reports, and LIMS displays. May include specimen accession number, aliquot sequence suffix, or study-specific naming conventions.

description: Current state of the aliquot in its operational lifecycle. 'available' indicates ready for use; 'consumed' indicates fully used in a workflow; 'depleted' indicates volume exhausted; 'reserved' indicates allocated to a pending workflow; 'quarantined' indicates held pending Quality Control (QC) review; 'disposed' indicates removed per retention policy; 'destroyed' indicates intentionally destroyed per protocol. Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 230nm measured by spectrophotometry. Indicates contamination by chaotropic salts, EDTA, or carbohydrates. Acceptable range is typically 2.0–2.2. Complements the A260/A280 ratio for comprehensive nucleic acid Quality Control (QC) assessment.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Metrics; ISO 15189; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP)

description: Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 280nm measured by spectrophotometry (e.g., NanoDrop). Indicates nucleic acid purity; acceptable DNA purity is typically 1.8–2.0 and RNA purity 2.0–2.2. Values outside range may indicate protein or solvent contamination affecting downstream Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) performance.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Metrics; ISO 15189; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP)

description: Intended downstream use of the aliquot. Drives workflow routing in LabVantage LIMS and determines which bioinformatics pipelines or Quality Control (QC) processes the aliquot will enter. Values include sequencing (Next-Generation Sequencing/NGS workflows), qc (Quality Control testing), archive (long-term storage), backup (redundancy), assay_validation (Clinical Assay Validation), and research (Research and Development).
reference: LabVantage LIMS Workflow Assignment; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; GxP Good Laboratory Practice (GLP)

description: Free-text notes recorded by laboratory personnel during Quality Control (QC) assessment. Documents observations, deviations, conditional pass justifications, or remediation actions taken. Supports audit trail requirements under Good Practice Regulations (GxP) and CLIA.

description: Result of the Quality Control (QC) assessment for this aliquot. 'pass' indicates aliquot meets all acceptance criteria for its intended purpose; 'fail' indicates one or more criteria not met; 'pending' indicates QC not yet completed; 'conditional_pass' indicates aliquot approved with documented exceptions; 'not_required' indicates QC waived per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP).
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Workflow; ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; GxP Good Practice Regulations

description: Identifier of the storage rack, plate, or box within the storage unit where this aliquot is positioned. Combined with well_position provides the precise physical location for robotic retrieval and manual locating in biobank operations.

RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to RNA aliquots only. Minimum RIN thresholds are defined per assay type (e.g., RIN ≥ 7 for RNA-seq library preparation). Null for non-RNA aliquots.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Metrics; ISO 15189; Agilent Bioanalyzer RIN Standard

description: Biological specimen type of the aliquot material (e.g., whole blood, plasma, serum, saliva, tissue, FFPE, cell line, DNA extract, RNA extract, cfDNA). description: Required storage temperature for this aliquot in degrees Celsius (°C). Common values include -80°C (ultra-low for long-term nucleic acid storage), -20°C (standard freezer), 4°C (refrigerated), and ambient (15–25°C). Drives cold-chain compliance monitoring and storage assignment.
