description: Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: subject has a collection_site_code (STRING) that identifies the site where the subject was enrolled/collected. lab_site is the authoritative master for sites within this domain. Adding lab_site_id FK

Foreign key linking to clinical.patient. Business justification: In precision medicine genomics, a research subject and a clinical patient represent the same individual. This link enables return-of-results workflows, incidental findings reporting obligations, longi

Foreign key linking to quality.risk_assessment. Business justification: Subject-level re-identification risk assessments are a formal regulatory requirement under GDPR Article 35 and genomic data governance frameworks. The plain attribute reidentification_risk_level shoul

Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Subjects are enrolled in studies — this is the fundamental enrollment relationship in clinical genomics research. Subject-to-study traceability is required for participant management, adverse event re

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In IVD clinical studies and genomics trials, subjects are enrolled under a specific regulatory submission (IDE, IND). This is distinct from IRB submission — it tracks the device/drug regulatory filing

Age of the subject in whole years at the time of study enrollment, derived from year_of_birth and enrollment_date at data ingestion. Stored as a pre-computed integer to support cohort age-stratification queries without requiring DOB. Enables pediatric vs. adult cohort segmentation and age-related genomic analyses.
reference: HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR §164.514(b); ICH E6(R2) GCP; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Self-reported or genetically inferred ancestry/ethnicity group of the subject, used for population genomics stratification, Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) reference panel selection, and pharmacogenomics analyses. Stored as a controlled vocabulary term aligned with gnomAD and 1000 Genomes Project population labels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AFR|AMR|EAS|EUR|MID|SAS|ASH|LAT|OTH — promote to reference product]
reference: gnomAD Population Labels; 1000 Genomes Project; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; GDPR Article 9 Special Category Data

description: Indicates whether the subject has consented to long-term biobanking of residual specimens for future research use beyond the primary study. Governs specimen retention policies, storage duration limits, and permissible secondary use of aliquots in LabVantage LIMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISBER Best Practices for Repositories; GDPR Article 89; HIPAA; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Biological sex of the subject as recorded at enrollment, distinct from gender identity. Used for sex-stratified genomic analyses, reference genome selection (e.g., chrX/chrY copy number), population genomics cohort stratification, and clinical assay interpretation.
reference: HL7 FHIR Patient.gender; ISO 5218; NIH Sex as a Biological Variable (SABV) Policy

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the subject's specimens were collected. Drives GDPR data residency requirements, export control compliance for genomic data, and population genomics geographic stratification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GDPR Article 44 Data Transfers; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; IVDR 2017/746

description: The version identifier of the informed consent document that was in effect and signed by the subject at the time of enrollment into this cohort. Required for IRB compliance and consent audit trails.

Timestamp when the subject record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, HIPAA, and GDPR, and enables data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records); HIPAA Security Rule; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; ISO 27001

description: Permissible data access level for genomic data derived from this subject, aligned with NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy tiers. Open access data is publicly available; registered access requires user registration; controlled access requires dbGaP or equivalent approval; restricted access is limited to named investigators under a data use agreement (DUA).
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy (NOT-OD-14-124); dbGaP Data Access Policy; GDPR Article 89; HIPAA

description: The HIPAA-recognized or GDPR-compliant method applied to de-identify or pseudonymize the subject record. HIPAA Safe Harbor removes 18 specified identifiers; Expert Determination uses statistical analysis to certify re-identification risk is very small. Pseudonymization replaces direct identifiers with a code while retaining a linkage key in a separate access-controlled system. Drives data access tier assignment and downstream PHI handling rules.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514(b) Safe Harbor; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514(b)(1) Expert Determination; GDPR Article 4(5); GDPR Recital 26

description: Classification of the subject record's de-identification level per HIPAA and GDPR frameworks. Fully de-identified records have no re-identification pathway; pseudonymized records retain a coded linkage to PHI managed separately; limited dataset records retain certain geographic and date information under a data use agreement (DUA). Governs permissible downstream data uses and sharing.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HIPAA Limited Dataset 45 CFR §164.514(e); GDPR Article 89; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Primary disease indication or phenotype associated with the subject, encoded using a standardized ontology term (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO). Drives cohort assembly for clinical genomics studies, variant interpretation in clinical assay workflows, and regulatory submission stratification. Free-text or ontology code depending on study protocol.
reference: ICD-10-CM; OMIM; Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO); IVDR 2017/746; FDA IVD Guidance

description: Ontology or coding system used to encode the disease_indication field (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO, SNOMED-CT). Enables interoperability across clinical, research, and regulatory systems and ensures consistent variant interpretation and cohort queries.
reference: ICD-10-CM; OMIM; HPO; SNOMED-CT; HL7 FHIR Condition.code

description: Role of this subject within the family pedigree identified by family_id. Used for trio-based variant calling, de novo mutation detection, and inheritance pattern analysis in WGS/WES (Whole Exome Sequencing) workflows. Null for unrelated subjects.
reference: ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; HL7 FHIR FamilyMemberHistory

description: Indicates whether GDPR applies to this subject's data, based on the subject's collection country and data processing location. When True, GDPR Article 9 special category data protections apply to genetic data, and data subject rights (access, erasure, portability) must be honored. Drives EU data residency and transfer compliance workflows.
reference: GDPR Article 9; GDPR Article 4(13) Genetic Data; GDPR Article 44 Data Transfers; GDPR Recital 34

description: Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit consent for their genomic data to be shared with external repositories (e.g., dbGaP, EGA, UK Biobank). Required for NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance and international data sharing agreements. When False, data must not be submitted to public or controlled-access repositories.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy (NOT-OD-14-124); GDPR Article 7; HIPAA Authorization 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E6(R2) GCP

business_glossary_term: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Applicable
description: Indicates whether HIPAA Privacy Rule applies to this subject's data, based on whether the subject is a US patient or the data is held by a HIPAA-covered entity or business associate. When True, PHI handling, minimum necessary standard, and de-identification requirements apply.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR Part 164; HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; HHS Guidance on De-identification

description: Indicates whether the subject has consented to receive incidental or secondary genomic findings (e.g., ACMG SF v3.2 actionable secondary findings) identified during WGS/WES analysis. Drives the return-of-results workflow and clinical reporting obligations for pathogenic variants in medically actionable genes.
reference: ACMG Secondary Findings v3.2 (SF v3.2); ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Subject ID
description: Native subject identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of accessioning. Used for cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS workflow. Enables reconciliation and lineage tracking back to the system of record.
reference: LabVantage LIMS Subject Master; ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Free-text field for study coordinator or clinical staff annotations regarding the subject record, such as protocol deviations, special handling instructions, or enrollment exceptions. Must not contain direct PHI. Reviewed during data quality audits and IRB annual reviews.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; LabVantage LIMS Subject Notes; ISO 15189

description: Indicates whether a PHI linkage record exists for this subject in the access-controlled consent and identity management system. When True, the subject_code can be re-linked to direct PHI by authorized personnel only. When False, the record is fully de-identified with no re-linkage pathway. Supports HIPAA Safe Harbor compliance auditing.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.502(d); GDPR Article 4(5)

description: The primary genomic assay type intended or performed for this subject's specimens. Drives bioinformatics pipeline selection, QC (Quality Control) metric thresholds, and data product routing. WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), targeted panel, SNP (Single Nucleotide Polymorphism) array, RNA-seq, and methylation array are the principal assay categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|RNA_seq|methylation_array|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS Assay Configuration; FDA IVD Guidance; IVDR 2017/746

description: Indicates whether the subject has consented to being re-contacted for additional studies, incidental findings disclosure, or updated consent. Relevant for longitudinal genomics studies and return-of-results workflows. When False, no re-contact is permitted without a new consent process.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ACMG Incidental Findings Policy; GDPR Article 7; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for the subject record, incremented on each update. Supports optimistic concurrency control, SCD Type 2 history tracking, and GxP audit trail requirements for regulated genomics data. Version 1 represents the initial record creation.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.5; Databricks Delta Lake Versioning

description: The reference genome assembly version used as the default for bioinformatics pipeline alignment and variant calling for this subject's specimens (e.g., GRCh38 for human, GRCm39 for mouse). Ensures consistency between sample metadata and downstream BAM/VCF (Variant Call Format) outputs. Critical for multi-cohort studies where genome build consistency is required.
reference: NCBI Reference Sequence (RefSeq); Ensembl Genome Browser; ACMG Variant Classification; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Operational system of record from which this subject record was originated or last updated. Enables data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and upstream operational systems (primarily LabVantage LIMS for sample domain records).
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Benchling R&D Notebook; ISO 27001; GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Biological species of the subject. Determines applicable reference genome, annotation pipeline, and regulatory classification (human vs. animal model). Critical for bioinformatics pipeline routing and IRB/IACUC compliance.
reference: NCBI Taxonomy; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; ISO 15189

description: Externally-known, de-identified alphanumeric code assigned to the subject at accessioning or enrollment. Used as the operational identifier across LIMS, bioinformatics pipelines, and clinical reports without exposing direct PHI. Equivalent to the participant ID or pseudonym in study documentation.
reference: HIPAA Safe Harbor (45 CFR §164.514(b)); GDPR Article 4(5) Pseudonymisation; LabVantage LIMS Subject Code

description: The calendar date on which the subject was formally enrolled into the cohort. Required for IRB compliance and cohort timeline reporting.
Current lifecycle status of the subject's enrollment in the cohort. Tracks whether the subject is actively participating, has withdrawn, or is in a pending/suspended state.

Classification of the biological source individual by their role in the study or clinical workflow. Drives consent requirements, regulatory handling, and data access controls. Research participants and patients require IRB/ethics board oversight; animal models require IACUC approval.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; IACUC

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the subject record in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental pipeline processing, and GxP audit trail compliance.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records); HIPAA Security Rule; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; ISO 27001

description: Regulatory use classification for data and specimens derived from this subject. RUO (Research Use Only) specimens are not intended for clinical diagnosis; IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) specimens are used in validated clinical assays; LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) specimens are used in laboratory-developed clinical tests. Drives regulatory handling, labeling, and data access controls.
reference: FDA IVD Guidance 21 CFR Part 809; IVDR 2017/746; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 13485

description: Date on which the subject formally withdrew consent or was removed from the study. Triggers downstream data use restriction workflows per HIPAA and GDPR right-to-erasure requirements. Populated only when subject_status is 'withdrawn'.
reference: GDPR Article 17; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Reason for subject withdrawal from the study or clinical program, recorded when subject_status transitions to 'withdrawn'. Supports regulatory audit trails, protocol deviation reporting, and IRB annual review documentation. Populated only when applicable.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 45 CFR Part 46; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; Veeva Vault Regulatory Document

description: Four-digit birth year of the subject. Stored instead of full date of birth (DOB) to comply with HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification requirements (45 CFR §164.514(b)(2)(i)), which prohibit retention of specific birth dates. Enables age-range calculations and cohort age-stratification for population genomics without re-identification risk.
reference: HIPAA Safe Harbor 45 CFR §164.514(b)(2)(i); GDPR Article 89 Research Exemption

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing index set record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Primary key for the index_set master resource.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2 — Document and Record Control

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Index sets used in IVD assays require regulatory clearance; 'regulatory_clearance_number' is a direct denormalized reference to an approval record. Linking to regulatory.approval enables proper tracea

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Index kits are batch-managed. Link enables lot traceability for quality investigations (index hopping, demux failures), recall management, expiry tracking, and GMP documentation. Removes denormalized

Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Index/adapter sets are sold as catalog items with specific SKUs. Critical for ordering, compatibility validation with library prep kits, regulatory documentation, and ensuring correct product versions

Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Index set version updates and deprecations (deprecation_date, superseded_by_set_code on index_set) are governed by change control in GxP genomics labs. Direct FK enables impact assessment of which ind

Reference identifier for the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with this index set lot, stored in Veeva Vault or MasterControl. Provides traceability to quality release documentation including purity, concentration, and sequence verification results.
reference: Veeva Vault — Quality Document Management; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4; GxP Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Index sets are governed by technical specifications and SOPs that are controlled documents (sop_reference on index_set is a denormalized reference). In GxP genomics, the index set specification must b

Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Index adapter lot traceability is a regulatory requirement (GMP, ISO 13485) — each index set must be traceable to a specific reagent lot for recall management, expiry enforcement, and QC release statu

Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Index kits are materials. Link enables inventory management, demand planning based on multiplexing forecasts, MRP planning, and supply chain integration for procurement and stock level optimization.

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Index sets can be nonconforming (failed validation, incorrect sequences, manufacturing defect, contamination). Labs track NCRs for supplier quality management, lot disposition, and regulatory reportin

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: GMP traceability requirement: index sets (sequencing adapters/barcodes) are manufactured products. Genomics biotech must trace each index set lot to its manufacturing production batch for quality inve

Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Index sets are procured physical reagent products requiring PO-level traceability for cost allocation, receiving inspection, and GMP batch record completeness. index_set already links to supply.batch

Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Index sets are reagent products; linking to reagent.catalog enables storage condition enforcement, compatibility validation, and regulatory clearance lookups. The existing catalog_item_id targets prod

Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: Index sets used in IVD applications carry regulatory clearance requirements. The plain column regulatory_clearance_number is denormalized from product.regulatory_classification. Linking enables proper

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Index set validation requires comparing actual index properties against product specifications. Columns min_hamming_distance, index_length_bp, max_multiplex_samples are denormalized from product.speci

Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Index kit supplier tracking. Enables supplier quality management for index-related issues (index hopping, low demux rates), performance evaluation, and supplier qualification. Removes denormalized man

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Index sets are validated for use in multiplexed sequencing (cross-contamination testing, demultiplexing accuracy, color balance verification). Labs link index sets to validation studies for regulatory
Full adapter sequence flanking the i7 index on the Read 1 side, used for adapter trimming in bioinformatics preprocessing pipelines. Stored in 5'→3' orientation per IUPAC convention.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet Specification; IUPAC Nucleotide Nomenclature

description: Full adapter sequence flanking the i5 index on the Read 2 side, used for adapter trimming in paired-end bioinformatics preprocessing pipelines. Null for single-end or single-index configurations.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet Specification; IUPAC Nucleotide Nomenclature

description: Date on which the index set was formally approved for production use by the sequencing operations quality authority. Null if not yet approved. Triggers availability for pool design and run scheduling.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.5 — Production and Service Provision; MasterControl — Document Approval Workflow; Veeva Vault QMS

description: Current lifecycle and approval status of the index set within Genomics Biotech sequencing operations. 'pending_validation' = under internal validation; 'validated' = technically validated but not yet formally approved; 'approved' = approved for production use; 'deprecated' = superseded by a newer set, no new runs; 'retired' = fully decommissioned.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.5 — Production and Service Provision; Veeva Vault — Quality Document Control; MasterControl — Document Approval Workflow

description: Name or employee identifier of the quality authority or sequencing operations manager who granted formal production approval for this index set. Required for GxP and ISO 13485 audit trails.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 5.5 — Responsibility and Authority; Veeva Vault QMS; MasterControl — Approval Workflow

business_glossary_term: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub Index Set ID
description: The unique identifier assigned to this index set within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Enables direct cross-reference between the Genomics Biotech lakehouse master record and the operational sequencing data management platform for run configuration and demultiplexing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Index Set Registry API

description: Indicates whether the index set provides adequate color (fluorescence channel) balance across all sequencing cycles on two-channel or four-channel chemistry instruments. 'balanced' = no spike-in required; 'unbalanced' = color imbalance detected; 'requires_spike_in' = PhiX or other spike-in control is mandatory. Prevents base-calling failures on patterned flow cells.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — Color Balance for Indexed Sequencing; Illumina NovaSeq/NextSeq System Guide

description: Comma-separated list of sequencing instrument models validated for use with this index set (e.g., 'NovaSeq 6000, NextSeq 550, MiSeq'). Ensures that run configuration and read cycle parameters are compatible with the index chemistry.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Instrument Compatibility; Illumina Instrument System Guides

description: Comma-separated list of library preparation kit names or catalogue numbers validated for use with this index set (e.g., 'TruSeq DNA PCR-Free, TruSeq Stranded mRNA, Nextera DNA Flex'). Managed by sequencing operations to prevent incompatible kit-index pairings during run setup.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Library Prep Compatibility Matrix; LabVantage LIMS — Reagent Compatibility

description: Timestamp when this index set record was first created in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GxP record integrity requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4 — Control of Records; GxP Guidelines; ISO 27001 — Audit Logging

description: Date on which the index set was deprecated or retired from active use. Null if still active. Used to enforce that no new sequencing runs are configured with deprecated index sets and to support historical traceability.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2 — Document and Record Control; MasterControl — Change Control

description: Indicates whether the index set was manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) conditions as required for IVD or clinical use. True = GMP-manufactured; False = non-GMP (RUO grade). Drives quality management and supplier qualification requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 — Quality System Regulation; ISO 13485 Section 7.5; GxP Guidelines

description: Nucleotide sequence of the i5 (read 2 side) index used in dual-index configurations. Null or empty for single-index sets. Stored as a representative or consensus sequence; individual per-sample i5 sequences are tracked at the library level.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet Specification; IUPAC Nucleotide Nomenclature

description: Nucleotide sequence of the i7 (read 1 side) index used for sample identification during demultiplexing. Stored as a representative or consensus sequence for the set; individual per-sample i7 sequences are tracked at the library level. Validated against IUPAC nucleotide codes.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet Specification; IUPAC Nucleotide Nomenclature

description: Total number of unique index sequences (barcodes) available in the set. Determines the maximum multiplexing capacity — i.e., the maximum number of samples that can be pooled in a single sequencing run using this index set.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pool Design; Illumina Multiplexing Guidelines

description: Classification of the indexing strategy used in the set. 'single' = single-end index (i7 only); 'dual' = dual-index (i7 + i5, may be combinatorial); 'UDI' = Unique Dual Index (each sample has a unique i7+i5 pair, preventing index hopping); 'combinatorial' = combinatorial dual index (i7 and i5 drawn from independent pools). Critical for demultiplexing configuration and index-hopping risk assessment.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — Indexed Sequencing Overview; SEQC2 Consortium Guidelines

description: Regulatory intended use classification for the index set. 'RUO' = Research Use Only; 'IVD' = In Vitro Diagnostic; 'LDT' = Laboratory Developed Test; 'research' = internal research; 'clinical' = clinical genomics applications; 'agricultural' = agricultural genomics; 'both' = dual RUO and clinical use. Governs which workflows and regulatory submissions this index set may support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RUO|IVD|LDT|research|clinical|agricultural|both — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 — Quality System Regulation; IVDR EU 2017/746; ISO 13485 Section 7.3

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Index Set ID
description: The unique identifier for this index set in LabVantage LIMS, the system of record for sample tracking and laboratory workflow management. Enables traceability between the lakehouse master record and LIMS-managed library preparation and pool design workflows.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Reagent and Index Management Module

description: Free-text field for sequencing operations scientists to record additional context, known limitations, special handling instructions, or validation observations for this index set that are not captured in structured fields.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Notes Field; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4

description: Recommended percentage of PhiX control library to spike into the pool when using this index set, to correct for color imbalance or low-diversity libraries. Expressed as a percentage of total library pool volume (e.g., 1.00, 5.00, 20.00). Zero if no spike-in is required.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — PhiX Control Library; Illumina Sequencing System Guide

description: Specifies the orientation in which the i5 index is read on the sequencer. Critical for correct demultiplexing on instruments with different flow cell orientations (e.g., NovaSeq v1.5 flow cells require reverse complement of i5 vs. standard orientation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: forward|reverse_complement|forward_reverse|reverse_complement_forward — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Technical Note — Index Read Orientation; Illumina NovaSeq System Guide

description: The sequencing chemistry type for which this index set is designed. Determines color balance requirements and base-calling algorithm compatibility. 'two_channel' = Illumina two-channel SBS (e.g., NextSeq, NovaSeq); 'four_channel' = Illumina four-channel SBS (e.g., MiSeq, HiSeq); 'ion_semiconductor' = Ion Torrent; 'nanopore' = Oxford Nanopore; 'other' = other chemistry.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — Sequencing Chemistry Overview; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code or catalogue number assigned to the index set by the manufacturer or internally by sequencing operations. Used as the business key for cross-system lookups and BOM references in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master; ISO 13485 Section 7.4

description: Human-readable name of the sequencing index set as defined by the manufacturer or sequencing operations team (e.g., 'Illumina TruSeq CD Index Set A', 'IDT xGen UDI-UMI Adapters'). Used as the primary display label in library preparation workflows and pool design tools.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Index Set Registry

description: Manufacturer-specified shelf life of the index set reagents in months from the date of manufacture. Used for expiry tracking, inventory management, and ensuring reagents are not used beyond their validated stability window.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.5.5 — Preservation of Product; SAP S/4HANA MM — Batch Expiry Management; GxP Guidelines

description: Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the use, handling, and storage of this index set. Links to the controlled document in MasterControl or Veeva Vault. Ensures procedural compliance during library preparation.
reference: MasterControl — Document Control; Veeva Vault — SOP Management; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3; GxP Guidelines

description: Required storage temperature for the index set reagents in degrees Celsius (e.g., -20.0, 4.0). Critical for reagent integrity, shelf-life compliance, and cold-chain management in the laboratory.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.5.5 — Preservation of Product; SAP S/4HANA MM — Warehouse Management; GxP Guidelines

description: Set code of the newer index set that replaces this one when it is deprecated. Enables migration guidance for sequencing operations teams transitioning from legacy to current index chemistries. Null if not superseded.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2 — Document and Record Control; MasterControl — Change Control

description: Indicates whether the index set includes Unique Molecular Identifiers (UMIs) integrated into the adapter design. UMIs enable PCR duplicate removal and error correction in downstream bioinformatics pipelines, critical for low-input and cfDNA applications.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — UMI Sequencing; SEQC2 Consortium Guidelines

description: Length of the UMI sequence in base pairs when UMIs are included in the index set design (e.g., 8, 12 bp). Null if umi_included is False. Required for configuring UMI extraction steps in bioinformatics pipelines.
reference: Illumina Technical Note — UMI Sequencing; Benchling R&D Notebook — Adapter Design

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this index set record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Tracks the last modification event for change control, data freshness monitoring, and GxP audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4 — Control of Records; GxP Guidelines; ISO 27001 — Audit Logging

description: Name or employee identifier of the sequencing operations scientist or engineer who performed and signed off on the internal technical validation of this index set. Supports GxP audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 5.5 — Responsibility and Authority; GxP Guidelines; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Date on which the index set completed internal technical validation by Genomics Biotech sequencing operations, confirming that demultiplexing performance, Hamming distance, and color balance meet acceptance criteria. Null if validation is pending.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.3.6 — Design and Development Verification; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Accreditation Standards

description: Version number of the index set definition, following semantic versioning convention (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.1', 'v1.0.3'). Incremented when index sequences, Hamming distance parameters, or compatibility matrices are updated. Supports change control and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2 — Document and Record Control; MasterControl — Version Control

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing library record within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for the library master record. MASTER_RESOURCE role — serves as the SSOT for library identity within the sequencing domain.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Libraries prepared for customer samples in service/contract research. Essential for sample ownership tracking, billing, data access control, consent management, and regulatory compliance (PHI/GDPR).

Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Libraries prepared for clinical/diagnostic use must be traceable to the customer's CLIA/CAP accreditation for regulatory audit and IVD compliance. Clinical genomics labs require this traceability at t

Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: A sequencing library is prepared from a specific aliquot. GMP chain-of-custody, freeze-thaw tracking, and QC failure investigations require knowing exactly which aliquot was used. Genomics LIMS expert

Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Libraries used in IVD/clinical workflows must be traceable to the regulatory approval authorizing the assay. Required for 21 CFR Part 820 device history records and ISO 13485 traceability. Genomics la

Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Libraries prepared for assay development validation must link to the assay_development record. Assay development programs track library type, insert size, and prep kit requirements — all defined in as

Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: A library is prepared according to an assay version's library_preparation_protocol and quality thresholds. CLIA labs must document which assay version governed library preparation for regulatory audit

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Sample submitter/technical contact for library-specific communications: QC results, resequencing decisions, consent verification. Critical for customer-facing service labs and regulatory traceability.
Identifier of the physical plate or rack in which the library is stored. Used for laboratory inventory management, retrieval workflows, and storage location tracking within the LIMS.

Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Libraries are prepared from extracted nucleic acid. Extraction quality metrics (RIN, yield, purity) are direct inputs to library QC. Regulatory traceability (CAP/CLIA) and method validation require li

Foreign key linking to sequencing.index_set. Business justification: Libraries are assigned index adapters from controlled index sets for multiplexing. library currently has adapter_sequence_i7, adapter_sequence_i5, and udi_plate_id but no FK to index_set. Adding index

Foreign key linking to sequencing.library_prep_run. Business justification: Each library is produced by a specific library preparation run. This is a standard parent-child relationship where library_prep_run is the batch execution entity and library is the output product. Cur

Foreign key linking to research.molecular_design. Business justification: Targeted sequencing libraries (capture panels, amplicon libraries) are built from specific molecular designs defining target loci and probe sequences. Linking library to molecular_design enables trace

Identifier of the multiplexing pool to which this library has been assigned for co-loading onto a flow cell. Enables tracking of pooled libraries, demultiplexing yield analysis, and per-library coverage attribution within a pooled run.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Libraries failing QC (qc_status, qc_failure_reason on library) require nonconformance records for material disposition decisions in GxP genomics. Library already links to oos_investigation and qc_resu

Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Libraries with out-of-specification QC results (concentration out of range, wrong fragment size, high adapter dimer) trigger OOS investigations. Labs link investigations to specific libraries for root

Reference to the original library record when this library is a resequencing attempt (resequencing_flag = True). Enables lineage tracking across preparation attempts and supports failure analysis workflows. Null for original preparations.

business_glossary_term: Prep Kit Catalog Item Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Library prep kits are commercial catalog items. Labs track which kit SKU was used for cost allocation, protocol validation, regulatory compliance, and troubleshooting quality issues. Essential for GxP

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Library prep kit SKU traceability is required for lot-level COGS accounting and inventory depletion. In regulated genomics labs, the specific kit SKU (pack size, configuration) used per library must b
Reference to the research or clinical project under which this library was prepared. Supports project-level tracking, cost allocation, and reporting across the R&D and clinical genomics domains.

Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Libraries undergo QC testing (concentration via Qubit/qPCR, fragment size via Bioanalyzer/TapeStation, adapter dimer assessment). Each library has multiple QC results tracked for batch release, troubl
Reference to the biological sample from which this library was prepared. Links the library to its upstream sample management record, enabling traceability from raw specimen through sequencing output.

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Library QC pass/fail is evaluated against product specifications (insert size range, PCR cycle count, reaction volume). For OOS investigations and CLIA compliance, the specification version the librar

Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Libraries prepared to meet customer-specified technical requirements (coverage, insert size, prep method). Links library design to customer engagement specifications for acceptance criteria validation

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: A library is prepared to fulfill a specific clinical test order. CLIA audit trails require direct traceability from library preparation to the clinical test order, enabling consent verification, TAT t

External identifier assigned to this library within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Enables cross-system traceability between the internal LIMS record and the BaseSpace sequencing data management platform for FASTQ retrieval and run association.

Measured molar concentration of the library in nanomolar (nM) units. Used for accurate loading onto flow cells and pooling calculations during multiplexing. Sourced from quantification instrument output.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Indicates whether valid informed consent has been obtained for the use of the source sample in sequencing and data sharing (True = consent confirmed; False = consent not confirmed or withdrawn). Required for HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.
reference: HIPAA; GDPR Article 9; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; IRB requirements

description: Timestamp when the library record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements, TAT calculations, and data lineage tracking. Aligns with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.

Indicates whether genomic data derived from this library is subject to data sharing restrictions (True = restricted; False = unrestricted). Restrictions may arise from consent limitations, IRB conditions, contractual obligations, or GDPR/HIPAA data subject rights. Governs downstream data product access controls.
reference: HIPAA; GDPR Article 9; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; IRB requirements

description: Mass of input nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) used for library preparation, measured in nanograms (ng). A key QC parameter — insufficient input mass is a leading cause of library preparation failure and low complexity.

Target or measured average insert size of the library fragments in base pairs (bp). A key MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (MASTER_RESOURCE category) for library quality assessment. Impacts read alignment efficiency, coverage uniformity, and variant calling accuracy. Typically 150–500 bp for WGS/WES.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; CAP; CLIA

description: Regulatory classification of the library's intended use. Determines applicable quality, documentation, and reporting requirements. Values: RUO — Research Use Only; IVD — In Vitro Diagnostic; LDT — Laboratory Developed Test; clinical_trial — use in regulated clinical study; research — internal R&D use.
reference: FDA IVD guidance; IVDR 2017/746; CLIA; CAP; ISO 13485

description: Indicates whether the library is designed for paired-end (True) or single-read (False) sequencing. Paired-end sequencing generates two reads per fragment, improving alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Drives instrument run configuration.

Indicates whether the library was prepared using a PCR-free protocol (True) or a PCR-amplified protocol (False). PCR-free libraries have lower duplicate rates and reduced GC bias, preferred for high-accuracy WGS applications.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Benchling; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the sequencing library, used in LIMS, BaseSpace, and laboratory documentation. Follows the internal naming convention LIB-{alphanumeric}. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE category) used across operational systems.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the sequencing library, typically incorporating sample identifier, preparation date, and assay type. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL (MASTER_RESOURCE category) for display in LIMS dashboards, BaseSpace projects, and laboratory reports.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Classification of the sequencing library by assay strategy. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection, coverage expectations, and reporting templates. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE (MASTER_RESOURCE category). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|amplicon|RNA-seq|targeted_panel|methylation|ChIP-seq|ATAC-seq|single_cell — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; CLIA; CAP

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Library ID
description: Native identifier for this library record within LabVantage LIMS. Supports bidirectional reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record for audit and data lineage purposes.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP

description: Type of nucleic acid used as input for library preparation. Drives protocol selection, QC thresholds, and bioinformatics pipeline configuration. Values: gDNA — genomic DNA; cfDNA — cell-free DNA (liquid biopsy); RNA — total or mRNA; cDNA — complementary DNA; FFPE_DNA — formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded DNA; amplicon_DNA — pre-amplified target DNA.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; CAP; CLIA; FDA IVD guidance

description: Number of PCR amplification cycles applied during library preparation. Impacts PCR duplicate rate, GC bias, and library complexity. PCR-free libraries have a value of 0. Tracked for QC benchmarking and protocol optimization.

Percentage contribution of this library to the total multiplexing pool by molar concentration. Used to balance per-library coverage in multiplexed sequencing runs and to investigate coverage imbalance post-sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Calendar date on which the library preparation protocol was completed. Used for lot traceability, reagent expiry correlation, and turnaround time (TAT) calculations. Aligns with GxP batch record requirements.

Free-text or coded description of the reason for QC failure when qc_status is 'fail' or 'conditional_pass'. Supports root-cause analysis, protocol improvement, and regulatory deviation reporting. Null when qc_status is 'pass' or 'pending'.

Current quality control disposition of the library. Determines whether the library is eligible for sequencing run loading. LIFECYCLE_STATUS (MASTER_RESOURCE category). Values: pass — meets all QC thresholds; fail — does not meet thresholds; pending — QC not yet completed; conditional_pass — meets minimum thresholds with caveats; requeue — failed and scheduled for re-preparation.

Analytical method used to measure library concentration and/or size distribution. Affects confidence in concentration accuracy and downstream loading calculations. Common methods: qPCR (most accurate for sequenceable molecules), Bioanalyzer/TapeStation (size + concentration), Qubit (fluorometric mass concentration).

Indicates whether this library was prepared as a resequencing attempt for a previously failed or insufficient-coverage library (True = resequencing; False = original preparation). Supports TAT tracking, cost analysis, and failure rate reporting.

Required or actual storage temperature for the library in degrees Celsius (e.g., -20°C for long-term, 4°C for short-term). Supports cold chain compliance, stability monitoring, and deviation investigation.

Well position (e.g., A01, H12) within the storage plate where the library aliquot is located. Combined with storage_plate_id, provides precise physical location for laboratory retrieval and inventory audits.

Indicates whether the library preserves strand-of-origin information for RNA-seq or other strand-aware assays. Values: unstranded — no strand information retained; forward — read 1 maps to sense strand; reverse — read 1 maps to antisense strand. Required for correct transcript quantification in RNA-seq pipelines.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Bioinformatics pipeline standards

description: Intended sequencing coverage depth (read depth) for this library, expressed as fold-coverage (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for WES, 500x for targeted panels). Drives sequencing run planning, flow cell loading calculations, and QC pass/fail thresholds.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; CAP; CLIA; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Intended sequencing read length in base pairs (e.g., 150 bp for paired-end short-read WGS). Determines sequencing run configuration, instrument chemistry selection, and bioinformatics pipeline parameters.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Identifier of the unique dual index (UDI) plate from which the index pair was assigned. Enables traceability of index assignments across batches and supports investigation of index hopping or cross-contamination events.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Well position (e.g., A01, B12) on the UDI plate from which the index pair was drawn. Together with udi_plate_id, uniquely identifies the index assignment for demultiplexing and audit purposes.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the library record. Supports change tracking, audit trail completeness, and Silver Layer incremental load processing. Aligns with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.

Current volume of library material remaining in storage, measured in microliters (µL). Tracks consumable inventory for re-sequencing eligibility and storage management. Updated after each aliquot event.

Unique surrogate identifier for the library preparation run record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete batch execution event with a full lifecycle from initiation through QC disposition.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Library prep services performed for customer accounts. Essential for service delivery tracking, billing, batch-level customer segregation, and GxP compliance in regulated service environments.

Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Library prep runs executed under GxP or CLIA-accredited conditions must link to the customer's accreditation record for regulatory traceability, audit trail completeness, and quality system compliance

Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: GMP chain-of-custody and QC failure root cause analysis require tracing a library prep run to the specific aliquot consumed. Genomics LIMS experts universally expect this link for lot genealogy, mater

Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Library prep runs executed during assay development must be traceable to the assay_development record for validation reporting. Assay development tracks precision CV, LOD, and reproducibility — all as

Identifier of the liquid handling instrument or automated workstation (e.g., Hamilton, Beckman Coulter) used to execute the library preparation run. Supports instrument qualification tracking, preventive maintenance scheduling, and GxP equipment traceability.

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Library prep kits are batch-managed. Link enables GMP traceability, QC correlation (prep failures to kit lots), recall management, and expiry tracking. Critical for regulated workflows. Removes denorm

Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: Library prep performed on customer-installed automation instruments. Tracks utilization, service delivery, and instrument-specific performance for customer-site operations.

Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Library prep kit specification lookups (storage conditions, hazard classification, GMP compliance) require linking library_prep_run to reagent.catalog. The existing kit_catalog_item_id targets product

Identifier of the reagent kit lot used in this library preparation run. Enables reagent lot traceability, recall management, and correlation of kit lot performance with QC outcomes.

Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: Library preparation site attribution is required for multi-site CLIA/CAP compliance, inter-site method comparison studies, and regulatory submissions that must identify the performing laboratory for e

Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Library prep kits are materials. Link enables inventory tracking, demand planning based on prep run forecasts, MRP triggers, and integration with supply chain for automated reordering and stock manage

Foreign key linking to research.molecular_design. Business justification: Library prep runs for targeted sequencing panels are executed against specific molecular designs (amplicon panels, capture probe sets). Direct linkage enables R&D teams to assess which molecular desig

Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: Library prep runs are physically performed in a CLIA-certified performing laboratory. CAP/CLIA compliance requires each prep run to be traceable to the performing lab for inspection readiness, capacit

Identifier of the research or clinical project to which this library preparation run is attributed. Enables project-level cost allocation, throughput reporting, and cross-run traceability within Benchling or the LIMS project hierarchy.
reference: Benchling; LabVantage LIMS; SAP S/4HANA CO

Identifier of the standard operating procedure (SOP) or library preparation protocol document used for this run. Links to the document control system (MasterControl/Veeva Vault) for version-controlled protocol traceability.

Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Library prep services governed by service agreements defining turnaround time, QC standards, pricing. Critical for SLA compliance verification and contract-specific protocol adherence.

Foreign key linking to product.service_offering. Business justification: Library prep performed as a contracted service must reference the service offering for billing, SLA compliance, and deliverables reporting. Genomics service labs link each prep run to the service offe

Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Automated library prep instruments run specific software versions. In GxP-regulated environments, the software version controlling the prep run must be traceable for batch records and regulatory submi

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Library prep runs are executed against product specifications defining acceptable fragment size, PCR cycles, and yield. For GxP batch release and OOS investigations, the specification version governin

Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Library prep kit supplier tracking. Enables supplier performance evaluation based on prep run outcomes (QC failures, adapter dimer rates), quality investigations, and supplier scorecarding for procure

Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Library prep runs are configured to fulfill specific customer technical requirements (insert size, library type, PCR cycles, coverage targets). This link enables traceability from prep execution back

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: Library prep runs are executed as part of a specific clinical test workflow. The test_catalog entry defines library prep requirements (insert size, PCR cycles, fragmentation method). Linking enables e

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Library prep runs are used in validation studies (kit validation, protocol verification, robustness testing). Labs track which prep runs contributed to validation data for regulatory documentation and

Percentage of adapter dimer contamination detected in the final library by electrophoresis (Bioanalyzer/TapeStation). High adapter dimer content reduces effective sequencing yield and is a key QC failure indicator. Typically must be below 5% for pass disposition.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485 Section 8.2.4

description: Method used to ligate sequencing adapters to the end-repaired DNA fragments. Tagmentation (Nextera) combines fragmentation and adapter ligation in a single step. Affects ligation efficiency, adapter dimer formation rate, and overall library yield.

Date and time when the library preparation run record received formal QA/supervisor approval. Distinct from run completion timestamp. Required for GxP electronic record compliance and regulatory audit trail.
reference: MasterControl; Veeva Vault; GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.5

business_glossary_term: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub Run ID
description: Run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for the downstream sequencing run associated with this library preparation batch. Enables end-to-end traceability from library preparation through raw data generation and FASTQ output.

Overall quality control disposition for the library preparation run batch. Determines whether the batch is approved for sequencing submission. A conditional pass may require additional review or re-quantification before proceeding.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 13485 Section 8.2.4; GxP; CLIA

description: Timestamp when this library preparation run record was first created in the source LIMS system. Supports GxP audit trail and data lineage requirements. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Free-text description of any deviation from the approved SOP observed during this library preparation run. Populated only when sop_deviation_flag is True. Supports GxP deviation management, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) initiation, and regulatory audit readiness.
reference: MasterControl; Veeva Vault; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 8.5.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Indicates whether an end-repair and A-tailing step was performed on the fragmented DNA prior to adapter ligation. End repair is required for blunt-end ligation protocols but may be skipped in tagmentation-based workflows.

Date and time when the library preparation run was completed or terminated. Used in conjunction with start_timestamp to compute total run duration and TAT metrics. Nullable if run is still in progress.

Measured concentration of the final pooled library in nanomolar (nM), typically quantified by qPCR or fluorometric assay (e.g., Qubit). Used to calculate the dilution factor for optimal flow cell loading and to assess library yield.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Total volume in microliters (µL) of the final pooled library submitted for sequencing. Used to calculate loading concentration for the flow cell and to assess library yield sufficiency for sequencing.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: DNA/RNA fragmentation approach applied during library preparation. Enzymatic fragmentation uses restriction enzymes or transposases (e.g., Nextera); mechanical fragmentation uses sonication or acoustic shearing (e.g., Covaris). Affects insert size distribution and library quality metrics.

Indicates whether the selected multiplexing index combinations were verified to have sufficient Hamming distance to prevent index hopping or misassignment during demultiplexing. A failed check requires index reassignment before sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Total mass of input DNA or RNA (in nanograms) used per sample in this library preparation run. Critical QC parameter — insufficient input material is a leading cause of library preparation failure and low-complexity libraries.

Type of nucleic acid input material used for library preparation (e.g., genomic DNA (gDNA), cell-free DNA (cfDNA), RNA, formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) DNA). Determines applicable protocol, QC acceptance criteria, and downstream analytical interpretation.

Classification of the sequencing library type prepared in this run. Determines downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection, QC thresholds, and sequencing parameters. Examples: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted|RNA-seq|amplicon|ChIP-seq|ATAC-seq|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Benchling; LabVantage LIMS

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Run ID
description: Native run identifier assigned by the source LabVantage LIMS system. Preserved as a cross-reference key to enable bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational LIMS without relying solely on the surrogate primary key.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Measured mean fragment size in base pairs (bp) of the final library as determined by Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, or equivalent electrophoresis instrument. Compared against target_insert_size_bp to assess size selection success and protocol adherence.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Name or identifier of the index/barcode set used for sample multiplexing in this library preparation run (e.g., Illumina TruSeq CD Index Set A, IDT for Illumina 10 bp UDI). Enables demultiplexing of pooled samples post-sequencing and prevents index collision errors.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Number of PCR amplification cycles applied during library preparation. Excessive PCR cycles introduce duplicate reads and GC bias, reducing library complexity. Zero cycles indicates a PCR-free protocol. Key parameter for library quality assessment and protocol compliance.

Version identifier of the library preparation SOP or protocol document applied during this run (e.g., v2.1.0). Critical for GxP compliance to confirm the correct, approved protocol version was followed and to detect protocol drift across runs.
reference: MasterControl; Veeva Vault; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3

description: Analytical method used to quantify and assess library quality (e.g., qPCR for accurate molar quantification, Qubit fluorometry for mass concentration, Bioanalyzer/TapeStation for fragment size distribution). Affects the reliability of concentration measurements and QC pass/fail decisions.

Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the library preparation run, typically generated by the LIMS (e.g., LP-NGSA-240315). Used for cross-system traceability, SOP deviation tracking, and GxP audit references. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.

Free-text field for operator observations, troubleshooting notes, or contextual comments recorded during or after the library preparation run. Supplements structured deviation documentation and supports root cause analysis.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; Benchling; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4

description: Current lifecycle state of the library preparation run. Drives workflow routing, QC gating, and downstream sequencing scheduling. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]

Total number of biological samples processed in this library preparation run batch. Used for capacity planning, throughput reporting, and cost-per-sample calculations. Represents the principal quantitative result of this transaction.

Method used to select the target insert size range from the library pool (e.g., SPRI bead-based cleanup, gel excision, Pippin Prep automated electrophoresis). Size selection removes adapter dimers and off-target fragments, improving sequencing efficiency.

Indicates whether any deviation from the approved standard operating procedure (SOP) was recorded during this library preparation run. A True value triggers mandatory deviation documentation and review per GxP requirements.
reference: MasterControl; Veeva Vault; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 8.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Date and time when the library preparation run was physically initiated in the laboratory. Represents the principal real-world business event time. Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculation and GxP audit trails. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; GxP; CLIA; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Intended DNA insert size in base pairs (bp) for the prepared library, as specified by the protocol. Insert size directly impacts sequencing read overlap, coverage uniformity, and variant calling accuracy. Typical ranges: 150–400 bp for short-read NGS.
reference: Benchling; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this library preparation run record. Enables change detection, incremental data loading, and GxP audit trail compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; GxP; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Regulatory use classification of the library preparation run. Research Use Only (RUO) runs are not subject to IVD regulatory controls; In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) and Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) runs require CLIA/CAP compliance and stricter QC documentation. Drives applicable regulatory requirements.
reference: FDA IVD Regulations; CLIA; CAP; IVDR 2017/746; ISO 13485

description: Identifier of the laboratory workgroup or team responsible for executing this library preparation run. Supports capacity planning, workload balancing, and team-level performance reporting.
reference: Oracle Cloud HCM; LabVantage LIMS; ISO 13485 Section 6.2

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the multiplexed library pool record. Primary key for the pool entity in the sequencing domain.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Pooling services for customer samples in multiplexed sequencing. Tracks service delivery, billing, and customer-specific pooling strategies. Essential for multi-customer pool segregation and data acce
Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, NextSeq) onto which this pool is intended to be loaded.

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Pool preparation uses specific reagent batches (dilution buffers, loading reagents) that must be traceable for QC failure root-cause analysis and supplier recall management. pool.lot_number is a denor
Reference to the specific flow cell onto which this pool will be or has been loaded for sequencing.
