Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Pooling operations use specific reagent lots (loading buffers, normalization reagents); lot traceability is required for QC investigations and recall management. The plain lot_number attribute is a de

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Pools failing QC (qc_overall_passed, qc_failure_reason on pool) require nonconformance records for material disposition in GxP genomics. Pool links to oos_investigation and qc_result but not nonconfor

Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Pools with OOS QC results (concentration deviation, fragment size out of range, index imbalance) trigger OOS investigations. Labs track which pool triggered each investigation for root cause analysis
Reference to the original pool from which this re-pool was derived, when is_repool is True. Enables lineage tracking and root cause analysis for failed pools.
Reference to the research or clinical project under which this pool was created. Links the pool to project-level objectives, funding, and reporting requirements.
reference: Benchling R&D Notebook — Project Entity; LabVantage LIMS

description: Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Pools undergo QC testing (concentration, fragment size, index balance, PhiX spike-in verification) before sequencing. QC results determine pool disposition, loading concentration adjustments, and repr

Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Pooling reagent specification lookups (storage temperature, hazard classification, GMP compliance) require linking pool to reagent.catalog. The existing reagent_catalog_item_id targets product.catalog

Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Pooling operations use reagents/normalization kits that are catalog items. Needed for cost accounting, protocol compliance, and tracking which commercial products were used for library normalization a

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Pool QC (loading concentration, fragment size, library count) is evaluated against product specifications. For sequencing run planning and QC investigations, the specification version governing pool a

Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Pool configuration (loading concentration, multiplex count, target read depth, read length) is directly driven by customer technical requirements. This link enables traceability from pooling decisions
External identifier assigned to this pool within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Used for cross-system traceability between the internal LIMS and the BaseSpace sequencing data management platform.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pool Object

description: Analytical method used to measure the pool concentration. Qubit and qPCR are preferred for accurate molar quantification; Bioanalyzer and TapeStation provide both concentration and fragment size data.
Timestamp when the pool record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory traceability.
reference: ISO 15189; GxP Audit Trail Requirements; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date after which the pool is considered expired and should not be loaded onto the sequencer. Determined by the stability window of the pooled libraries under specified storage conditions.
reference: Illumina Library Storage and Stability Guidelines; LabVantage LIMS; GxP

description: Measured molar concentration of the pool in nanomolar (nM) after pooling, as determined by Qubit fluorometry or qPCR quantification. Used to calculate dilution for loading concentration.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Library QC Guidelines; LabVantage LIMS

description: Specific lane(s) on the flow cell to which this pool is assigned for loading. A pool may occupy a single lane or the entire flow cell depending on the instrument type and run design.
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Loading Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Lower bound of the acceptable fragment size distribution range in base pairs (bp) for this pool, as defined by the QC specification. Fragments below this threshold indicate adapter contamination or over-fragmentation.
reference: Illumina Library QC Specifications; LabVantage LIMS QC Thresholds

description: Instrument or method used to assess the fragment size distribution of the pool (e.g., Agilent Bioanalyzer, Agilent TapeStation, Advanced Analytical Fragment Analyzer, or gel electrophoresis).
reference: Agilent Technologies QC Standards; Illumina Library QC Guidelines

description: Upper bound of the acceptable fragment size distribution range in base pairs (bp) for this pool. Fragments above this threshold may indicate incomplete fragmentation or genomic DNA contamination.
reference: Illumina Library QC Specifications; LabVantage LIMS QC Thresholds

description: Assessed risk level of index sequence collision (cross-contamination between demultiplexed samples) within this pool, based on Hamming distance analysis of the index set. Low risk indicates sufficient edit distance between all index pairs.
reference: Illumina Index Diversity Guidelines; Demultiplexing QC Standards

description: Indicates whether this pool is a re-pool created to replace or supplement a previously failed or underperforming pool (True = re-pool, False = original pool). Used for tracking remediation events.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pool Remediation Tracking; ISO 15189

description: Total number of individually indexed libraries combined into this pool. Determines the degree of multiplexing and directly impacts per-library read depth allocation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pool Composition; NGS Multiplexing Standards

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Pool ID
description: Native pool identifier from LabVantage LIMS, used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational LIMS source system.
Target loading concentration of the diluted pool in picomolar (pM) for the specific instrument and flow cell type. Directly impacts cluster density and sequencing quality.
reference: Illumina Instrument-Specific Loading Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Mean insert fragment size of the pool in base pairs (bp) as measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation electrophoresis. Critical for assessing library quality and estimating molar concentration.
reference: Agilent Bioanalyzer/TapeStation QC Standards; Illumina Library QC Guidelines

description: Free-text field for laboratory scientists to record observations, deviations, or special handling instructions associated with this pool that are not captured in structured fields.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Notes Field; ISO 15189; GxP

description: Percentage of PhiX Control library spiked into the pool prior to sequencing. PhiX is used as an internal sequencing quality control to monitor cluster density, error rate, and base calling performance.
reference: Illumina PhiX Control Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Human-readable, externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the pool for tracking across LIMS, BaseSpace, and laboratory worksheets. Used as the business identifier on COAs and run manifests.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pool Barcode; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Descriptive human-readable name assigned to the pool, typically encoding project, date, and cohort information for easy identification in run planning and reporting.

Current lifecycle status of the pool. Tracks progression from initial creation through QC verification, loading onto the sequencer, sequencing completion, or expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|qc_in_progress|qc_passed|qc_failed|loaded|sequenced|expired — promote to reference product]
Calendar date on which the individual libraries were combined to form this pool. Used for traceability, stability window calculation, and laboratory scheduling.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pool Creation Date; ISO 15189; GxP

description: Method used to combine individual indexed libraries into the pool. Equimolar pools combine equal molar quantities; custom ratio pools use user-defined proportions; normalized pools use concentration normalization prior to combining; volume-based pools combine equal volumes.
reference: Illumina Library Pooling Guidelines; Benchling R&D Protocol

description: Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or laboratory protocol used to perform the pooling procedure. Ensures reproducibility and regulatory traceability.
reference: MasterControl — Document Control; ISO 13485; GxP SOP Management

description: Indicates whether the pool passed the concentration QC threshold check (True = passed, False = failed). A pool must pass concentration QC before it is eligible for loading onto the sequencer.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Module; ISO 15189; GxP QC Standards

description: Date on which QC measurements (concentration and fragment size) were performed on the pool. May differ from pooling date if QC is conducted on a subsequent day.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Module; ISO 15189; GxP

description: Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) for QC failure when qc_overall_passed is False. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA initiation, and laboratory troubleshooting.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Module; ISO 13485 CAPA; GxP

business_glossary_term: Quality Control (QC) Fragment Size Pass Flag
description: Indicates whether the pool passed the fragment size distribution QC check (True = passed, False = failed). Ensures the pool's insert size profile is within acceptable bounds for the target sequencing application.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Module; ISO 15189; GxP QC Standards

description: Composite QC pass/fail flag indicating whether the pool has passed all required QC checks (concentration and fragment size) and is approved for sequencer loading. True = all QC checks passed.
reference: LabVantage LIMS QC Module; ISO 15189; GxP QC Standards

description: Intended use classification of the sequencing run for which this pool is prepared. RUO (Research Use Only) pools are not subject to clinical regulatory requirements; IVD and LDT pools require CLIA/CAP compliance.
reference: FDA IVD Regulations; CLIA; CAP; ISO 13485

description: Genomic sequencing application type for which this pool was prepared. Determines bioinformatics pipeline routing, QC thresholds, and coverage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wgs|wes|targeted_panel|amplicon|rna_seq|chip_seq|atac_seq|methyl_seq — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Application Notes; NGS Workflow Standards

description: Physical storage location identifier (freezer, rack, box, position) where the pool is stored in the laboratory. Used for sample retrieval and inventory management within LIMS.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sample Storage Module; ISO 15189

description: Required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for maintaining pool stability and integrity. Typically -20°C for long-term storage or 4°C for short-term use.
reference: Illumina Library Storage Guidelines; LabVantage LIMS; GxP

description: Expected average sequencing read depth (coverage depth) per library within the pool, expressed as number of reads. Used for run planning, yield allocation, and per-sample coverage estimation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Planning; NGS Coverage Depth Standards

description: Intended sequencing read length in base pairs (bp) for this pool (e.g., 150 bp paired-end). Determines the sequencing run configuration and impacts data output volume.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Run Configuration Standards; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Total volume of the combined pool in microlitres (µL) after all libraries have been pooled. Used to assess whether sufficient material exists for loading and archival.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pool Volume Field; Illumina Library Preparation Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the pool record. Used for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and data synchronization between source systems and the lakehouse.
reference: ISO 15189; GxP Audit Trail Requirements; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pool-library association record. Primary key for this junction entity linking a sequencing library to a pool.

Foreign key linking to sequencing.index_set. Business justification: pool_library tracks individual library assignments within pools with their specific index sequences. Currently has index_kit_name (string) and index sequences but no FK to index_set. Adding index_set_
Reference to the sequencing library that has been assigned to this pool. Enables reconstruction of which library contributed reads in a pooled run.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Library Preparation Entity; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Reference to the sequencing pool to which this library has been assigned. Supports demultiplexing traceability back to the originating pool.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pool Entity

description: The measured proportion (%) of this library's molar contribution in the final pool, determined by qPCR or fluorometric quantification. Deviations from expected fraction indicate pipetting or quantification errors.
reference: Illumina Library Quantification Technical Guide; qPCR Quantification SOP

description: Total number of sequencing reads successfully demultiplexed and assigned to this library from the pool run. Populated post-sequencing from demultiplexing output (e.g., bcl2fastq/BCL Convert reports). Used to assess yield per library.
reference: Illumina bcl2fastq/BCL Convert Demultiplexing Report; FASTQ Output Standard

description: Percentage of total pool reads assigned to this library after demultiplexing. Compared against expected molar fraction to assess pooling balance and identify over- or under-represented libraries.
reference: Illumina Demultiplexing QC Metrics; NGS Run QC SOP

description: Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this library was assigned to the pool in the LIMS or BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Serves as the primary business event timestamp for this junction record.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Audit Trail; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Current lifecycle status of the library-to-pool assignment. Tracks whether the library is pending confirmation, actively loaded, failed QC, or removed from the pool.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sample Workflow Status; ISO 13485 Section 8.3

description: Identifier of the FASTQ dataset generated for this library in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub following demultiplexing. Links the pool-library assignment to the resulting sequencing data artifact for downstream bioinformatics pipeline ingestion.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub API — Dataset Resource; FASTQ Output Standard

description: Cluster density (in thousands per mm²) on the flow cell lane attributable to this library's loading contribution. Excessively high or low cluster density impacts sequencing quality and read assignment accuracy.
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Metrics; Sequencing Run QC SOP

description: Number of base mismatches permitted during demultiplexing for this library's index sequence. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase sensitivity but risk misassignment between similar indexes.
reference: Illumina bcl2fastq/BCL Convert User Guide; FASTQ Demultiplexing SOP

description: The theoretical proportion (%) of this library's molar contribution relative to the total pool. Used to balance sequencing depth across libraries in a multiplexed run. Sum across all libraries in a pool should equal 100%.
reference: Illumina Library Normalization and Pooling Guide; NGS Multiplexing Best Practices

description: The i5 (read 2 side) barcode index nucleotide sequence assigned to this library for dual-index multiplexing. Required for dual-indexed library pools to prevent index hopping. Typically 6–10 bases.
reference: Illumina Adapter Sequences Document (1000000002694); FASTQ Demultiplexing Standard

description: The i7 (read 1 side) barcode index nucleotide sequence assigned to this library for multiplexing identification. Used during demultiplexing to assign reads back to the originating library. Typically 6–10 bases.
reference: Illumina Adapter Sequences Document (1000000002694); FASTQ Demultiplexing Standard

description: Indicates whether this library's index sequence conflicts (collides) with another library's index in the same pool, which would impair demultiplexing accuracy. True = collision detected; False = no collision. Populated by index compatibility checking tools.
reference: Illumina Index Collision Detection; FASTQ Demultiplexing Best Practices

description: Minimum Hamming distance between this library's index sequence and all other index sequences in the same pool. A distance of ≥2 is typically required for reliable demultiplexing with 1 mismatch tolerance. Low values indicate collision risk.
reference: Illumina Index Adapter Sequences; Hamming Distance Demultiplexing Standard

description: Human-readable name or catalog designation of the index pair (e.g., 'IDT-10nt-UDI-0001', 'TruSeq-D701-D501'). Corresponds to the index kit nomenclature used during library preparation.
reference: Illumina Index Adapter Kits; IDT xGen Dual Index UMI Adapters

description: Mean insert size in base pairs (bp) of this library as measured prior to pooling. Insert size affects read overlap, coverage uniformity, and compatibility with the sequencing run parameters.
reference: Illumina Library QC Metrics; Agilent Bioanalyzer/TapeStation Electropherogram Standard

description: The specific flow cell lane(s) to which this library-pool combination is assigned for sequencing (e.g., '1', '1,2', 'all'). Relevant for patterned flow cells with lane-level loading (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq S4 flow cell).
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Lane Assignment Guide; BaseSpace Sequence Hub Run Setup

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Pool Library Reference
description: External reference identifier for this pool-library association record as recorded in LabVantage LIMS. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational LIMS source of record.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pool Library Entity; ISO 13485 Section 4.2

description: The ratio of this library's contribution relative to the total pool volume or molar amount at the time of flow cell loading. Used to normalize read depth expectations and assess pooling balance post-sequencing.
reference: Illumina Sequencing System Denature and Dilute Libraries Guide; NGS Pooling SOP

description: Average Phred quality score across all bases in reads assigned to this library. Provides a summary measure of base call accuracy for this library's contribution to the pool run.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Quality Metrics; Phred Score Standard (Ewing & Green 1998)

description: Name of the instrument or software tool used to quantify and normalize this library prior to pooling (e.g., 'Qubit 4 Fluorometer', 'Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100', 'Agilent TapeStation 4200', 'KAPA Library Quantification qPCR'). Supports reagent and instrument traceability.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Instrument Tracking; ISO 15189 Section 5.3

description: Free-text field for laboratory operator observations, deviations, or contextual remarks specific to this library's pooling event. Supports GxP batch record documentation and investigation workflows.
reference: GxP Batch Record Requirements; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; LabVantage LIMS — Notes Field

description: Percentage of bases in reads assigned to this library that meet or exceed Phred quality score Q30 (≥99.9% base call accuracy). Key NGS quality metric; typically ≥80% Q30 is required for clinical-grade sequencing.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Quality Metrics; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP NGS Checklist

description: Calendar date on which this library was physically added to the pool. Used for traceability, lot expiry checks, and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pooling Workflow; ISO 15189 Section 5.4; GxP Batch Record Requirements

description: Method used to determine the volume of this library added to the pool. Options include equimolar normalization, volume-based, mass-based, qPCR-normalized, or fluorometric quantification-based pooling.
reference: Illumina Library Normalization and Pooling Guide; NGS Pooling SOP

description: Regulatory classification of this library's intended use context within the pool. RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), or GxP-governed (GMP/GCP/GLP). Determines applicable compliance requirements for data handling and reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; IVDR EU 2017/746; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 13485

description: Reason code explaining why this library was removed from the pool. Populated only when assignment_status is 'removed' or 'failed'. Supports root cause analysis and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) workflows.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 8.5 — CAPA; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; GxP Deviation Management SOP

description: Date and time when this library was removed or disqualified from the pool, if applicable. Null if the library remains active in the pool. Supports lifecycle tracking and investigation of failed runs.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Audit Trail; ISO 13485 Section 8.3

description: Row position of this library entry in the Illumina sample sheet (SampleSheet.csv) used to configure the sequencing run and demultiplexing. Ensures correct index-to-sample mapping during BCL conversion.
reference: Illumina SampleSheet.csv Format Specification; bcl2fastq/BCL Convert User Guide

description: The NGS application type for which this library was prepared and pooled. Determines expected coverage depth, read length, and bioinformatics pipeline routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|amplicon|RNA_seq|ChIP_seq|ATAC_seq|methylation_seq|single_cell — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Application Notes; NGS Assay Classification SOP

description: Indicates whether this library entry represents a spike-in control (e.g., PhiX, synthetic spike-in) rather than a biological sample library. True = spike-in control; False = biological sample. Spike-ins are used for sequencing quality calibration and are excluded from biological analysis.
reference: Illumina PhiX Control v3 Library Guide; NGS Run QC SOP

description: The intended sequencing coverage depth (×) for this library in the pool (e.g., 30× for WGS, 100× for WES, 500× for targeted panels). Used to calculate required read count and validate pooling balance.
reference: ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines; CAP NGS Checklist; Clinical Sequencing SOP

description: Indicates whether this library contributed a disproportionate number of reads to the undetermined (unassigned) category during demultiplexing, suggesting index sequence issues or contamination. True = flagged; False = within acceptable threshold.
reference: Illumina bcl2fastq QC Metrics; NGS Demultiplexing Failure Investigation SOP

description: Actual volume in microliters (µL) of this library pipetted into the pool. Critical for molar balance calculations and audit of physical pooling operations.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Pooling Workflow; ISO 15189 Section 5.4

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing protocol record. Primary key for the sequencing_protocol data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: IVD/CLIA-validated sequencing protocols must be directly traceable to the regulatory approval authorizing their clinical use. This is distinct from the SOP document link — the approval captures author

Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Sequencing protocols must reference approved reagent kit catalog entries for GMP validation and regulatory submission. The existing approved_kit_catalog_item_id targets product.catalog_item; reagent.c

business_glossary_term: Approved Kit Catalog Item Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Validated sequencing protocols specify approved/compatible kit catalog items. Essential for SOP compliance, regulatory submissions (FDA/IVDR), quality system requirements, and ensuring only validated

Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Sequencing protocols specify the approved demultiplexing/basecalling software version for regulatory compliance (CLIA, CAP). The plain column demultiplexing_tool is a denormalized reference to product

Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Sequencing protocols are developed and validated as deliverables of assay development programs. Linking sequencing_protocol to assay_development enables traceability of which protocol version was prod

Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: A sequencing protocol operationalizes an assay version's sequencing parameters (read length, index cycles, minimum Q30). Linking sequencing_protocol to assay_version enables traceability from the cont

Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Changes to sequencing protocols (parameter updates, new applications, cycle count changes) require change control in GxP environments. Labs link protocol changes to change control records for validati

Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Sequencing protocols ARE controlled documents in GxP environments. Every protocol must link to its document record for version control, training tracking, change control, and regulatory compliance. Fu

Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Sequencing protocols specify approved materials (reagent kits, consumables) for GMP compliance. Link enables procurement to validate material purchases against approved protocols and supports MRP plan

Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline. Business justification: Sequencing protocols are validated and operationally paired with specific analysis pipelines (e.g., WGS protocol → DRAGEN WGS pipeline). Protocol-pipeline compatibility is a documented validation requ

Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: Sequencing protocols are classified by regulatory use (IVD, RUO, GxP) which determines documentation, validation, and change control requirements. The plain column gxp_classification is a partial deno

Foreign key linking to quality.risk_assessment. Business justification: Sequencing protocols undergo formal risk assessment as part of validation and regulatory submission (ISO 14971 for IVD applications). sequencing_protocol links to change_control and validation_study b

Document control identifier for the approved SOP governing execution of this sequencing protocol, as managed in MasterControl or Veeva Vault. Provides a direct link from the protocol record to the procedural document for GxP audit trails and training compliance.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3; MasterControl Document Control; Veeva Vault

description: Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Sequencing protocols define acceptance criteria derived from product specifications. Columns min_q30_threshold_pct, min_yield_gb, target_coverage_depth are denormalized from product.specification. Lin

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Sequencing protocols are validated through validation studies (method validation, protocol verification, performance qualification). Each protocol version must link to its validation study for regulat

Date on which the protocol version received final approval from the qualified approver. Distinct from effective_date (when it becomes operational). Required for GxP audit trails and regulatory submissions. Captured from MasterControl electronic signature timestamp.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3; MasterControl

description: Current lifecycle state of the sequencing protocol within the GxP change control workflow. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Only protocols in 'approved' status may be used for clinical or regulated sequencing runs. Managed in Veeva Vault and MasterControl.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2; Veeva Vault Quality Management; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Name or employee identifier of the qualified person (QP) or scientific director who granted final approval for this protocol version in the GxP change control system. Supports regulatory audit trail requirements. Captured from MasterControl or Veeva Vault electronic signature records.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); ISO 13485 Section 5.5; MasterControl

description: Comma-separated list of approved library preparation kit names and catalog numbers validated for use with this sequencing protocol (e.g., 'Illumina DNA Prep (20018704), TruSeq DNA PCR-Free (20015963)'). Only kits on this list may be used in GxP-regulated runs. Validated in Benchling and controlled in MasterControl.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 7.4; MasterControl Document Control; Benchling R&D

business_glossary_term: College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accredited Flag
description: Indicates whether this protocol is covered under a CAP-accredited laboratory's scope of accreditation. True = included in CAP accreditation scope; False = not within CAP scope. Relevant for clinical genomics laboratories reporting patient results.
reference: CAP (College of American Pathologists) Accreditation Standards; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189

business_glossary_term: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) Validated Flag
description: Indicates whether this protocol has been validated under CLIA requirements for clinical laboratory use. True = CLIA validation completed and documented; False = not CLIA validated (RUO or research only). Required for protocols used in LDT or clinical reporting contexts.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP (College of American Pathologists) Accreditation Standards; ISO 15189

description: Comma-separated list of sequencing instrument models validated for use with this protocol (e.g., 'NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X Plus, NextSeq 2000'). Ensures run parameters are only applied to compatible hardware. Validated against instrument qualification records in LabVantage LIMS.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Instrument Compatibility; ISO 13485 Section 6.3; LabVantage LIMS

description: Timestamp when this sequencing protocol record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Populated automatically on insert and immutable thereafter. Supports GxP audit trail requirements.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which this protocol version became (or becomes) effective for use in sequencing operations. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM category. Protocols may not be used in regulated runs before this date. Managed through GxP change control in MasterControl.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; MasterControl

description: Specific flow cell cartridge type required by this protocol (e.g., 'S4 Flow Cell', 'SP Flow Cell', 'P2 Flow Cell', '25B Flow Cell'). Determines lane count, cluster capacity, and maximum data output. Must match the instrument model and run configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S1|S2|S4|SP|P1|P2|P3|25B|nano — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Flow Cell Configuration; NGS Best Practices

description: Number of cycles for the Index 1 (i7) read used in multiplexing to demultiplex samples on a shared flow cell. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Required for all multiplexed library pools. Null for non-indexed single-sample runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Multiplexing Configuration; NGS Demultiplexing Standards

description: Number of cycles for the Index 2 (i5) read used in dual-index multiplexing schemes. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Null for single-index or non-indexed protocols. Dual indexing reduces index-hopping artifacts in high-multiplex runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Multiplexing Configuration; NGS Demultiplexing Standards

description: Regulatory classification of the protocol's intended use. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared); LDT = Laboratory Developed Test (CLIA-regulated); CE-IVD = EU Conformité Européenne In Vitro Diagnostic. Determines applicable regulatory framework, labeling requirements, and permissible clinical use.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 (IVD); IVDR EU 2017/746 (CE-IVD); CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 (LDT); ISO 13485

description: Maximum acceptable cluster density on the flow cell surface in K/mm². Exceeding this threshold causes cluster overlap (over-clustering), degrading base call quality and Q30 scores. Paired with min_cluster_density to define the acceptable loading range.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Flow Cell QC; NGS Best Practices

description: Maximum acceptable DNA insert size in base pairs for library fragments. Inserts exceeding this threshold may indicate insufficient fragmentation, leading to reduced cluster density and read-through artifacts. Paired with min_insert_size_bp to define the acceptable library size range.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Library QC; NGS Library Preparation Best Practices; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Minimum acceptable cluster density on the flow cell surface, expressed in thousands of clusters per square millimeter (K/mm²). Below this threshold, insufficient data yield is expected. Used as a pre-run and mid-run QC gate in Illumina BaseSpace.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Flow Cell QC; NGS Best Practices

description: Minimum acceptable DNA insert size in base pairs for library fragments sequenced under this protocol. Insert sizes below this threshold indicate over-fragmentation and may degrade paired-end alignment quality. Used as a library QC acceptance criterion in LabVantage LIMS.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Library QC; NGS Library Preparation Best Practices; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Number of allowable base mismatches in the index sequence during demultiplexing before a read is assigned to the 'undetermined' bin. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase sample assignment rates but risk index cross-contamination in high-multiplex pools.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Parameters; NGS Best Practices

description: Maximum number of samples that can be pooled and multiplexed on a single flow cell lane under this protocol while maintaining the target coverage depth per sample. Governs library pooling decisions in LabVantage LIMS and run planning in BaseSpace.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Multiplexing Guidelines; NGS (Next-Generation Sequencing) Best Practices

description: Name or employee identifier of the scientist or team lead responsible for maintaining and updating this sequencing protocol. The protocol owner initiates change control requests and is accountable for scientific validity. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 5.5; GxP (Good Practice Regulations); Oracle Cloud HCM

business_glossary_term: Protected Health Information (PHI) Data Generated Flag
description: Indicates whether sequencing runs executed under this protocol generate Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA. True = PHI is generated (clinical patient samples); False = no PHI (research/RUO samples). Governs data handling, storage, and access control requirements for resulting FASTQ and downstream files.
reference: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) 45 CFR Parts 160/164; GDPR Article 9 (Special Category Data); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this protocol and version (e.g., WGS-PCR-v2.1). Used as the business identifier across LIMS, BaseSpace, and regulatory submissions. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Protocol Configuration; ISO 13485 Section 4.2

description: Full descriptive name of the sequencing protocol (e.g., 'Whole Genome Sequencing PCR-Free 150bp Paired-End'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used in reporting, SOP references, and customer-facing documentation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Protocol Configuration; MasterControl Document Control

description: Number of sequencing cycles for Read 1 (R1), determining the read length in base pairs for the forward read. Common values include 75, 100, 150, 250 cycles. Directly impacts data quality, coverage, and FASTQ file size. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Parameters; NGS (Next-Generation Sequencing) Best Practices

description: Number of sequencing cycles for Read 2 (R2) in paired-end configurations. Null for single-end protocols. Must match R1 cycle count for symmetric paired-end runs or may differ for asymmetric configurations. Governs reverse read length in base pairs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Parameters; NGS Best Practices

description: Specifies whether the sequencing run is configured for single-end or paired-end reads. Paired-end reads generate two reads per fragment (R1 and R2), improving alignment accuracy and variant calling. Directly governs flow cell programming and FASTQ output structure.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Configuration; NGS Best Practices

description: Standard reference genome assembly version used for alignment and variant calling under this protocol (e.g., GRCh38 for human, GRCm39 for mouse). Ensures reproducibility and comparability of genomic results across runs and cohorts. Aligns with NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy requirements.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; NCBI Reference Sequence Database; ACMG Variant Interpretation Standards

description: Date on which this protocol version was retired or superseded and is no longer approved for new sequencing runs. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category. Null for currently active protocols. Historical runs executed under this protocol remain valid after retirement.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2; MasterControl Document Control

description: Classification of the genomic sequencing application this protocol supports. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection and QC thresholds. WGS = Whole Genome Sequencing; WES = Whole Exome Sequencing; RNA-seq = RNA Sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Configuration; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Free-text narrative description of the sequencing protocol, including its scientific rationale, key distinguishing parameters, intended sample types, and any special handling requirements. Authored by the protocol owner and reviewed during the GxP approval process.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2; MasterControl Document Control; Benchling R&D Notebook

description: Version of the SOP document referenced by sop_document_id that is current and approved for use with this protocol version. Ensures the protocol record is always linked to the correct procedural revision, supporting GxP change control.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3; MasterControl Document Control

description: Specifies the strand orientation of the sequencing library for RNA-seq and methylation protocols. Unstranded = no strand information preserved; Forward = sense strand; Reverse = antisense strand. Critical for accurate transcript quantification and differential expression analysis. Null for DNA-based protocols.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — RNA-seq Configuration; ENCODE RNA-seq Standards

description: Protocol code of the newer version that replaces this protocol upon retirement. Null for the current active version. Enables forward navigation in the protocol version chain for change control traceability and migration of active runs.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3; MasterControl Document Control

description: File path or artifact identifier for the BED (Browser Extensible Data) file defining the genomic target regions for targeted panel or WES protocols. Null for WGS protocols. Used by bioinformatics pipelines for on-target coverage calculation and variant calling region restriction. Stored in genomic artifact repository.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Panel Configuration; UCSC Genome Browser BED Format Standard; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this sequencing protocol record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated automatically on every write. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and GxP audit trail requirements.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.4

description: Semantic version number of the sequencing protocol (e.g., 2.1.0). Incremented upon any approved change to run parameters, kit compatibility, or QC thresholds. Supports GxP change control traceability.
reference: GxP (Good Practice Regulations); ISO 13485 Section 4.2.3 — Document Control; MasterControl

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each coverage statistic record computed post-demultiplexing for a sequencing library. Primary key for the coverage_stat data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed from demultiplexed reads produced by the demultiplexing process. Adding demux_result_id to coverage_stat directly links the coverage computation to the specific demux

Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Coverage statistics are analyzed per-experiment to assess whether sequencing depth meets experimental objectives (e.g., variant detection sensitivity requirements). Drives resequencing decisions, expe

Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Coverage statistics are correlated with extraction quality in method validation studies and QC trending dashboards. Linking coverage_stat to extraction enables analysis of how extraction RIN/yield imp

Foreign key linking to sequencing.fastq_file. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed directly from FASTQ output files post-demultiplexing. Adding fastq_file_id to coverage_stat creates a direct, traceable link from the computed coverage metrics back to

Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed directly from BAM/alignment genomic artifacts. Labs must link coverage stats to the specific artifact for reprocessing triggers when artifacts are updated, QC dashboar
Reference to the sequencing library for which this coverage statistic record was computed. Links to the library preparation entity in the sequencing domain.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Library Entity

Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed for runs performed at a specific performing laboratory. Linking enables per-lab coverage QC trending, CAP proficiency testing compliance reporting, and inter-lab perfo
Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that computed these coverage statistics post-demultiplexing. Enables data lineage tracing from raw FASTQ to coverage metrics.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Analysis Run; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Coverage statistics are a formal QC checkpoint in clinical genomics (qc_status on coverage_stat). In CLIA/CAP regulated labs, coverage QC outcomes must be traceable to formal qc_result records for sam
Reference to the biological sample associated with the library. Enables traceability from coverage statistics back to the originating sample in the Sample Management domain.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sample Entity; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Reference to the sequencing run that produced the raw reads from which this coverage statistic was derived. Enables traceability from coverage metrics back to the instrument run.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Entity; ISO 13485 Section 7.5

Reference to the clinical or research assay definition against which coverage QC thresholds are evaluated. Determines the minimum coverage requirements applicable to this library.
reference: Veeva Vault — Assay Specification; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189

description: Indicates whether this library's coverage statistics have been reviewed and approved for downstream bioinformatics analysis (variant calling, annotation). Set to True only when qc_status is Pass and any required manual review has been completed.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sample Disposition; ISO 15189 Section 5.6; CAP Checklist GEN.55500

description: Classification of the sequencing assay type for which coverage statistics are computed. Determines applicable QC thresholds and on-target rate relevance. WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), Targeted panel, Amplicon, or RNA-Seq. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|Targeted|Amplicon|RNA-Seq|Other — promote to reference product if additional assay types are added]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 15189

description: Date and time when the coverage statistics were computed by the bioinformatics pipeline post-demultiplexing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Analysis Completion; ISO 15189 Section 5.5; GxP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Percentage of target bases covered at greater than or equal to 20% of the mean coverage depth. Measures how evenly reads are distributed across the target region. Higher uniformity indicates more consistent library preparation and hybridization efficiency.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Uniformity Metric; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Date and time when this coverage statistic record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2; GxP 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Percentage of mapped reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates. High duplication rates reduce effective coverage depth and can introduce variant calling artifacts. Computed by duplicate-marking tools (e.g., Picard MarkDuplicates) during bioinformatics processing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Duplicate Metrics; Picard Tools Documentation; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Quantitative measure of GC content bias in coverage distribution, typically expressed as the normalized coverage deviation across GC content bins. Values near zero indicate minimal GC bias; large deviations indicate systematic under- or over-representation of GC-rich or GC-poor regions.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — GC Bias Metrics; Picard CollectGcBiasMetrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Mean insert size of the sequencing library in base pairs (bp). Reflects the average length of the DNA fragment between adapter sequences. Deviations from expected insert size indicate library preparation issues affecting coverage uniformity and read alignment.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Insert Size Metrics; Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics; ISO 15189

description: Standard deviation of the insert size distribution in base pairs (bp). High standard deviation indicates heterogeneous fragment sizes, which may affect coverage uniformity and paired-end alignment efficiency.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Insert Size Metrics; Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics; ISO 15189

description: Number of reads successfully aligned to the reference genome. Used to compute mapping rate and assess library quality. Low mapped read counts relative to total reads indicate contamination, adapter issues, or reference mismatch.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Alignment Metrics; SAMtools flagstat; ISO 15189

description: Percentage of total reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. A key indicator of library quality and sample integrity. Typically expected to be >95% for high-quality human genomic libraries.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Alignment Metrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5; ACMG Sequencing Guidelines

description: Mean Phred quality score across all bases in the library. Phred scores represent the probability of a base call error (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). Used to assess sequencing chemistry performance and overall run quality.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Quality Metrics; Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green 1998); ISO 15189

description: Average number of times each base in the target region (or genome for WGS) is sequenced, expressed in fold-coverage (X). Primary metric for assessing sequencing depth adequacy. Computed as total aligned bases divided by target region size.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5; ACMG Sequencing Guidelines

description: Median number of times each base in the target region is sequenced, expressed in fold-coverage (X). Less sensitive to outlier regions than mean coverage; used alongside mean to assess coverage distribution skewness.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Free-text field for laboratory scientists or bioinformaticians to record observations, exceptions, or contextual information about this coverage statistic record. Used during manual QC review to document borderline cases or special circumstances.
reference: ISO 15189 Section 4.13; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1445; CAP Checklist GEN.55500

description: Percentage of aligned reads that map to the intended target region (capture panel or amplicon targets). Applicable for WES and targeted sequencing assays. Low on-target rate indicates poor hybridization capture efficiency or library quality issues.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Enrichment Metrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5; CAP Checklist MOL.37600

description: Fraction of target bases (expressed as a percentage) achieving a minimum coverage depth of 100X. Relevant for somatic variant calling in oncology panels where high depth is required to detect low-frequency variants (low LOD applications).
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; ACMG/AMP Somatic Variant Guidelines; ISO 15189

description: Fraction of target bases (expressed as a percentage) achieving a minimum coverage depth of 10X. Standard threshold for variant calling sensitivity in WES and targeted sequencing panels.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines; ISO 15189

description: Fraction of target bases (expressed as a percentage) achieving a minimum coverage depth of 20X. Common minimum threshold for clinical-grade variant calling in WES and targeted panels per CAP and CLIA guidelines.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; CAP Checklist MOL.37600; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Fraction of target bases (expressed as a percentage) achieving a minimum coverage depth of 30X. Recommended threshold for high-confidence germline variant calling in clinical WGS and WES workflows.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Coverage Metrics; ACMG Sequencing Guidelines; ISO 15189

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases with Q30 or Higher (Phred Score ≥30)
description: Percentage of bases in the library with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating 99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard metric for sequencing run quality assessment. Illumina typically specifies ≥80% Q30 as a run quality threshold.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Quality Metrics; Illumina Run Quality Specifications; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Descriptive reason for QC failure when qc_status is Fail or Borderline. Documents which specific coverage metric(s) fell below assay-defined thresholds, supporting root cause analysis and resequencing decisions.
reference: ISO 15189 Section 5.6; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Checklist GEN.55500

description: Overall QC pass/fail determination for this library's coverage statistics evaluated against assay-specific thresholds. A Pass status indicates the library meets minimum coverage requirements for downstream bioinformatics analysis. Borderline indicates marginal coverage requiring manual review.
reference: ISO 15189 Section 5.6; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP Checklist GEN.55500

description: Version of the reference genome used for read alignment and coverage computation (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, GRCm39). Critical for reproducibility and cross-sample comparability of coverage statistics. Must match the reference used in downstream variant calling pipelines.
reference: NCBI Reference Sequence Database; Ensembl Genome Browser; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Indicates whether this library has been flagged for resequencing due to insufficient coverage or QC failure. When True, the library must be re-run on the sequencer before downstream bioinformatics analysis can proceed.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sample Disposition; ISO 15189 Section 5.6; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: Date and time when the manual QC review of this coverage statistic record was completed. Supports audit trail requirements under CLIA, CAP, and GxP regulations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1445; CAP Checklist GEN.55500; GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189 Section 4.13

description: Name or identifier of the laboratory scientist or bioinformatician who performed the manual QC review of this coverage statistic record. Required for clinical assays under CLIA and CAP accreditation to maintain an auditable review trail.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1445; CAP Checklist GEN.55500; ISO 15189 Section 4.13

description: Operational system of record from which this coverage statistic record originated. Supports data lineage and provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Typically Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for automated pipeline outputs or LabVantage LIMS for manually entered records.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2; GxP 21 CFR Part 11; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Human-readable business identifier for this coverage statistic record, following the COVSTAT-{alphanumeric} naming convention. Used for cross-system referencing and audit trail purposes in LIMS and BaseSpace.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Analysis Report; ISO 13485 Section 4.2

description: Name or path of the BED file defining the target genomic regions used for on-target coverage calculations. Applicable for WES and targeted sequencing assays. Ensures coverage metrics are computed against the correct capture or amplicon panel definition.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Panel Definition; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Total size of the target genomic region in base pairs as defined by the BED file. Used as the denominator for coverage depth calculations. For WGS, this represents the effective genome size used in analysis.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Panel Metrics; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Total number of sequencing reads generated for this library after demultiplexing. Represents the raw read count before any filtering or alignment steps. Used to assess sequencing yield and library loading efficiency.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Report; ISO 15189 Section 5.5

description: Date and time when this coverage statistic record was last modified, such as after a QC review decision or recomputation. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP and ISO 13485. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 4.2; GxP 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each demultiplexing result record. Primary key for the demux_result data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures the discrete business event of demultiplexing a pooled sequencing run into per-sample FASTQ outputs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Result Object

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are delivered to customers and must be tied to accounts for data delivery tracking, consent management, and billing. Customer data portals and delivery pipelines require direct

Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_specimen. Business justification: Demux results are produced for a specific clinical specimen. While demux_result links to sample_specimen_id, the clinical_specimen carries accession number and chain of custody. Direct link enables CL

Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are evaluated in experiment context to determine if per-sample read counts meet experimental design (e.g., minimum reads per sample for statistical power). Drives sample reseque
Reference to the sequencing library (prepared sample) whose reads were assigned during demultiplexing. Provides the authoritative link between the demux result and the source library.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Library Preparation Record; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Library Object

description: Reference to the original demux_result record when this record represents a reprocessing event (reprocessing_flag = True). Enables full lineage tracing from reprocessed results back to the original demultiplexing output. Null when reprocessing_flag = False.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 — Audit Trail); ISO 15189 — Corrective Action Traceability

description: Reference to the research or clinical project under which this sequencing and demultiplexing activity was performed. Enables project-level aggregation of demux results for reporting, billing, and data governance purposes.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Project Record; Benchling — Project Object; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Demultiplexing results represent a formal QC checkpoint (qc_pass_flag, qc_failure_reason on demux_result). In CLIA/CAP regulated genomics, the demux QC outcome must be traceable to a formal qc_result

Foreign key linking to sequencing.run_lane. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are generated per lane within a sequencing run. demux_result currently has run_id (to sequencing_run) and lane_number (integer), but no formal FK to the run_lane master record.
Reference to the sequencing run from which this demultiplexing result was produced. Links the demux output back to the originating instrument run and flow cell.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Object; LabVantage LIMS — Run Record

description: Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Data delivery format, retention period, turnaround time, and release conditions for demux results are governed by the customer's service agreement. Direct linkage enables automated compliance checking

Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are produced by specific software versions. Columns demux_software and demux_software_version are denormalized from product.software_release. For data provenance, regulatory sub

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are produced per-sample as part of a clinical test order workflow. Linking demux_result to test_order enables direct traceability from raw demux output to the clinical order for

Total number of sequencing reads assigned to this library after demultiplexing. Primary quantitative outcome of the demux process. Used to assess library representation and coverage depth adequacy for downstream analysis.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Read Count Metric; NGS QC Standards

description: Percentage of total reads in the lane assigned to this library (assigned_read_count / total lane reads × 100). Indicates library balance within the pool. Values significantly deviating from expected multiplexing ratio trigger QC review.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demux Metrics; NGS Multiplexing QC Guidelines

description: The date and time when this demux result record was first captured in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and GxP compliance.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records); ISO 27001 — Audit Logging

description: Indicates whether the FASTQ files produced by this demux result have been formally released for downstream bioinformatics processing or delivery to the customer/requester. True = released; False = held (pending QC review or approval). Supports data governance and release management workflows.
reference: ISO 15189 — Result Release Authorization; CLIA — Result Reporting; HIPAA — PHI Release Controls

description: The date and time at which the demultiplexing process completed for this library within the sequencing run. Principal business event timestamp. Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculation and SLA monitoring.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Job Completion Time; ISO 15189 — TAT Monitoring

description: Externally-known business identifier for the demultiplexing execution job, as assigned by the demux software (e.g., bcl2fastq job ID or DRAGEN BCL Convert run name). Used for cross-referencing with Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub and bioinformatics pipeline logs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demux Job Identifier; bcl2fastq / DRAGEN BCL Convert

description: The date and time at which the demultiplexing process was initiated for this run. Used in conjunction with demux_completion_timestamp to calculate demux processing duration for performance monitoring.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Job Start Time

description: Current lifecycle status of the demultiplexing job for this library-run combination. 'completed' indicates successful assignment; 'partial' indicates some reads assigned but below threshold; 'failed' indicates job error or no reads assigned.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Job Status; LabVantage LIMS — Workflow Status

description: Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Index Read 1 (i7 index) sequences. Generated when index reads are retained in FASTQ format. Used for index QC validation and troubleshooting demultiplexing failures.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Index FASTQ; bcl2fastq Documentation

description: Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Read 1 (forward reads) generated for this library after demultiplexing. Provides the authoritative link between the demux result and the resulting FASTQ output file for downstream bioinformatics pipeline ingestion.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — FASTQ File Object; FASTQ Format Specification (Cock et al., 2010)

description: Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Read 2 (reverse reads) generated for this library after demultiplexing. Populated only for paired-end sequencing runs. Null for single-end runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — FASTQ File Object; FASTQ Format Specification (Cock et al., 2010)

description: Number of reads assigned to this library that contained one or more base mismatches in the index sequence relative to the expected index, within the allowed mismatch tolerance. Elevated counts may indicate index synthesis errors or library contamination.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demux Mismatch Metrics; bcl2fastq Documentation

description: The nucleotide index sequence (i7 and optionally i5 dual-index) matched to assign reads to this library during demultiplexing. Supports single-index (e.g., ACGTACGT) and dual-index (e.g., ACGTACGT+TTGGCCAA) formats. Critical for multiplexing validation and index collision analysis.
reference: Illumina Index Adapter Sequences; NGS Library Preparation Standards

description: The sequencing instrument platform model used for the run (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 2000, MiSeq). Determines flow cell format, lane count, and applicable demux parameters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NovaSeq_6000|NovaSeq_X|NextSeq_2000|MiSeq|HiSeq_4000|PromethION|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Instrument Specifications; LabVantage LIMS — Instrument Registry

description: The physical lane number on the flow cell from which reads were demultiplexed. Flow cells may contain 1, 2, 4, or 8 lanes depending on instrument type (e.g., NovaSeq, HiSeq). Required for per-lane QC analysis and troubleshooting.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Lane Metrics; Illumina Flow Cell Specification

description: Mean Phred quality score across all bases in Read 1 for this library. Phred score (Q score) represents base call accuracy (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key NGS QC metric; values below Q30 threshold may trigger QC failure.
reference: Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green, 1998); Illumina BaseSpace QC Metrics; CLIA — Analytical Quality Requirements

description: Mean Phred quality score across all bases in Read 2 for this library. Typically lower than R1 due to signal degradation in paired-end sequencing. Null for single-end runs.
reference: Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green, 1998); Illumina BaseSpace QC Metrics

description: The minimum number of assigned reads required for this library to pass QC, as defined by the applicable assay protocol or SOP. Stored at the record level to capture the threshold in effect at the time of demultiplexing, supporting historical QC audits.
reference: ISO 15189 — QC Acceptance Criteria; CAP — NGS Checklist; Internal SOP

description: Number of allowable base mismatches permitted when matching a read's index sequence to the expected library index during demultiplexing. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase assignment rate but risk index cross-contamination between libraries.
reference: bcl2fastq User Guide; DRAGEN BCL Convert Documentation; Illumina Demultiplexing Best Practices

description: Percentage of Read 1 bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30). Industry-standard NGS QC threshold; Illumina specifies ≥75–80% Q30 as passing for most platforms. Used in run-level and library-level QC reporting.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Quality Metrics; CLIA — Analytical Performance Standards; CAP — NGS Checklist

description: Percentage of Read 2 bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30). Null for single-end runs. Compared against run-specific QC thresholds for paired-end quality assessment.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Quality Metrics; CAP — NGS Checklist

description: Descriptive reason for QC failure when qc_pass_flag = False. Examples include: 'low_read_count', 'high_undetermined_rate', 'index_collision', 'low_q30'. Populated by automated QC rules or manual review. Null when qc_pass_flag = True.
reference: ISO 15189 — Non-Conformance Documentation; CLIA — QC Failure Reporting; CAP — NGS QC Checklist

description: Indicates whether the demultiplexing result for this library passed all defined QC thresholds (read count, percentage assigned, undetermined rate). True = QC passed; False = QC failed. Authoritative gate for downstream bioinformatics pipeline processing.
reference: ISO 15189 — Quality Control; CLIA — Laboratory QC Requirements; Illumina BaseSpace QC Metrics

description: Length in base pairs (bp) of Read 1 sequences produced for this library. Determined by the sequencing run configuration. Critical for downstream alignment and variant calling pipeline compatibility.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Run Parameters; NGS Read Length Standards

description: Length in base pairs (bp) of Read 2 sequences produced for this library. Null for single-end runs. Used to confirm paired-end symmetry and pipeline input validation.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Run Parameters; NGS Read Length Standards

description: Indicates whether this demux result was generated by reprocessing a previously demultiplexed run (e.g., due to sample sheet correction, software update, or QC failure remediation). True = reprocessed; False = original demux. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 — Audit Trail); ISO 15189 — Corrective Action Records

description: Intended use classification of the sequencing run from which this demux result originates. RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), clinical_validation, or research. Governs applicable regulatory requirements and data handling obligations.
reference: FDA — IVD Regulation; IVDR (EU) 2017/746; CLIA — Test Classification; CAP Accreditation

description: Version identifier of the sample sheet used to configure the demultiplexing run. The sample sheet defines library-to-index mappings and run parameters. Versioning ensures traceability and supports reprocessing with corrected configurations.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet Specification; LabVantage LIMS — Sample Sheet Management

description: Classification of the sequencing assay type for this library (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel, amplicon, RNA-seq). Determines applicable QC thresholds and downstream bioinformatics pipeline routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted|amplicon|RNA-seq|single-cell|metagenomics|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; CAP — NGS Checklist; CLIA — Test Classification

description: Total number of reads generated in the flow cell lane from which this library was demultiplexed. Used as the denominator for calculating assigned_read_pct and undetermined_read_pct. Enables per-lane yield assessment.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Lane Metrics; Illumina Run QC Documentation

description: Number of reads in the lane that could not be assigned to any library due to index mismatch or ambiguity. High undetermined counts indicate index contamination, library preparation issues, or incorrect sample sheet configuration.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Undetermined Reads Metric; bcl2fastq Documentation

description: Percentage of total lane reads that were unassigned (undetermined_read_count / total lane reads × 100). A key QC metric; values exceeding the defined threshold (typically >5%) trigger run-level QC failure investigation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demux QC Metrics; NGS QC Thresholds

description: The date and time when this demux result record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data lineage auditing.
reference: GxP (21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records); ISO 27001 — Audit Logging

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the FASTQ file record in the sequencing data inventory. Primary key for this master resource entity representing a raw sequencing output file.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Dataset Object Model

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: FASTQ files are delivered to customers; account linkage is required for data access control, PHI consent enforcement, retention policy management, and customer billing. Genomics data portals enforce p

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: FASTQ files from IVD/clinical runs must be traceable to the regulatory approval governing their use classification for data governance and regulatory compliance. 'intended_use_class' is a denormalized

Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: FASTQ files are generated under a specific assay version's sequencing parameters (read length, pipeline version, quality thresholds). CLIA labs must document which assay version governed the sequencin

Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, NextSeq, MiSeq) that generated the raw data for this FASTQ file. Enables instrument-level performance tracking, maintenance correlation, and QC trend analysis.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Instrument Management; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Instrument Registry

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_specimen. Business justification: FASTQ files are derived from a clinical specimen. While fastq_file links to sample_specimen_id, the clinical_specimen carries accession number, chain of custody, and consent status. Direct link enable

Reference to the demultiplexing process execution that generated this FASTQ file from the raw BCL sequencing output. Enables traceability to the specific demultiplexing parameters, software version, and sample sheet used.

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: FASTQ file release decisions (pipeline_ready_flag, data_release_flag) are made against product specifications (Q30 threshold, read length, quality score). For data release QC and regulatory compliance

Indicates whether adapter sequences were trimmed from reads during or after demultiplexing. Adapter trimming affects downstream alignment and variant calling accuracy. Relevant for pipeline configuration validation.

description: The unique dataset identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub to the dataset object containing this FASTQ file. Used for cross-referencing and API retrieval from BaseSpace.

description: The compression algorithm applied to the FASTQ file. Standard for NGS data is gzip (.gz). Determines decompression requirements for bioinformatics pipeline ingestion and storage cost calculations.
reference: FASTQ Format Specification; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — File Storage Standards

description: The informed consent status of the subject associated with this FASTQ file's sample. Governs permissible uses of the genomic data (research, clinical, secondary use). Withdrawn consent triggers data deletion or de-identification workflows per GDPR and HIPAA.

description: The data sensitivity classification level assigned to this FASTQ file per Genomics Biotech data governance policy. Restricted = highest sensitivity (PHI/PII), Confidential = sensitive business data, Internal = general internal use, Public = no restrictions. Drives access control, encryption, and retention policies.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Classification; HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; GDPR Article 32; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Indicates whether this FASTQ file is encrypted at rest in cloud storage. Encryption is mandatory for PHI-containing files under HIPAA Security Rule and for GxP-regulated data. Supports security compliance auditing.

description: The full file name of the FASTQ output file including extension (e.g., SampleA_S1_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz). Follows Illumina naming conventions encoding sample, lane, read number, and index.
