description: Cloud storage URI indicating the full path to the FASTQ file on BaseSpace or Amazon S3 (e.g., s3://genomics-biotech-seq/runs/RUN001/SampleA_R1.fastq.gz). Serves as the authoritative location reference for downstream bioinformatics pipeline access.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Storage API; AWS S3 Object Naming

description: The size of the FASTQ file in bytes as stored in cloud storage. Used for storage capacity planning, data transfer cost estimation, and file integrity verification. Compressed (gzip) size is recorded.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — File Metadata; AWS S3 Object Metadata

description: Current lifecycle status of the FASTQ file in the sequencing data inventory. Pending = demultiplexing in progress, Available = ready for pipeline consumption, Archived = moved to cold storage, Deleted = purged per retention policy, Failed = generation error, Quarantined = held for QC investigation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — File Lifecycle Management; GxP Data Integrity Guidelines

description: The date and time when this FASTQ file was generated by the demultiplexing process. Represents the principal business event timestamp — the moment raw sequencing data became available as a FASTQ artifact. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations and data lineage.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — File Metadata; GxP Data Integrity Guidelines; CLIA TAT Requirements

description: The nucleotide index sequence (barcode) used to identify and demultiplex this sample from a multiplexed sequencing run. Composed of ACGT bases. Critical for validating correct sample assignment and detecting index hopping or cross-contamination.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet; Multiplexing and Demultiplexing Standards

description: The MD5 cryptographic hash of the FASTQ file used to verify file integrity during transfer, storage, and archival. Mandatory for data integrity validation in GxP-regulated environments and regulatory submissions.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records; GxP Data Integrity Guidelines (MHRA, EMA); ISO 27001

business_glossary_term: Mean Phred Quality Score (Base Quality Score)
description: The mean Phred quality score (Q-score) across all bases in this FASTQ file. Phred scores represent the probability of a base call error (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key QC metric for raw sequencing data quality assessment per NGS best practices.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — QC Metrics; Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green, 1998); ISO 15189

description: The mean length of sequencing reads in this FASTQ file measured in base pairs (bp). Reflects the sequencing chemistry and run configuration. Relevant for alignment pipeline parameter selection and quality benchmarking.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Parameters; FASTQ Format Specification

business_glossary_term: Percentage of Bases at Q30 or Above (Phred Score ≥30)
description: The percentage of bases in this FASTQ file with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard QC threshold for NGS data quality; typically ≥80% Q30 is required for clinical-grade sequencing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — QC Metrics; FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Devices; ISO 15189

description: The percentage of reads in this FASTQ file that passed the Illumina chastity filter (PF reads). Reads failing the filter are excluded from analysis. A key indicator of cluster quality and flow cell performance.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Statistics; Illumina Cluster Density and PF Metrics

description: Indicates whether this FASTQ file is associated with Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA. When True, the file is subject to HIPAA Privacy and Security Rule requirements, access restrictions, and audit logging obligations. Drives data governance and access control enforcement.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR Part 164; GDPR Article 9 (Special Category Data); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Indicates whether this FASTQ file has passed all QC checks and is cleared for ingestion into bioinformatics analysis pipelines. When True, the file is eligible for alignment, variant calling, and downstream processing. Prevents failed or quarantined files from entering production pipelines.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Pipeline Trigger Logic; LabVantage LIMS — Workflow Automation

description: Free-text description of the reason for QC failure when qc_status is 'fail' or 'review'. Documents specific quality issues (e.g., low Q30 percentage, insufficient read count, adapter contamination) for root cause analysis and CAPA processes.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP NGS Checklist; GxP CAPA Requirements

description: The QC assessment outcome for this FASTQ file based on Phred scores, read counts, and other quality metrics. Pass = meets all QC thresholds, Fail = below minimum thresholds, Review = borderline requiring manual review, Not Assessed = QC not yet performed. Governs eligibility for downstream bioinformatics processing.
reference: ISO 15189 — Medical Laboratories Quality; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; CAP NGS Checklist; FDA NGS IVD Guidance

description: Designates the read type of this FASTQ file within a paired-end or indexed sequencing run. R1 = forward read, R2 = reverse read, I1 = index 1 (i7), I2 = index 2 (i5). Critical for demultiplexing validation and downstream alignment pipeline configuration.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — FASTQ File Naming Convention; NGS Library Preparation Standards

description: The calculated date on which this FASTQ file becomes eligible for deletion or archival based on the applied retention policy. Enables automated data lifecycle management and compliance with regulatory retention mandates.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1105; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.530; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: The data retention policy applied to this FASTQ file, governing how long the file must be retained before eligible for deletion or archival. Clinical data typically requires 10-year retention per CLIA/CAP; RUO data may follow shorter policies. Drives automated lifecycle management.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR Part 493.1105; CAP Accreditation Standards; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.530; GDPR Article 5(1)(e); FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: The type of NGS sequencing assay that produced this FASTQ file. Determines applicable bioinformatics pipelines, QC thresholds, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|amplicon|RNA-seq|metagenomics|single_cell — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Guidance on NGS-Based IVD Devices (2018); Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Application Types

description: The cloud storage tier in which this FASTQ file currently resides. Hot = frequently accessed (active analysis), Warm = infrequent access, Cold = rare access, Archive = long-term retention (e.g., AWS Glacier). Drives storage cost management and retrieval SLA expectations.
reference: AWS S3 Storage Classes; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Storage Management

description: The total number of sequencing reads contained in this FASTQ file after demultiplexing. A primary indicator of sequencing yield for this sample-lane combination. Used to assess whether minimum coverage depth thresholds are met for downstream analysis.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Statistics; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493

description: The date and time when this FASTQ file record was most recently modified in the sequencing data inventory. Tracks status changes, QC updates, and metadata corrections for audit compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 — Audit Trails; GxP Data Integrity Guidelines; ISO 27001

description: The date and time when this FASTQ file was successfully uploaded to cloud storage (BaseSpace or S3). Distinct from generation_timestamp; captures the transfer completion event for data availability tracking and SLA monitoring.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each flow cell master record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Primary key for the flow_cell data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

description: Current lifecycle status of the physical flow cell consumable. Tracks the operational state from receipt through consumption or disposal. 'in_stock' = received and available; 'reserved' = allocated to a planned run; 'loaded' = currently on instrument; 'consumed' = run completed; 'failed' = quality failure; 'expired' = past expiry date; 'quarantined' = held for investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_stock|reserved|loaded|consumed|failed|expired|quarantined — promote to reference product]

description: Instrument-specific flow cell format designation indicating throughput tier and lane configuration (e.g., S1, S2, S4, SP for NovaSeq 6000; MID, HIGH for NextSeq 550; P1, P2, P3 for NovaSeq X). Determines maximum output (Gb), number of lanes, and cluster density specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S1|S2|S4|SP|MID|HIGH|P1|P2|P3 — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Specification Sheets; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub Metadata Standards

description: The sequencing instrument platform for which this flow cell is designed and compatible (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 2000, MiSeq). Governs compatibility rules for loading, reagent kits, and run parameters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NovaSeq 6000|NovaSeq X|NextSeq 550|NextSeq 2000|MiSeq|HiSeq 4000|iSeq 100 — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Instrument Compatibility Matrix; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Primary sequencing application for which this flow cell is designated (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted sequencing, RNA-seq, single-cell). Drives run parameter configuration and quality acceptance criteria. Also distinguishes Research Use Only (RUO) from In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) use contexts for regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_sequencing|amplicon|RNA_seq|single_cell|RUO|IVD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

description: Indicates whether the flow cell is designed for single use only (True) or supports multiple sequencing runs (False). Governs reuse policies, disposal procedures, and run scheduling logic. Most modern Illumina flow cells are single-use; this flag supports future platform compatibility.

description: Number of independent sequencing lanes on the flow cell (e.g., 1, 2, or 4 lanes for NovaSeq S1/S2/S4). Each lane can be loaded with a separate library pool, enabling multiplexing strategies. Drives capacity planning for sample batching and run scheduling.
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Specification Sheets; NGS Run Planning Best Practices

description: Barcode label scanned into LabVantage LIMS at receipt to register the flow cell in the laboratory inventory system. Used for physical tracking, chain-of-custody documentation, and automated workflow triggering in the LIMS. May differ from the manufacturer serial number.

description: Date and time when the flow cell was physically loaded onto the sequencing instrument, marking the transition from 'reserved' to 'loaded' status. Recorded in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub and LabVantage LIMS. Used for run scheduling, instrument utilization analysis, and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.

description: Date the flow cell was manufactured by the supplier, as printed on the product label or Certificate of Analysis (COA). Used for shelf-life calculations, lot traceability, and quality investigations. Sourced from the COA accompanying the shipment.

description: Free-text field for laboratory personnel to record observations, anomalies, deviations, or special handling instructions associated with this flow cell. Examples include cold chain deviation details, physical damage observations, or QA hold reasons. Captured in LabVantage LIMS.

description: SAP S/4HANA purchase order number under which this flow cell was procured. Provides procurement lineage linking the physical consumable to the financial transaction, supplier contract, and goods receipt documentation. Supports COGS tracking and financial reconciliation.

description: Date on which the incoming QC inspection was performed for this flow cell. Used for compliance audit trails, SOP adherence tracking, and calculating time-from-receipt-to-clearance metrics.
reference: ISO 13485 Section 7.4.3; GMP 21 CFR Part 211.84; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Result of the incoming quality control inspection performed upon receipt of the flow cell. 'pending' = inspection not yet completed; 'passed' = meets acceptance criteria; 'failed' = does not meet criteria, quarantined; 'waived' = inspection waived per approved SOP (e.g., for pre-qualified lots). Managed in SAP S/4HANA QM and LabVantage LIMS.

description: Lot number of the sequencing reagent kit used in conjunction with this flow cell. Enables paired traceability between the flow cell consumable and its associated reagent kit for quality investigations, recall management, and GMP compliance. Sourced from LabVantage LIMS run setup records.

description: Date the flow cell was physically received at the Genomics Biotech facility and logged into inventory. Recorded in SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt and LabVantage LIMS. Used to calculate storage duration, validate shelf life, and support FIFO inventory management.

description: Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number printed on the physical flow cell cartridge. Used as the primary external identifier for traceability across LabVantage LIMS, Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, and SAP S/4HANA inventory management. Enables one-to-one mapping between the physical consumable and its digital record.

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the physical storage location (e.g., freezer unit, shelf, rack position) where the flow cell is currently stored within the facility. Managed in SAP S/4HANA MM Warehouse Management and LabVantage LIMS. Enables rapid physical retrieval and inventory audits.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Warehouse Management; ISO 13485 Section 6.3 — Infrastructure

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius as specified by the manufacturer. Paired with storage_temperature_min_c to define the acceptable cold chain range. Monitored against environmental monitoring system data to ensure GMP-compliant storage conditions.

description: Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius as specified by the manufacturer. Used to validate cold chain compliance during receipt inspection and ongoing storage monitoring. Deviations from this range trigger quarantine and quality investigation workflows.

description: Actual procurement cost per flow cell unit in the operating currency (USD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM invoice verification. reference: GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Unique identifier for this lane as recorded in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, the primary system of record for sequencing data management. Enables direct linkage between the enterprise data lakehouse and the BaseSpace platform for data retrieval and audit.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Number of clusters generated per square millimeter on this lane, expressed in thousands (K/mm²). A key sequencing performance metric; values outside the instrument-specific acceptable range indicate over- or under-clustering, leading to reduced data quality or yield.
reference: Illumina NovaSeq 6000 System Guide; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Percentage of clusters on this lane that pass the Illumina chastity filter, indicating the proportion of high-quality reads retained for downstream analysis. Industry benchmark is typically ≥75–80%. Low values indicate poor library quality, phasing issues, or flow cell defects.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CAP

description: Date and time when this run lane record was first created in the system, typically upon run initiation or lane assignment. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED. Required for data lineage, GxP audit trail, and regulatory compliance.
reference: GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; ISO 27001

description: Current status of the demultiplexing process for this lane, which separates pooled sample reads by index barcode to generate per-sample FASTQ files. Tracks whether the bioinformatics demultiplexing step has been initiated, completed, or encountered errors.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Sequencing error rate for this lane, expressed as a percentage, calculated from PhiX alignment. Reflects the per-base mismatch rate against the PhiX reference and is a key indicator of instrument and reagent performance. Typical acceptable threshold is <1%.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CAP

description: File system or cloud storage path (e.g., AWS S3 URI or BaseSpace project path) where the demultiplexed FASTQ files for this lane are stored. Enables downstream bioinformatics pipelines to locate and consume the raw sequencing data output.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; AWS; ISO 27001

description: Length of Index Read 1 (i7 index) in base pairs used for demultiplexing samples on this lane. Defines the barcode length for sample identification in multiplexed sequencing runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Illumina Multiplexing Guidelines

description: Length of Index Read 2 (i5 index) in base pairs used for dual-index demultiplexing on this lane. Null for single-index runs. Dual indexing reduces index hopping and improves sample assignment accuracy in high-multiplex runs.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Illumina Multiplexing Guidelines

description: Date and time when sequencing completed on this lane. Together with lane_start_timestamp, enables calculation of lane-level sequencing duration and instrument cycle time for operational efficiency reporting.

Free-text or coded description of the root cause when a lane fails QC (lane_qc_status = 'fail'). Captures failure categories such as low cluster density, high error rate, low Q30, PhiX misalignment, or reagent lot issues. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA, and instrument troubleshooting.
reference: ISO 15189; CAP; CLIA; GxP; Veeva Vault QMS

description: Physical lane position number on the flow cell (e.g., 1–8 for NovaSeq 6000 S4 flow cell). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_SEQUENCE — the ordinal position of this lane within the flow cell/run.
reference: Illumina NovaSeq 6000 System Guide; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Overall Quality Control (QC) disposition for this lane based on cluster density, clusters passing filter, PhiX alignment, and yield thresholds. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline gating and sample release decisions. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pass|fail|warning|pending|under_review|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA; CAP; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Date and time when sequencing commenced on this lane. Used for Turnaround Time (TAT) calculation, instrument utilization reporting, and scheduling of downstream bioinformatics processing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Total sequencing output (data yield) generated from this lane, expressed in gigabases (Gb). Primary throughput metric for lane-level performance assessment and SLA compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_QUANTITY.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CAP

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Lane Reference
description: External identifier for this lane record as assigned by the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), such as LabVantage. Enables cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer analytical record and the operational LIMS source of record.
reference: LabVantage LIMS; GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Concentration of the library or pool loaded onto this lane, expressed in picomolar (pM). Critical operational parameter that directly influences cluster density and sequencing quality. Deviations from the target loading concentration are a primary root cause of lane failures.
reference: Illumina Library Preparation and Sequencing SOPs; ISO 15189

description: Percentage of bases on this lane with a Phred quality score ≥30 (Q30), indicating a base call accuracy of 99.9% or higher. Industry-standard quality threshold for NGS data; Illumina specifies Q30 ≥75–80% as a minimum acceptance criterion for most applications.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CAP; CLIA

description: Average Phred base quality score across all bases sequenced on this lane. Provides a single summary quality metric complementing the Q30 percentage for lane-level quality trending and instrument performance monitoring.

Count of individual samples multiplexed (pooled) and loaded onto this lane. Determines the expected per-sample read depth after demultiplexing and is a key parameter for capacity planning and SLA compliance per sample.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; LabVantage LIMS

description: Percentage of reads on this lane that aligned to the PhiX reference genome. Used to validate the PhiX spike-in and assess lane-level sequencing error rate. Significant deviation from the expected PhiX spike-in percentage indicates a lane-level quality issue.

Percentage of PhiX Control v3 library spiked into this lane for sequencing quality monitoring and calibration. PhiX is an Illumina-standard internal control used to assess error rate, base quality, and instrument performance per lane.
reference: Illumina PhiX Control v3 Guide; ISO 15189; CAP

description: Date and time when the QC review of this lane was completed and the QC disposition (pass/fail/warning) was recorded. Required for GxP audit trail and regulatory submission traceability.
reference: GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; CAP; CLIA

description: Reference to the Quality Control (QC) scientist or analyst who reviewed and approved or rejected the lane QC results. Required for GxP electronic signature compliance and audit trail integrity under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; CAP; MasterControl

description: Length of Read 1 in base pairs (bp) for this lane, as configured in the sequencing run setup. Determines the sequencing mode (e.g., 150 bp paired-end) and directly impacts the genomic applications supported (WGS, WES, targeted panels).
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Illumina Run Setup Documentation

description: Length of Read 2 in base pairs (bp) for paired-end sequencing on this lane. Null for single-read runs. Together with read_length_r1, defines the paired-end read configuration (e.g., 2×150 bp) used for library insert size estimation and alignment accuracy.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Illumina Run Setup Documentation

description: Number of reads on this lane that passed the Illumina chastity filter and are available for downstream bioinformatics processing. Directly determines the effective sequencing depth available for variant calling and genomic analysis.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CAP

description: Number of times this lane has been requeued for resequencing due to QC failure or insufficient yield. Tracks repeat failure patterns for root cause analysis and supports escalation decisions when a lane repeatedly fails.

Indicates whether this lane requires resequencing (requeue) due to QC failure or insufficient yield. Triggers downstream sample management and scheduling workflows in the LIMS to prioritize reruns and maintain customer SLA commitments.

Sequencing run mode for this lane: paired-end (reads generated from both ends of the DNA fragment) or single-read (reads generated from one end only). Determines FASTQ file structure and alignment strategy for downstream bioinformatics processing.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; Illumina Run Setup Documentation

description: Genomic sequencing application type assigned to this lane (e.g., Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq). description: Total number of sequencing reads generated from this lane before quality filtering. Provides a raw throughput count used alongside yield (Gb) for per-lane performance benchmarking and capacity planning.

Percentage of reads on this lane that could not be assigned to a sample index during demultiplexing. High undetermined read rates indicate index contamination, index hopping, or sample sheet errors. Typically acceptable threshold is <5–10%.

Date and time when this run lane record was last modified, such as when QC metrics were populated post-run or when the QC status was updated. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED. Required for data lineage and GxP audit trail.
reference: GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 15189; ISO 27001

Percentage of reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. Low alignment rates may indicate sample contamination, wrong species, or library preparation failures. Sourced from samtools flagstat or DRAGEN alignment metrics.

Classification of the sequencing assay type performed: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, amplicon, RNA-seq, or other. Determines applicable coverage thresholds and QC pass/fail criteria for the run or library.
reference: CLIA; CAP; ISO 15189; FDA IVD guidance

description: Number of clusters per square millimeter on the flow cell surface, expressed in thousands (K/mm²). Optimal cluster density is critical for sequencing quality; too high causes overcrowding and quality degradation, too low reduces yield. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 13485; GMP

description: Percentage of clusters that pass Illumina's chastity filter, indicating the proportion of high-quality reads retained after filtering. A key run-level QC metric; typically ≥75–85% is expected for acceptable run quality. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Percentage of target bases covered at ≥20% of the mean coverage depth, measuring how evenly reads are distributed across the target region. High uniformity indicates consistent library preparation and sequencing performance. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Date and time when this QC metric record was first created in the system, typically upon automated ingestion from the source QC tool (InterOp, BaseSpace, Picard, or samtools). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.

Percentage of reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates, which are removed prior to variant calling. High duplication rates reduce effective coverage and indicate over-amplification during library preparation. Sourced from Picard MarkDuplicates.

Percentage of base calls that do not match the reference sequence, typically measured using PhiX control spike-in. Reflects sequencing chemistry and instrument performance. Lower values indicate higher accuracy. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Quantitative measure of GC content bias in sequencing coverage, indicating the degree to which GC-rich or GC-poor regions are over- or under-represented relative to expected coverage. Values near 0 indicate minimal bias. Sourced from Picard CollectGcBiasMetrics.

Standard deviation of the insert size distribution in base pairs. High standard deviation indicates heterogeneous fragmentation, which can affect alignment accuracy and coverage uniformity. Sourced from Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics.

Average number of times each base in the target region (genome, exome, or panel) is covered by sequencing reads, expressed as fold-coverage (X). Primary metric for assessing whether sufficient sequencing depth was achieved for reliable variant calling. Populated for library-level records. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Average size of the DNA fragment inserted between sequencing adapters, in base pairs. Reflects library preparation quality; deviations from expected insert size indicate degradation, over-fragmentation, or adapter contamination. Sourced from Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics.

Average Phred quality score across all bases in the run or lane, representing overall base call accuracy. A Phred score of 30 corresponds to 99.9% accuracy. Used for instrument performance benchmarking and run-level QC trending.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Median fold-coverage across the target region, less sensitive to outliers than mean coverage. Used alongside mean_coverage_depth to assess coverage uniformity and detect regions of extreme over- or under-representation. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Indicates the level of aggregation at which this QC metric record applies: 'run' for instrument-wide metrics, 'lane' for per-lane flow cell metrics, or 'library' for per-library/per-sample coverage metrics. Determines which metric fields are populated and how the record is used in QC workflows.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; bcl2fastq; ISO 15189

description: Identifies the upstream tool or system from which this QC metric record was derived: Illumina InterOp files, BaseSpace, bcl2fastq demultiplexing stats, Picard alignment tools, samtools, DRAGEN, or other. Supports data lineage tracking and metric reproducibility audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: InterOp|BaseSpace|bcl2fastq|Picard|samtools|DRAGEN|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; GxP; ISO 27001

description: Percentage of aligned reads that map to the intended target region (exome capture or targeted panel). Applicable for WES and targeted sequencing assays. Low on-target rates indicate poor library enrichment or capture efficiency. Sourced from Picard CollectHsMetrics.

Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 100X, expressed as a percentage. Used for high-depth targeted sequencing panels, somatic variant calling, and liquid biopsy applications requiring high sensitivity at low allele frequencies. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 10X, expressed as a percentage. Used as a coverage adequacy threshold for low-depth applications and as a QC gate for variant calling sensitivity. Sourced from Picard CollectHsMetrics or samtools depth.

Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 20X, expressed as a percentage. Standard coverage threshold for WES clinical assays; used to assess whether sufficient depth was achieved for reliable heterozygous variant detection. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 30X, expressed as a percentage. Common coverage threshold for WGS clinical applications; used to confirm adequate depth for high-confidence variant calling across the genome. Sourced from Picard or samtools.

Number of reads passing Illumina's chastity filter (PF reads), representing high-quality reads used in downstream alignment and variant calling. Distinct from total_reads which includes all raw clusters. Sourced from Illumina InterOp or bcl2fastq.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Percentage of reads that align to the PhiX bacteriophage control genome, which is spiked into sequencing runs as an internal quality control standard. Used to validate sequencing chemistry performance and calibrate error rate measurements.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Sequencing error rate calculated from PhiX control reads, providing an unbiased measure of instrument and chemistry accuracy independent of sample biology. Sourced from Illumina InterOp. Used for instrument performance trending and GxP compliance.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA; GxP

description: Percentage of bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard instrument performance metric; Illumina typically requires ≥75–80% Q30 for run acceptance. Sourced from Illumina InterOp or BaseSpace.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA; CAP

description: Free-text or structured description of the reason(s) for QC failure or warning status, such as 'Q30 below threshold', 'high duplication rate', or 'low on-target rate'. Populated when qc_status is 'fail' or 'warning'. Supports root-cause analysis and corrective action tracking.

Username or employee identifier of the scientist or QA analyst who performed the manual QC review and sign-off for this metric record. Required for GxP audit trail and electronic signature compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Date and time when the QC review and sign-off was completed for this metric record. Supports GxP audit trail, turnaround time (TAT) tracking, and regulatory submission documentation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11; GxP; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Overall QC determination for this metric record: 'pass' indicates all applicable thresholds met, 'fail' indicates one or more critical thresholds breached, 'warning' indicates marginal performance requiring review, 'pending_review' indicates awaiting manual QC sign-off, 'invalidated' indicates the run or library was voided. Central to GxP compliance reporting and run release decisions.
reference: ISO 15189; CLIA; CAP; GxP; FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: Length of sequencing reads in base pairs (bp) for this run or library (e.g., 150 bp for paired-end 150). Determines the resolution of variant calling and structural variant detection. Sourced from run configuration in Illumina BaseSpace.

Indicates whether the sequencing run used single-end or paired-end read chemistry. Paired-end reads provide improved alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Affects coverage calculation and bioinformatics pipeline configuration.
Calendar date on which the sequencing run was initiated. Used as the principal business event date for instrument performance trending, throughput reporting, and time-series QC analysis.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Total number of sequencing reads generated for the run, lane, or library before quality filtering. Used to assess raw sequencing throughput and as the denominator for percentage-based QC metrics such as clusters passing filter and undetermined index rate.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; bcl2fastq; ISO 15189

description: Percentage of reads that could not be assigned to a sample index during demultiplexing, indicating index hopping, contamination, or library preparation issues. High undetermined rates may trigger run failure or sample reprocessing. Sourced from bcl2fastq demultiplexing stats.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; bcl2fastq; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Date and time when this QC metric record was last modified, such as when a QC status was updated following manual review or when a metric value was corrected. Required for GxP audit trail and data integrity compliance.

Total sequencing output in gigabases (Gb) for the run, lane, or library. Represents the volume of sequence data generated and is used to assess whether sufficient data was produced to meet coverage requirements for the assay type. Sourced from Illumina BaseSpace or bcl2fastq.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; ISO 15189; CLIA

description: Number of flow cell lanes configured in this sample sheet. Determines how samples are distributed across lanes for multiplexing. Varies by flow cell type (e.g., 2 lanes for SP, 4 lanes for S2/S4 on NovaSeq 6000).
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Lane Configuration Guide; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: The name and version of the library preparation kit used to prepare samples for sequencing (e.g., Illumina DNA Prep, TruSeq DNA PCR-Free, Nextera DNA Flex). Determines adapter sequences, index compatibility, and recommended run parameters.
reference: Illumina Library Prep Kit Documentation; LabVantage LIMS — Library Preparation Workflow

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Request ID
description: The work order or sequencing request identifier from LabVantage LIMS that initiated this sample sheet. Provides bidirectional traceability between the sample sheet and the originating laboratory workflow request.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Sequencing Request Management; ISO 15189 Section 5.4

description: The MaskShortReads BCLConvert setting specifying the read length threshold below which reads are masked (replaced with Ns) rather than discarded. Complements minimum_trimmed_read_length to control short-read handling behavior in FASTQ output.
reference: Illumina BCLConvert User Guide — MaskShortReads Parameter

description: The MinimumTrimmedReadLength BCLConvert setting specifying the minimum acceptable read length (in base pairs) after adapter trimming. Reads shorter than this threshold are discarded. Impacts FASTQ output quality and downstream alignment performance.
reference: Illumina BCLConvert User Guide — MinimumTrimmedReadLength Parameter

description: The OverrideCycles parameter string in Illumina sample sheet v2 format (e.g., 'Y151;I8;I8;Y151') that explicitly defines the cycle allocation for each read and index segment. Overrides default cycle settings and is used by BCLConvert for precise demultiplexing configuration.
reference: Illumina BCLConvert User Guide; Illumina Sample Sheet v2 Specification

description: The project or study name associated with the samples in this sample sheet. Used for grouping FASTQ output files by project in BaseSpace and downstream bioinformatics pipelines. Maps to the 'Sample_Project' column in the sample manifest.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Project Management; LabVantage LIMS

description: Number of sequencing cycles for Read 1 (forward read). Directly determines the read length in base pairs for the primary read. Typical values range from 50 to 300 cycles depending on application and instrument platform.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet v2 Specification — [Reads] Section; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Number of sequencing cycles for Read 2 (reverse read) in paired-end runs. Null for single-end runs. Must match Read 1 cycles for symmetric paired-end configurations or may differ for asymmetric designs.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet v2 Specification — [Reads] Section; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Specifies whether the sequencing run is configured as single-end (one read direction) or paired-end (forward and reverse reads). Paired-end reads improve alignment accuracy and are standard for WGS and WES applications.
reference: Illumina Sequencing Technology Guide; NGS Best Practices

description: Free-text description field from the [Header] section of the Illumina sample sheet. May include run purpose, special instructions, or notes relevant to the sequencing configuration. Supports operational context for lab staff.

Total number of samples listed in the sample manifest section of this sample sheet. Represents the multiplexing depth for the run. Used for capacity planning, lane loading calculations, and demultiplexing validation.
reference: Illumina Multiplexing Guidelines; LabVantage LIMS — Sample Tracking

description: Version of the Illumina sample sheet format used (v1 or v2). v2 supports multi-section configuration including [Header], [Reads], [BCLConvert_Settings], and [BCLConvert_Data] sections. Determines downstream demultiplexing compatibility and parser behavior.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet v2 Specification; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Human-readable name or title assigned to the sample sheet file, typically following the Illumina naming convention (e.g., SampleSheet_RunID_YYYYMMDD). Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this document. Aligns with Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub sample sheet naming standards.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Sample Sheet Specification

description: The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the sample sheet was submitted to the instrument or uploaded to Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub; GxP Audit Trail Requirements; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this run sample sheet record. Supports change tracking and GxP audit trail requirements for controlled documents.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GxP Electronic Records; ISO 13485 Section 4.2

description: Regulatory intended use classification for the samples in this run. RUO (Research Use Only) indicates non-clinical research; IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) indicates regulated diagnostic use; LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) indicates a lab-specific validated test; GMP indicates manufacturing-grade production run.
reference: FDA IVD Regulations 21 CFR Part 809; IVDR EU 2017/746; CLIA 42 CFR Part 493; ISO 13485

description: Free-text summary of validation errors or warnings identified during automated sample sheet validation (e.g., index collision detected, invalid cycle count, missing required fields). Populated when validation_status is 'failed_validation'. Supports rapid troubleshooting by lab operators.
reference: Illumina BCLConvert Validation Rules; LabVantage LIMS QC Workflow; GxP Non-Conformance Handling

description: Current lifecycle and validation state of the sample sheet. 'draft' indicates in-preparation; 'pending_validation' awaiting automated or manual QC check; 'validated' passed all checks and approved for run execution; 'failed_validation' contains errors blocking run start; 'superseded' replaced by a corrected version; 'cancelled' withdrawn before use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_validation|validated|failed_validation|superseded|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: GxP Document Control; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault Quality Management

description: Document version number of this sample sheet (e.g., v1.0, v1.1, v2.0). Incremented upon each revision or correction. Supports GxP document version control and supersession tracking. The combination of sample_sheet_name and version_number uniquely identifies a document revision.
reference: GxP Document Control; ISO 13485 Section 4.2; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl Document Control

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) run record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for the sequencing_run entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this entity represents a discrete NGS sequencing execution event with a full operational lifecycle.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Object Model

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Sequencing runs performed as service for customer accounts in contract research/service lab operations. Essential for billing, SLA tracking, regulatory compliance, and customer-specific data segregati

Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Sequencing runs performed under CLIA/CAP-accredited workflows must be traceable to the customer's active accreditation record for regulatory audit, inspection readiness, and clinical reporting complia

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: IVD/clinical sequencing runs must be traceable to the regulatory approval (e.g., 510(k), CE-IVD) authorizing their use. GxP-regulated labs require this for audit trails and compliance reporting. 'regu

Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Sequencing runs executed as assay development validation runs must be traceable to the assay_development record. Assay development teams track run performance against target coverage_depth and technol

Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: CLIA labs must document which assay version governed each sequencing run. The assay_version defines minimum_coverage_depth, minimum_phred_score, and sequencing_platform acceptance criteria applied to

Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Reagent kit batch traceability for sequencing runs is required for supplier recall management and lot-level QC investigations. sequencing_run has reagent_kit_lot_id (reagent domain) but no FK to the p

Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Systematic sequencing run failures (repeated QC failures, instrument issues, process deviations) trigger CAPAs. Labs must link runs to CAPAs for effectiveness checks, trend analysis, and regulatory co

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Technical contact for run-specific communications: QC notifications, result delivery, troubleshooting. Critical for customer-facing service operations and regulatory traceability of data delivery.

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Sequencing runs frequently trigger quality deviations (failed QC, instrument errors, contamination events). GxP labs must link runs to deviations for investigation, root cause analysis, and regulatory

Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Sequencing runs generate data for specific experiments. Tracking this link enables experiment-level data aggregation, cost allocation to R&D experiments, and traceability from raw sequencing output to

Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: A sequencing run executes on a specific customer-installed instrument. This link is essential for instrument utilization reporting, warranty/service tracking, and SLA compliance — a genomics ops team

Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: Multi-site genomics labs require run-to-site traceability for proficiency testing, regulatory submissions (CLIA/CAP site-specific reporting), and inter-site QC comparisons. A genomics operations exper

Foreign key linking to product.license_entitlement. Business justification: Each sequencing run consumes software license entitlements (BaseSpace compute, DRAGEN analysis, data_processing_limit_gb). License compliance reporting and usage-based billing require runs to referenc

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Direct traceability from sequencing run to manufactured reagent batch enables rapid quality investigations. When Q30 scores drop or error rates spike, QC teams must immediately identify affected manuf

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Vertically-integrated genomics companies manufacture custom reagent kits in-house. Sequencing runs must trace to manufacturing work orders for regulatory compliance (21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485), qualit

Foreign key linking to instrument.model. Business justification: Sequencing runs are executed on a specific instrument model determining chemistry, read length capability, flow cell compatibility, and regulatory classification. GxP audit trails, throughput reportin

Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: Each sequencing run is physically performed in a CLIA-certified performing laboratory. CAP/CLIA compliance requires every run to be traceable to the performing lab for inspection, proficiency testing,
Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., NovaSeq, NextSeq, HiSeq) on which this run was executed. Used to trace run performance back to a specific physical instrument asset.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Instrument Registry; ISO 13485 §7.6 Control of Monitoring and Measuring Equipment

description: Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Sequencing runs consume reagent kits purchased via PO. Link enables cost accounting per run, supplier performance tracking, and GMP traceability required for regulated assays. Critical for COGS calcul

Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Reagent kit compatibility validation and recall management require linking each sequencing run to the reagent.catalog entry to access storage requirements, GMP compliance status, and shelf-life specs.

business_glossary_term: Reagent Kit Catalog Item Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Sequencing runs consume specific reagent kit catalog items. Essential for cost-per-run analysis, inventory management, regulatory lot traceability, and procurement planning. Labs must track which comm

Reference to the reagent kit lot (LOT number) consumed during this sequencing run. Enables traceability from run output back to reagent manufacturing lot for quality investigations and COA verification.
reference: ISO 13485 §7.5.3; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; SAP S/4HANA MM — Batch Management

description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Inventory depletion and COGS reporting require the specific SKU (pack size, regional config) consumed per run, not just the catalog item. Procurement reorder triggers and run-level cost accounting in

Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: IVD vs RUO classification of a sequencing run determines GxP requirements, data handling, and regulatory reporting obligations. The plain column regulatory_use_classification is denormalized from prod

Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: A sequencing run executed as a service must reference the sales order that commissioned it. This is fundamental traceability in genomics service labs — linking lab execution to commercial order for bi

Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_protocol. Business justification: Sequencing runs must follow approved, validated sequencing protocols that define run configuration, QC thresholds, and regulatory compliance requirements. sequencing_run currently has run_mode, run_ty

Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Runs performed under specific service agreements defining SLA terms, turnaround time, pricing tier, regulatory classification. Essential for contract compliance verification and billing.

Foreign key linking to product.service_offering. Business justification: Sequencing-as-a-service runs must reference the service offering for billing, SLA compliance, and deliverables tracking. Contract research organizations and genomics service labs link each run to the

Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Regulatory submissions (FDA, CLIA) and reproducibility require tracing which basecalling/RTA software version executed each run. GxP-regulated sequencing environments mandate software version traceabi

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Run QC pass/fail decisions are made against product specifications (min Q30, min yield, coverage depth thresholds). In CLIA/CAP-regulated labs, the specification version the run was validated against

Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Runs executed to fulfill customer-specified technical requirements (coverage depth, read length, platform). Links run parameters to customer engagement specifications for QC validation and acceptance

Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: A sequencing run is executed to fulfill a specific clinical test catalog entry. CLIA labs must document which test (CPT code, TAT requirement, assay type) each run was performed for, enabling billing,

Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Sequencing runs are used in validation studies (assay validation, instrument qualification, method verification, software validation). Labs must link validation runs to study records for regulatory su

Actual number of samples successfully demultiplexed and yielding usable FASTQ data from this run. May differ from planned_sample_count due to index mismatches, low-quality libraries, or run failures. Key metric for run efficiency and yield reporting.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Demultiplexing Report; CLIA §493.1291

description: The unique run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for this sequencing run. Used to link the internal enterprise record to the BaseSpace cloud platform for FASTQ retrieval, run monitoring, and bioinformatics pipeline initiation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run API Object; Illumina Run Monitoring Documentation

description: The density of sequencing clusters generated on the flow cell surface, measured in thousands of clusters per mm². A critical run quality parameter — too low indicates under-loading, too high causes cluster overlap and quality degradation. Reported by the instrument and BaseSpace.
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Loading Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Metrics

description: Percentage of clusters that pass the Illumina chastity filter (PF = Passing Filter), indicating the proportion of reads with sufficient signal purity for reliable base calling. A key run-level QC metric; typically expected to be >75-80% for a passing run.
reference: Illumina Quality Metrics Documentation; CAP NGS Checklist NGS.40100; ISO 20166

description: Date and time when this sequencing run record was first created in the enterprise data system (LIMS or BaseSpace ingestion). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Distinct from run_start_timestamp which reflects the physical run start. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records; ISO 27001 Audit Logging; SOX Section 404

description: Data retention classification for this sequencing run record and its associated FASTQ/BAM/VCF outputs, determining the minimum retention period per applicable regulations. 'standard' = 2 years (RUO); 'extended' = 10+ years (clinical/GxP); 'permanent' = indefinite (regulatory submission data); 'restricted_deletion' = deletion requires regulatory approval.
reference: HIPAA §164.530(j) Record Retention; CLIA §493.1105; FDA 21 CFR §211.180; GDPR Article 5(1)(e); NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy

description: Current status of the FASTQ demultiplexing process for this run, which separates pooled (multiplexed) sample reads by their index barcodes into individual sample FASTQ files. Tracks the post-run data processing step that precedes bioinformatics pipeline execution.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — DRAGEN Demultiplexing; Illumina bcl2fastq Documentation

description: Date and time when the sequencing run completed (final cycle finished or run terminated). Used with run_start_timestamp to calculate run duration and TAT. Null if run is still in progress or queued. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run End Time; LabVantage LIMS

description: The base-level sequencing error rate for this run expressed as a percentage, calculated using PhiX control spike-in alignment. Lower values indicate higher sequencing accuracy. Used for run QC acceptance/rejection decisions and instrument performance trending.
reference: Illumina PhiX Control Library; Illumina BaseSpace Run Metrics; CAP NGS Checklist NGS.40200

description: The storage path or URI (e.g., S3 bucket path, BaseSpace project URL, or HPC filesystem path) where the FASTQ output files for this run are stored. Confidential as it reveals internal data infrastructure topology. Used by bioinformatics pipelines to locate raw sequencing data.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; HIPAA §164.312 Technical Safeguards

description: The type/format of flow cell used in this run (e.g., SP, S1, S2, S4 for NovaSeq; P1, P2, P3 for NovaSeq X; nano, micro for MiSeq). Determines lane count, cluster capacity, and maximum output yield. Critical for run planning and cost accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SP|S1|S2|S4|P1|P2|P3|nano|micro|high|mid — promote to reference product]
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Specifications; SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (Consumables)

description: The instrument-generated run folder name following Illumina naming convention (e.g., '240115_A00001_0001_AHXXXXXX'). Contains date, instrument serial, run number, and flow cell ID. Used for data traceability between instrument output and enterprise data systems.
reference: Illumina Instrument Run Folder Naming Convention; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub

description: Number of base pairs sequenced for the index (barcode) reads used in multiplexing/demultiplexing. Typically 6, 8, or 10 bp per index. Determines the number of samples that can be multiplexed on a single flow cell and the accuracy of demultiplexing.
reference: Illumina Multiplexing Guidelines; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Parameters

description: Indicates whether this run was configured as paired-end (True) or single-read (False) sequencing. Paired-end sequencing generates reads from both ends of a DNA fragment, improving alignment accuracy and variant detection. Critical parameter for bioinformatics pipeline configuration.
reference: Illumina Paired-End Sequencing Technology; NGS Library Preparation Best Practices

description: Number of lanes on the flow cell used in this sequencing run (e.g., 1 for MiSeq, 2 for NextSeq, 4 for NovaSeq SP/S1/S2, 8 for NovaSeq S4). Used for per-lane QC analysis, sample distribution planning, and run capacity calculations.
reference: Illumina Flow Cell Architecture Documentation; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Lane Metrics

description: Name or catalog identifier of the library preparation kit used to prepare samples for this sequencing run (e.g., 'Illumina DNA Prep', 'TruSeq Stranded mRNA', 'Nextera DNA Flex'). Affects sequencing performance, adapter trimming parameters, and bioinformatics pipeline configuration.
reference: Illumina Library Preparation Kit Catalog; LabVantage LIMS — Protocol Management; ISO 13485 §7.5

description: Free-text field for operator or QC reviewer notes about this sequencing run, including observations about run performance, deviations from SOP, troubleshooting actions taken, or contextual information relevant to data interpretation. Supports GxP deviation documentation.
reference: GxP SOP Documentation Requirements; CLIA §493.1291; ISO 15189 §5.8

description: Total sequencing data output generated by this run measured in gigabases (Gb). Represents the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT for this non-monetary TRANSACTION_HEADER. Used for run performance benchmarking, storage capacity planning, and comparison against instrument specifications.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Metrics; Illumina Instrument Specification Sheets

description: Percentage of PhiX bacteriophage control library spiked into the sequencing run for real-time quality monitoring and error rate calibration. Typically 1-5% for standard runs. Used to calculate error_rate_pct and validate instrument performance.
reference: Illumina PhiX Control v3 Library Documentation; CAP NGS Checklist

description: Number of samples planned to be multiplexed and sequenced in this run as defined in the sample sheet at run setup. Used for capacity planning, reagent consumption estimation, and comparison against actual demultiplexed sample count.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet Specification; LabVantage LIMS — Run Planning

description: Percentage of bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. The primary industry-standard run quality metric. Typically required to be >75-80% for a run to pass QC. Directly impacts variant calling accuracy and clinical reporting confidence.
reference: Phred Quality Score Standard (Ewing & Green 1998); CAP NGS Checklist; CLIA §493.1253; Illumina BaseSpace Run Metrics

description: Date and time when the QC review of this sequencing run was completed and the run_qc_status was formally assigned. Part of the GxP electronic audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The overall QC pass/fail determination for this sequencing run based on evaluation of run metrics (Q30%, cluster density, error rate, yield). 'pass' = all metrics within specification; 'fail' = one or more critical metrics out of specification; 'pending_review' = awaiting manual QC review; 'conditional_pass' = passed with noted deviations. Determines whether downstream FASTQ data is released for analysis.
reference: CAP NGS Checklist; CLIA §493.1253; ISO 15189 §5.6; GxP QC Release Procedures

description: The number of base pairs sequenced per read in this run (e.g., 75, 100, 150, 250 bp). A key sequencing parameter that determines data quality, coverage, and application suitability. Configured at run setup and recorded for QC and reproducibility.
reference: Illumina Sequencing by Synthesis Technology; NGS Best Practices; ISO 20166 Molecular In Vitro Diagnostic Examinations

description: The NGS application type or sequencing strategy for this run. Determines the library preparation approach, bioinformatics pipeline, and downstream analysis. WGS = Whole Genome Sequencing; WES = Whole Exome Sequencing; targeted = targeted panel sequencing; amplicon = amplicon-based sequencing; RNA-seq = transcriptome sequencing; metagenomics = environmental/microbial sequencing.
reference: NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy; Illumina Application Notes; IVDR 2017/746 Annex I

description: Human-readable name assigned to the sequencing run, typically following a lab naming convention (e.g., 'RUN-2024-001-NovaSeq'). Used for identification in LIMS, BaseSpace, and operational dashboards. Maps to the Run Name field in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Name Field; LabVantage LIMS

description: Externally-known business identifier for the sequencing run, assigned by the LIMS or laboratory operations system. Used for cross-system referencing, customer reporting, and regulatory traceability. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.
reference: LabVantage LIMS — Run Number; CLIA 42 CFR §493.1291; ISO 15189 §5.8

description: Current lifecycle state of the sequencing run. Drives operational workflows, downstream pipeline triggering, and SLA monitoring. 'queued' = scheduled but not started; 'in_progress' = actively sequencing; 'completed' = run finished successfully; 'failed' = run terminated with errors; 'aborted' = manually stopped; 'paused' = temporarily halted. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Status Enumeration; LabVantage LIMS Workflow States

description: Classification of the run by its intended use context. 'clinical' = IVD/LDT clinical diagnostic run subject to CLIA/CAP; 'research' = RUO research run; 'validation' = assay validation run; 'QC' = quality control run; 'development' = R&D development run. Determines applicable regulatory framework and data handling requirements.
reference: CLIA 42 CFR §493; CAP Accreditation Standards; FDA IVD Regulations 21 CFR Part 820; IVDR 2017/746

description: Version identifier of the Illumina sample sheet file used to configure this sequencing run, specifying sample IDs, index sequences, and demultiplexing parameters. Used for run reproducibility, troubleshooting, and audit trail. Maps to Illumina SampleSheet.csv versioning.
reference: Illumina Sample Sheet v2 Specification; Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Configuration

description: Date and time when the sequencing run was initiated on the instrument (first cycle commenced). Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculation, SLA monitoring, and run scheduling. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Start Time; LabVantage LIMS; CLIA §493.1291

business_glossary_term: Target Coverage Depth (Mean Depth of Coverage)
description: The intended mean sequencing coverage depth (number of times each base is sequenced) specified at run design time (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for WES, 500x for targeted panels). Used to validate whether the run met its coverage objectives and for clinical reporting thresholds.
reference: ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines; CAP NGS Checklist; CLIA §493.1253

description: Total number of sequencing chemistry cycles executed in this run. For paired-end runs, this includes both read 1, index reads, and read 2 cycles (e.g., 2×150 bp = 300 cycles + index cycles). Used for run cost accounting, reagent consumption tracking, and run configuration validation.
reference: Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub — Run Parameters; Illumina Reagent Kit Specifications

description: Date and time when this sequencing run record was last modified in the enterprise data system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED timestamp for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GxP 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 27001; SOX Section 404

description: Unique identifier for the production or procurement batch. Primary key for the batch master record.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: GMP batch release requires verification that batch specifications conform to approved regulatory dossier. Critical for QC disposition decisions, certificate of analysis generation, and ensuring batche

Foreign key linking to regulatory.device_identifier. Business justification: FDA UDI rule and EU MDR require lot/batch-level UDI-PI traceability linking each batch to its UDI device identifier. material has device_identifier_id for product-level UDI, but batch-level UDI tracea
Reference to the material master record for which this batch was produced or procured. Links to the material product in the supply domain.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where the batch was produced. Links to the facility master in the supply or manufacturing domain.
Reference to the purchase order under which this batch was procured. Null for internally manufactured batches.
Reference to the supplier master record if the batch was externally procured. Null for internally manufactured batches.
Reference to the primary warehouse location where the batch is stored. Links to warehouse master in the supply domain.
Current unrestricted quantity available for use or shipment from this batch. Updated in real-time as inventory is consumed or reserved.
Business-facing batch or lot number assigned to the production or procurement batch. Used for traceability and quality control. Also known as lot number.
Overall lifecycle status of the batch indicating its current state in inventory and usability.
reference: SAP MM Custom Field; GMP Guidelines

description: Quantity from this batch that is blocked for quality or compliance reasons and cannot be used or shipped until released.
Unique identifier for the Certificate of Analysis document that certifies the batch meets quality specifications. Required for GMP compliance and customer shipments.
reference: GMP Guidelines; ISO 13485 Section 7.5.3; SAP QM Custom Field

description: Indicates whether the batch has been maintained within required cold-chain temperature ranges throughout its lifecycle. False indicates a temperature excursion event occurred.
reference: GMP Guidelines; ISO 13485 Section 7.5.5; Custom Field

description: Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the batch was manufactured or procured. Required for customs, trade compliance, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP MM Custom Field

description: Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SAP MM MCH1-ERSDA; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Date after which the batch should not be used due to potential degradation or loss of efficacy. Mandatory for reagents, consumables, and IVD products.
reference: SAP MM MCH1-VFDAT; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; ISO 13485 Section 7.5.3

description: Indicates whether the batch was manufactured under GMP, non-GMP, or GMP-equivalent conditions. Critical for regulatory compliance and product classification.
reference: GMP Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; EMA GMP Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the batch contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and shipping procedures.
reference: OSHA HCS 29 CFR 1910.1200; DOT 49 CFR; Custom Field

description: User ID or name of the person who last modified the batch record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
reference: SAP MM Custom Field; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Timestamp when the batch record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
reference: SAP MM MCH1-LAEDA; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which the batch was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf-life calculation and expiry determination.
reference: SAP MM MCH1-HSDAT; ISO 13485 Section 7.5.3; GMP Guidelines

description: Source of the batch indicating whether it was produced internally, procured from an external supplier, manufactured by a contract manufacturer, reworked from a previous batch, or is a sample batch.
reference: GMP Guidelines; SAP MM Custom Field

description: Date on which the batch was officially released by Quality Assurance for use or distribution. Null if batch is not yet released.
reference: SAP QM Custom Field; ISO 13485 Section 8.2.4

description: Current quality control release status of the batch. Only released batches are available for use or shipment. Managed by Quality Management module.
reference: SAP QM QALS-PASTRTERM; ISO 13485 Section 8.2.4; GMP Guidelines

description: Quantity from this batch that is in quality inspection and not yet released for use. Managed by Quality Management.
Date on which the recall was initiated for this batch. Null if no recall has been issued.
Indicates whether this batch is subject to a product recall. True triggers immediate inventory blocking and customer notification workflows.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; ISO 13485 Section 8.5.1; Custom Field

description: Detailed explanation of why the batch was recalled. Includes quality defect description, safety concern, or regulatory non-compliance issue.

Regulatory category of the batch indicating its intended use and compliance requirements. IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), RUO (Research Use Only), GMP API (Good Manufacturing Practice Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient).
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; IVDR 2017/746; Custom Field

description: Date by which the batch must be retested to confirm continued quality and stability. Applicable to raw materials and intermediates.
reference: SAP MM MCH1-VFDAT; GMP Guidelines; ICH Q7

description: Unique identifier for the Safety Data Sheet associated with this batch. Required for hazardous materials and chemicals.
reference: OSHA HCS 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS; Custom Field

description: Total shelf life of the batch in days from manufacturing date to expiry date. Used for inventory planning and FEFO (First Expired First Out) logic.
reference: SAP MM Custom Field; GMP Guidelines

description: Total quantity produced or procured in this batch. Expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.
Unit of measure for the batch size quantity. Examples: EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), ML (milliliter).
Date on which the batch was sterilized. Null if sterilization is not applicable.
Method used to sterilize the batch if applicable. Critical for consumables and reagents used in clinical or diagnostic applications.
Required storage condition for the batch to maintain quality and stability. Defines temperature and environmental requirements.
reference: SAP MM Custom Field; GMP Guidelines; ISO 13485 Section 7.5.5

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the batch. Exceeding this threshold may compromise batch quality.
Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the batch. Used for cold-chain monitoring and compliance.
Original batch or lot number assigned by the supplier for externally procured materials. Used for supplier traceability and quality investigations.
reference: SAP MM Custom Field; GMP Guidelines

description: Primary key for goods_receipt

description: Date when the genomic result was officially reported to the ordering physician or patient. Key milestone for turnaround time (TAT) tracking.

The primary genomic finding value. Format varies by result type: genotype call (e.g., A/G), copy number (e.g., 3), variant notation (e.g., c.1234G>A). Protected Health Information (PHI).

Fraction of sequencing reads supporting the alternate allele at this position. Used to assess variant heterogeneity and quality.

Version identifier of the variant annotation databases (ClinVar, gnomAD, dbSNP) used for interpretation. Critical for result reproducibility.
reference: ISO 15189 - Medical Laboratories Quality and Competence

description: Zygosity status of the detected variant. Indicates whether the variant is present on one or both chromosomes.

Format in which the clinical report is delivered (e.g., PDF, HL7 FHIR, HL7 v2).

description: Date the specimen was collected from the patient.
Type of biological specimen analyzed (e.g., whole blood, saliva, tissue biopsy).
Actual turnaround time from specimen receipt to report finalization, measured in hours.
Textual summary of key variant findings included in the report, listing genes and variant classifications.
reference: ACMG Standards and Guidelines; CAP Checklist MOL.30850

description: Version number of this report, incremented with each amendment or correction.
Unique identifier for the clinical variant interpretation record. Primary key.
Reference to the clinical assay under which this variant was interpreted.

business_glossary_term: ACMG (American College of Medical Genetics and Genomics) Classification
description: Clinical significance classification per ACMG/AMP guidelines: pathogenic, likely pathogenic, VUS (Variant of Uncertain Significance), likely benign, or benign.

description: Comma-separated list of ACMG/AMP evidence codes applied to support the classification (e.g., PVS1, PS1, PM2, PP3, BA1, BS1, BP4).
Population allele frequency of this variant from reference databases (e.g., gnomAD).
The observed variant nucleotide sequence that differs from the reference.
Chromosome on which the variant is located (1-22, X, Y, M/MT).
Version number of the classification, incremented when the interpretation is updated or reclassified.
Narrative description of the clinical significance of the variant, including disease association and expected phenotype.
ClinVar accession number for this variant if previously submitted.
Clinical significance classification from ClinVar database.
Timestamp when this variant interpretation record was first created in the system.
Name of the disease or condition associated with this variant (e.g., Cystic Fibrosis, Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome).
reference: OMIM (Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man) / MONDO Disease Ontology

description: Genomic coordinate of the variant on the reference genome.
Version of the ACMG/AMP or other clinical guidelines used for this interpretation (e.g., ACMG 2015, ClinGen 2018).
Mode of inheritance for the disease associated with this variant: autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, X-linked dominant, X-linked recessive, mitochondrial, or multifactorial.
Date when the variant interpretation was completed.
Detailed rationale and evidence summary supporting the variant classification, including literature references and functional studies.
Current lifecycle status of the interpretation: draft, final, amended, reviewed, or archived.

Timestamp when this variant interpretation record was last modified.
MONDO ontology identifier for the associated disease.

The nucleotide sequence in the reference genome at this position.
Indicates whether this variant interpretation should be included in the clinical report delivered to the ordering physician.
Date when the interpretation was last reviewed or updated.
Predicted functional consequence of the variant on the protein (e.g., missense, nonsense, frameshift, splice site, synonymous).
Human-readable identifier for the genomic variant, typically in HGVS notation (e.g., NM_000059.3:c.1521_1523delCTT).

Zygosity status of the variant in the patient: heterozygous, homozygous, hemizygous, or compound heterozygous.
